title: Louvre Museum
text: We weren't able to go inside, but judging from only the sizes of the building, one day is not enough. We only took a little peak of the statue park, close to the Pyramid. But the building itself is also truly amazing! A masterpiece of architecture if you ask me. If you don't go inside at least a walk around the museum is a must if you are in Paris, we did that!

title: Louvre Museum
text: What a magical place! Housed in 3 wings over several floors, the collection is vast and varied. As I was only in Paris for 1 day, I invested in a guided tour, which was worth every penny. The Louvre is simply wonderful.

title: Louvre Museum
text: What an amazing museum filled with incredible art and rich history. This place is a a must see for anyone visiting. With such an intensive collection, it would take over 3 months to truly see every single piece. I highly recommend going with a tour guide to help navigate and make most of your visit through this magnificent museum.

title: Louvre Museum
text:
What can you say…..biggest museum in the world. We used  guide that helped with quicker access and insightful commentary for a 2 hour tour that helps you feel like you really experienced the important things to see.

I’d recommend not trying to see it all….take your time and enjoy what you do see. Break for lunch or a drink. Enjoy more. And then go look for a French meal. Trying to see it all just creates a blur of everything.

Oh. And the Mona Lisa is there as well. No need to wait in the huge group. To save a half hour just walk past the group to the next room and see it very close but just off to the side a little.

Bon chance!

title: Louvre Museum
text: Words can’t describe the experience in the most visited  museum in the world.  Went with a guide so entering was a breeze.  Saw only a fraction of things but left extremity happy.

title: Louvre Museum
text:
World class museum; staff can be difficult if you don’t speak French, very unfortunate.

If you don’t buy tickets in advance, or they’re sold out online, there is still an opportunity to buy and enter through a separate cue!

title: Louvre Museum
text: You have to visit at least once in your life. We came just 3-4 hours before closing time thinking that would be sufficient but this museum is huge and there's so much to see and learn about. To say their collection is vast is truly an understatement. Seeing the artworks they had in real life was breathtaking. If you're someone who appreciates art and history, I recommend spending an entire day here. They also have a great gift shop, there's a souvenir for everyone. Will definitely visit again.

title: Louvre Museum
text: You must book several weeks in advance, otherwise you will pay to enter through an agency who have blocked tickets. Too many visitors in the summer! seeing the Mona Lisa is like a rugby scrum. Museum is accessible but awkward. For visitors with any mobility issues, there are elevators but their location is hard to find.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
The museum is not designed to handle the volume of visitors it accommodates: waiting and circulation areas, restrooms, and dining spaces are simply insufficient. I also question whether they truly know how to manage an evacuation when internal passageways become as crowded as Tokyo’s Shibuya. It is insupportable that it seems a majority of people are almost forced to take a guided tour. There are so many large groups at the same time that sometimes it is just impossible to walk if you go by yourself !

A shame for such an exceptional collection—one of the worst museum experiences because there is no crowd regulation, and the museum is not adapted for modern times.

title: Vatican Museums
text: The paintings in the Vatican Museums are far inferior to those in the Uffizi Gallery, and the sculptures are not as impressive as those in Piazza della Signoria of Florence. the museum was incredibly crowded by huge of people far beyond its capacity. The enclosed passageways were filled with a mix of perfumes and body odor; I almost threw up afterward.

title: Vatican Museums
text: If you love museums that praise whiteness, you'll love this place. I am glad I went, but its a one time thing for me. The guide was fabulous. Be prepared for A LOT of walking — at a comfortable pace for an adult, tough for an elder. Very few opportunities to sit throughout the space, even at the on-site cafe. Many stairs and very little accessibility.

title: Vatican Museums
text: No way to buy ticket there, you'll wait for ever, even if you have a ticket you'll wait for long time due to longest queue, super crowded and huge, experience was good but the Vatican was better

title: Vatican Museums
text:
We bought tickets in advance but still had to wait outside in the heat for about 30-45 min. There was no organization of the lines, so it was very confusing which line we should use.

The museum itself is massive, but did not have a accurate and readable map so it was difficult to decide where to go, and how long it would take to get there.

Due to the heat and the crowds, I found the museum to be very uncomfortable and stressful. The tickets cost 25 euro pp, but it's hard to get your money's worth because you're just pushed forward by the crowds. There is no time to appreciate the art.

It takes at least 1 hour of walking through the halls of the museum to get to the Sistine chapel. There is no short cut. There are little to no places to exit the building and get fresh air.

There are very few accessibility features. Considering the amount of elderly persons visiting this location, the accessibility features should be drastically improved.

The Gardens are beautiful and peaceful. They offer a much needed refuge from the chaos that is inside the walls of the museum.

Finally, I find it quite problematic that the people who guard and service the museum do not speak English. I understand that we are in  Italy (well Vatican City, in this case) and I do not generally expect people to speak English. However, at one of the busiest tourist locations on earth, I would expect a worker to be able to tell me how I can reach the exit.

title: Vatican Museums
text: As a person that isn’t really interested in history much or museums, i didn’t really like this as it’s too much of repetitive stuff and long walks for no reason. Too many steps aswell, lifts would be useful. the chapel wasn’t too bad but photography isn’t allowed.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Be ready for the discombobulated unorganization inside and outside of the Vatican. If you are claustrophobic and have issues with big crowds...this might be rough for you.

They need to take some of the proceeds from the money it takes in daily and buy metal crowd control fences to better organize the mass people waiting outside to go in.

Minimally, the waiting qué staff need signs with times on long sticks to hold up in the air to let the crowds of people know where their time slots are. Even though you would ask a staff member and they would tell you, tons of more people would come in by the heards...including mass guided tours of people..butting in and no one once again would not know where to go or where to be.. over and over. All the time slots were blending. It was a complete and absolute mess. It took us an hour beyond our time slot to get in.

Also it's not ideal having the exit in the same exact spot as the entrance...people coming from yet another direction, splitting the lines and pushing amongst the mass confusion.

It's BEYOND packed inside the buildings and felt like we were being pushed (literally) and rushed through because of the amount of people. You don't really get to stop and look at things in many of the rooms because of this and if you try, you get cut off by a tour of 20 people.

Other than that ..the art and grand scale beauty was absolutely amazing and certainly worth the visit.

(Added some photos for reference of the crowds...but these don't do it justice.  It was packed.)

title: Vatican Museums
text: Booked with a tour guide, who did their best, but the visit of King Charles meant that sections were closed, and that concentrated the large number of visitors. If most of the art hadn't been on the ceiling, we'd never have seen anything. A great place to visit, if you're royalty, and can do so without anyone else present.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Don’t get me wrong, the museum is a 10/10.
Considering this year’s jubilee, thou, there is a pre-established routing in place; which is great for tourists with little time, but makes it difficult to “savor” most spaces and art pieces before the Sistine Chapel (which is almost at the end of the path). The waiting time, with reservation was still considerable, so I recommend arriving a little earlier.

title: Vatican Museums
text: i advise to book the ticket through this app Tiqets in advance , This app  is the cheapest I found and very friendly , avoid the crowd and chose the time early in the morning plus is you choose the time then you should be there before at least 10 minutes morover try to avoid the weekend. If you are interested in art and paint and history the this is your place, it is so huge.

title: Vatican Museums
text: I had a disappointing experience due to a 3-hour wait in the queue, largely because many tickets were bought online by scalpers. If you're planning to visit with family, I strongly advise purchasing tickets in advance to avoid frustration. The Sistine Chapel was the highlight of the visit for me, but overall, I've been to better places. One drawback was that the tour was only in Italian, which limited my understanding. Honestly, I think the place is overrated. If you're looking for a similar experience, I'd recommend visiting the Basilica instead – it's free to enter, and the statues there are extremely impressive. Just remember to get tickets for the Basilica too, if needed.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
So, here’s my experience at the Louvre. I was aware of the crowds beforehand, but it still felt overwhelming.

This was my first time feeling claustrophobic in a room full of people, and it was simply not worth it.

The tour guide was entertaining, but her jokes about her ex-boyfriend were a bit too much. She was trying too hard to be funny, and it just didn’t work.

Walking through the museum felt like walking through a herd of cows in a slaughterhouse. Everyone was moving in the same direction, and there were people everywhere, in front of you, behind you, and to the sides. It was like being in a sardine can.

The museum has a vast collection of art, but you’ll only see a small fraction of it while being bombarded by the smell of all the people around you. It was a terrible experience.

The tour lasted about two hours, but it felt much longer.

In conclusion, if you’re planning to visit the Louvre, be prepared to spend a lot of time in lines and smell everyone around you. It’s not a great experience, but if it’s your dream to see it, go for it.

P.S. Don’t forget to bring a lot of water.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
The place was full of wonderful treasures. Definitely worth a visit. Our guide was a super nice lady who is clearly passionate about her job.

My complaints were that 1. You’d have to purchase the ticket ahead of time online. 2. The staff in the Sistine Chapel herded us as if we were animals and were extremely rude. 3. I wish that there were more explainations for the art that we saw.

title: Vatican Museums
text: This was my third time here in 20 years, and the most disappointing one. The number of people that's allowed in is stupefying. Every corner of the museum is always crowded. How can one enjoy and contemplate the beauty and the history if there are literally hundreds of people around? Talking, recording videos, and not paying attention to others.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
We visited the Vatican Museums and the Sistine Chapel as part of a guided tour. The artwork and history are, of course, absolutely incredible and something everyone should experience at least once.
However, we found the tour itself far too rushed. There is so much to see and take in, but the pace didn’t really allow time to properly appreciate the surroundings or fully absorb the information being shared.
It was also extremely crowded — at times it felt like we were packed in like sardines, which made it difficult to see some of the artwork clearly. Unfortunately, inside the Sistine Chapel there were quite a few tourists being disrespectful, taking unpermitted photos and being very noisy despite the clear rules. This detracted from what should have been a quiet and reflective experience.
While the site itself is breathtaking, the overcrowding and rushed nature of the tour did take away from the overall enjoyment.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
When I say THOUSANDS of people, it's not an exaggeration! There's way too many people going in, do they even have a limi? I don't think so, it's the Catholic Church so the more money the better...
The museum itself is gorgeous, many amazing pieces, the Sistine Chapel (which you can't even try to take a picture or you'll be humiliated like Jesus was). All of this spoiled by the number of visitors. And it doesn't matter if you have an online ticket. This means nothing. You'll wait in a huge line outside. So arrive 2 hours before your entry time. Of all the places I visited in Rome, the Vatican Museums and the St. Peter Basilica were the ones that disappointed me the most. Both are incredibly gorgeous, but the greed of the church leeds to a complete chaos of unorganized and stressful day. There needs to be a limit of people inside these 2 places!

title: Vatican Museums
text: A fantastic experience with thanks to our guide who brought us around. You do really need the guides to properly understand what you are looking at, plus we got access to the gardens which you don’t get without a guide! The place as expected was packed so be ready to be rushed through the museums, it’s like a constant conveyor belt of people and you don’t always get to stop because you are trying to keep up with your guide and their flag identifying themselves! However the art is gorgeous and it is a lot to take in.

title: Vatican Museums
text: An incredibly fascinating and art-rich place that is absolutely worth visiting. I had a guided tour that lasted about 2 hours – during that time we learned a lot of interesting facts and saw the main highlights. After the guided part, we were allowed to stay inside and continue exploring the museum on our own. The collections are huge and diverse, filled with masterpieces from all over the world – honestly, one day is not enough to see everything that is there. The Sistine Chapel itself is breathtaking, especially Michelangelo’s frescoes. Be prepared though – it is always very crowded inside and can get quite hot, which makes the visit a bit tiring.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Awesome ceilings and a view of the wealth the church has! Glad I did the tour. Its awesome. Avoid the shops as the same items are mostly available in the stalls around Rome for a fraction of the price. They have prosecco in the little cafe though for 5€. 😀

title: Vatican Museums
text: Beautiful undoubtedly and great service as they let us enter after we informed them of our delayed flight even tho it was very past our designated time But The actual museum being so messy makes it impossible to see everything and you don’t even know where to go to see what and the biggest problem being that to go to the chapel you had to see modern art and go through 26 rooms full of people which was so irritating.!However it was wonderful With arts that you can only see there!

title: Vatican Museums
text: Book your tickets ahead of time. When you show up for your scheduled time, there will be a very long line to get in, however it moves quickly. Once inside, it takes about 2.5 hours to walk through the museum to get to the Sistine Chapel. The art is amazing, easily some of the most beautiful and opulent museums I’ve ever seen. As always, it will be very crowded with huge tour groups blocking a lot of the art. If you are someone that loves art, consider getting an audio guide or doing a tour. If you like to look at art but don’t need to hear the stories and history behind it, walking around self-guided is more than enough. You will need to adhere to the dress code for the Sistine Chapel so be mindful of that before coming. You are not allowed to take photos or videos inside of the Sistine Chapel, nor are you allowed to talk. Sadly, as there is a large crowd gathered in the center of the chapel, a lot of tourists do not respect these rules. Again, it will take about 2.5 hours to get to the Sistine Chapel because you have to walk through the museums before you make your way there. It looked like some of the tour groups were able to take shortcuts. Overall, we spent about 3 hours here.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Definitely worth a visit! The museum is so huge that makes you feel exhausted by walking the whole building. 🤣 The architecture itself is magnificent and gorgeous, in the indoors, it makes use of a lot of ceiling to display the paintings and artwork. The awe toward the artworks just hits you like the sunset in the picture…

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Firstly, get your tickets in advance, as its just not worth to wait for hours in the line. Some say you can show up very early, but that's still a gamble. With advance I mean buy them 1 month ealier otherwise the ticket price will just increase.

Get a local guide in person, because the museum is very big and there are too many artefacts, things to see, and the crowd is very big so its not easy to aproach every item and read what it is, or is it worth reading.

Is it worth visiting once in a lifetime, of course, but a second time i dont think so. For me personally, its just 2much. Too many people and most of the area is not a 2 way route but you have to walk until you leave a part of the museum, meaning you cant go back, and you can't rest, so you have to keep on walking.
I was suprised to see that most of the Greek sculptures are Roman replicas, i.e from the 3rh century, still very old but not the original ones.
The paintings are worth going to because they are world famous, the creation of Adam etc. its very interesting to see them in person, and you can stare at the celling for hours if you ask me, but because of the crowd, i just wanted to leave because we got tired of walking.

Overal worth visiting, get a ticket in advance, local audio guide in person and go early. Bring small food with you

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Get the tickets online, show up 30 minutes early, and get the happy hour pass. Totally worth it! A lot to see and includes Sistine chapel. Don't buy the audio guide people really use them. Recommend doing a tour because there's so much. Expect a highlight tour instead of a look at everything.
Make sure to spend time in room of maps!

title: Vatican Museums
text: Great guide, organized, extensive. One day is not enough to see the wealth of artifacts displayed here. Always very busy, but well kept. Interpretation signs are limited or not easily found for many of the statues. Having a guide is recommended.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Had a small wait to get inside as we arrived a little earlier than our time slot.  Once inside it was worth the wait and very interesting. Definitely worth visiting if you're in Rome .

title: Vatican Museums
text:
How to start a good day? By visiting the Vatican Museums of course!

For starters, I recommend you booking tickets online specifically the 8AM ones. I am aware it would mean getting ready at 6 to be there early 7 in the morning, but believe me it is the best thing to do.

When the gates open you are practically alone inside the museums, my family and I stayed inside the Capella Sistina ALONE for at least 10 minutes, enjoying the art, but not only.

And being able to see the sculptures up close without having to shove thousands of people away was absolutely worth the early morning.

(If you need some breakfast or coffee, there's some coffee stores already open, but be mindful that some are scams or touristy.)

I also recommend truly enjoying the Etruscan and Egyptian (+ the Mesopotamian) museums that lead to the Laocoön sculptures among others.

Also as an information, if you have reserved tickets you need to go to the front of where is written "Museu Vaticani", where gateways are separated in 3. It's quite confusing.

Thank you for reading!

(For transparency it has 4/5 because it is not really elderly, nor wheelchair accessible most of the time, a lot of stairs.)

title: Vatican Museums
text: I got lost at the beginning didn't know where to start. It's amazing and very rich but I was drained during the day because of the so much walking and waiting. Better to book in advance online and there will also be waiting a bit. You won't be allowed to enter in another time than the one mentioned in your ticket so you have to go on time. I recommend going 30 min before your schedule time.

title: Vatican Museums
text: If you visit here, be prepared for a long walk, lots of people, lots of history and lots of etruscan pots :-) The site is huge and made up of several standalone museums and outside areas with views of the dome of St Peters Basilca. The highlight of one of these culminates in visiting the Sistine Chapel(SC) Dont make the mistake of not visiting the others areas first. Perhaps not as inspiring as what you will see at  the last, but nevertheless still full of Italian history.  Once you start the trip( and it is a long trip) to where everyone wants to go, you are swept along with them. So beware, you might find it difficult to get back to see anything you might have missed. There are countless rooms of Roman, Egyptian, Etruscan artifacts to walk through before you get to the SC. So much to take in, and too much to comment upon here. But the round room with "Neros bathtub" as its centrepiece is particularly impressive. But if as I suspect you have come for the frescos alone, then you won't be disappointed. Most rooms and corridors have these, particularly as you get closer to the SCl. No photos are allowed within the Sistine Chapel but not the rest of the museums. The SC was as you can imagine, full of people, noisy, and beautiful all at the same time. Unfortunately, it not a place that you are welcomed to stay in and the default perhaps is to be ushered through to the exit. Try to stay as long as you can, there are seats (if you can get one). Don't stop and look upwards in the walkways you will be shouted at by staff. Standing looking upwards  for a long time can be a strain on the neck, particularly when trying to negotiate the use of varifocile glasses at the same time. Personally I found the Raffaello rooms and his style prior to the SC to be equally if not more inspired frescos. spend some extra time in these before you go on to the SC. Of course, don't mind me this is your visit and its all a matter of what you personally like.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Large impressive collection of art. But the crowds are overwhelming. Booking in advance is a must. Difficult to enjoy the location due to heavy crowds.  In principle you must just go with the flow and jump from one room to another. For me personally, was tiring the visit.  On the other hand : seeing the Sixteenth Chappell, the final stairs …. Brush a way a little the crowd madness :).

title: Vatican Museums
text: My wife and I decided to go with the VIP Key Masters Tour and we were so glad we did. Being in the museum before anyone else was a divine experience. For those who are mobility challenged, there are no places to rest.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Really quite a beautiful place, it has so much here you would need a day to look at it all. Unfortunately it's splendor is let down by the crowds and just how rude most of the tourists are. They seem as though they must get to a place.
It is large enough and is open long enough for you to wait.

title: Vatican Museums
text: really worth a visit. Such a huge museum and you could easily spend hours in here! However the line to enter is super long, even if you buy a ticket online. We got there at 1pm for our 1.30pm slot and got in around 1.40pm.

title: Vatican Museums
text: The guide was very good and the tour is interesting, but too crowded to really enjoy. They should somehow limit the number of visits, either by increasing the price or decreasing the number of daily entries. I literally walked out of the Sistine chapel right away, since it was packed, people sitting in the corners scrolling their phones, feeling of total chaos and disrespect towards a sacred place and the priceless art.

title: Vatican Museums
text: The saying goes you can spend an entire life walking through this museum and never see everything. That's a true statement. So much to see. So little time no matter how long you're there. If you're a museum lover, this is the place to be. If you love history, this is a must not miss. Nothing but positive things to say about the facilities, the people and more. Definitely recommend a guided tour just to help you figure out which way to go and what to see.

title: Vatican Museums
text: The Vatican City is beautiful and full of amazing artwork, old maps, Egyptian artifacts, and the famous Sistine Chapel. There is so much to see and look at but it was so busy that you could barely walk.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
The Vatican Museums are among the most famous in the world – and with good reason. They are absolutely stunning, both inside and out. The atmosphere feels magical, with an energy that is truly surreal. I highly recommend everyone to visit this sacred place at least once in their lifetime.

That said, here are some important notes: officially, you’re not supposed to enter wearing short clothing that shows your arms, stomach, or legs, but I noticed many women inside with short and revealing outfits. Be prepared to dedicate almost an entire day to this visit. Even with tickets in hand, the queues are extremely long, though the security system is very efficient.

My biggest disappointment was with the large tour groups. Honestly, it was overwhelming – crowds moving in packs, stopping everywhere just to take endless photos and videos, often blocking the view and disturbing those who genuinely want to admire the art. I chose to take only a few pictures because I preferred to truly appreciate Michelangelo’s breathtaking masterpieces.

However, the experience was tough: the sheer number of people, the pushing, bumping, and lack of courtesy made it exhausting. I only lasted about two hours before leaving. Since it was August and very hot, I ended up cooling down at a bar nearby with a refreshing lemonade.

Tips: wear light, breathable clothing, prepare for the crowds, and most importantly – good luck!

title: Vatican Museums
text: The Vatican museums are, in a word, overwhelming. That’s not necessarily a bad thing. But you’re never going to see all of these treasures in multiple visits.  Like many museums in Europe, it is, overcrowded. But you expect that going in. Even when you take the so-called skip the line tour towards towards the beginning of the day, you’re still going to wait close to an hour to get in but that may have been more function of meeting our tour guide. The one thing I will say at least is that you don’t really feel like cattle being led to slaughter, except perhaps in the Sistine chapel or in the line to get inside the museum.  It was all in all a good experience.

title: Vatican Museums
text: This was an amazing place to visit but so many things to see. Almost too many. We bought tickets online before we left for our trip which I am glad we did. What they don’t tell you is that yes you get to skip the ticket line but you don’t get to skip the line of people who have their tickets and get in line to get into the museum. This is also the line to get into the Sistine chapel which is after you go through the museum. We had to walk around the outside of St Peter’s square for 2 or 3 blocks and then another 3 or 4 blocks to get to the end of the line that led to the entrance of the museum. There is no other way to get into unless you buy the more expensive tickets. By the time we got to the Sistine chapel I had a sensory overload and didn’t even know I was in the chapel. We finally sat down for a minute and then I started to appreciate what I was looking at. It was amazing! We had tickets to St Peter’s Basilica and I hadn’t allowed enough time in between and we almost missed that. Give yourself about 3 hours between the two places.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Visiting the Vatican during a Jubilee year was incredible — and honestly, a bit overwhelming! The crowds were huge, the energy was intense, and there was so much to take in that it was hard to absorb it all at once. I’m not really into statues or artwork the way some people are, but even so, it was absolutely worth the experience. The sheer scale, history, and sense of reverence are unlike anything else I’ve ever seen.

If you go, plan ahead for long lines and give yourself time to just take it in. Don't be so picture happy that you miss the experience! Even if it’s not “your thing,” standing in a place that holds so much significance and history is something you’ll never forget.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Wasn’t sure what to expect but there is almost too much stuff to see, you could spend the whole day in there and still not see everything! Be prepared for a lot of walking but it is definitely worth it. Would recommend pre booking tickets to avoid cues. A definite must see if your in Rome

title: Vatican Museums
text:
We found the Vatican Museum and Sistine Chapel well worth the visit. A very impressive display of beautiful realics.
Unfortunately, we encountered a staff member in the ticket queue who was so rude and condescending. She could have been helpful but instead chose to be a smarta**.
My critism overall is that signage and wayfinding is lacking in general in the surrounding areas - so give yourself time to find your way -  but we worked it out!!

title: Vatican Museums
text: We had a guided tour and it was great! Very nice guide and we have seen a lot. The downside is the amount of people present, but otherwise a great experience.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Well worth the visit, the Sistine chapel was very busy, full of people, you're only supposed to be in the room for 20mins. In my opinion, not the best thing about the Vatican museum anyway. We spent over 5 hours so worth the money I spent on skip the queue, even though you had to queue in the skip the queue line 🤣

title: Vatican Museums
text: Worthy spend of a few hours, I was slightly underwhelmed by the Sistine Chapel - although magnificent there were plenty of amazing painted ceilings across Italy I thought were just as good. We arrived on time for our passes so only had a 10min wait in line and seems like people tried to get there much earlier than their time so had to wait. The overall experience is huge and once we tried to pick up the pace to get the Sistine chapel there was still a long way to go! The signs are a little bit confusing as to how much museum before you get the chapel but it’s obviously at the end of the tour so not a worry. No photos are allowed of the Sistine Chapel itself but there were also nice gardens to sit and rest before or afterwards.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Vatican Museums

Extraordinary. An absolute must-see.

Pre-book your tickets and be on time — preferably choose the earliest possible entry. You will need the entire day, and even then, it still won’t feel like enough.

The art is truly jaw-dropping: centuries of history unfold before your eyes, culminating in masterpieces by Michelangelo himself. Walking through the museums feels like traveling through hundreds of years of human creativity, faith, and genius.

Be well prepared for a lot of walking, take your time, and let the experience sink in. This is not just a visit — it’s a once-in-a-lifetime journey through art and history.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
A fantastic experience.
I bought the ticket on (Getyourguide) I do not recommend it, though, try to buy it on the website earlier.
You need around 2hs for the visit.
I am posting photos from the Egyptian section.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
A good idea to buy your tickets online to save some time. Long queues so try to come early. Stunning artwork from Michelangelo to Rafaello and even Egyptian artefacts including a mummy… Beautiful artwork everywhere you look from ceilings to floors and all around. Sistine Chappell definitely worth the wait and view at the end of the full museum tour.

Preferable to wear clothes that close your shoulders and knees.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
A great evidence of what ancient masterminds could do and did, the greatest collections of arts and history. I am still amazed how the detailed maps of every region could be drawn back in those days.

A guided tour by the Museum is highly recommended to have the full benefit of the visit. Thank you to my wonderful tour guide!

title: Vatican Museums
text: A highlight to our visit to Rome. Not to be missed visiting the Vatican Museum is taking a walk through the history of the world in 4 hours. We used the audio guide and were able to spend as much time as we liked with objects of interest. Wear comfortable shoes and wear layers if your like us and visiting in January. I can't image how crowded it must be in high season.

title: Vatican Museums
text: A lot of awesome history and beautiful art. Very crowded so if you are easily overwhelmed, prepare yourself. Highly recommend, but it's huge and will wear a person out! Buy tickets in advance, or be prepared to wait hours (not exaggerating, it'll be hours) before you get in.

title: Vatican Museums
text: A must see!! If you are into Egyptian history at all this is full of treasures!!! Including the amazing Sekhmet statues.  I cried in this museum... so much more than just your "typical" museum, if there is such a thing

title: Vatican Museums
text: A must visit in Vantican, too many treasures and too many people. You'd better book your timed entry ticket in advance. The experience was not very pleasant, a tiresome walking including stairs and crowded and not very good air circulation.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
A must visit when visiting Rome. Around 2-4 hours to see it. Be careful the security guards inside the museum, their behaivour is bad and not polite.
Beware not to brother them when they are sitting and play with their smartphones because they get angry.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Absolutely incredible place. St Peters Basilica the highlight, Sistine chapel impressive but maybe not so iconic (based on seeing the basilica right after it)
Again the amount of people makes it not so easy to enjoy and you’re almost herded around. But absolutely a bucket list place to visit if ever in Rome.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Absolutely incredible, this was definitely the highlight of my trip to Rome.  The most beautiful corridor to walk through to the Sistine Chapel, exquisite. It's a long walk and be mindful about going around in circles as it's easy to if you don't take note of the signs.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Absolutely overwhelming in the best way possible. The collection is massive and every room feels like a masterpiece. The Sistine Chapel alone is worth the visit, seeing it in person is unreal. Plan at least 3–4 hours because there is so much to see

title: Vatican Museums
text: Absolutely worth visiting – a cultural and historical must-see. The Vatican Museums house an incredible collection of art, history, and beauty that leaves you speechless. The crowds are truly endless, but we bought our tickets in advance and skipped the entire line, which made the experience much smoother. Every room is impressive, with the highlight being the breathtaking Sistine Chapel. I highly recommend visiting and booking your tickets online beforehand to fully enjoy this unforgettable experience.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Along with Louvre, Uffizi and Ermitage the best Museum in the world. Add the Sistina Chapel to the lot and it's first place is (in our opinion) secured ! The guided visit is a must and the final stop in the Sistina is simply stunning !

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Amazing
Ilana was a terrific guide.
So much history and information.
What a collection of art and artifacts.
Hard to imagine the painters in there day to create 1000s of works of art.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Amazing experience, so much to see: paintings, tapestries and sculptured. We felt we didn't give the Vatican Museums justice even though we spent 3 1/2 hours. Sistine Chapel is breathtaking, so glad we had the opportunity to view it.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Amazing history! Took a little over 2 hours to walk on self guided. So many amazing this to see and the Sistine chapel was breathtaking!!!!!

title: Vatican Museums
text: Amazing place to visit and a must visit! Prepare for a lot of people walking slow. Go early in the week as it will be very packed and you won’t be able to see anything from all of the people! So many things to can see that your mind won’t process it until you sit down later!

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Amazing place, once in a lifetime experience.
Enormous place and be prepared to spend a lot of time.  Also buy tickets in advance and hope for a smaller queue.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Amazing! There’s so much to see but it’s not for one day IMO. What I dislike is the amount of the people there 🙈 Sometimes you can feel like on pilgrimage 😹

title: Vatican Museums
text:
An ABSOLUTELY must-visit place if you come to Rome (even tho it’s in the Vatican). We didn’t book tickets, arrived at 9 and waited one hour in a line (no tickets reserved). The line in the afternoon when we came out was triple the size, so come early as possible if you have no reservation. Of course if you reserve tickets it’s  MUCH quicker. If ANY of the workers outside the liberty telling you “how long you will wait” or that you “can go in right now” don’t list to them unless you wanna pay €50. Regular ticket is ~€20, deducted one (EU citizens under 25) was ~€7-8.
It took 3 hours to go through all the museum, it was extraordinary. Just get ready, because some of the hallways are tight and crowded, but other than that it was really chill. Would go again, definitely worth it.

title: Vatican Museums
text: An amazing place, full of history, amazing arhitecture, fine art and the Sistine chapel. Make sure you book your ticket in advance or you will have to wait in line for a very long time, very, very long time. Not worth wasting precious tine if you are visiting Rome, so just book your ticket in advance. If you are planning to take pictures in the Sistine chapel just know that it is forbidden.

title: Vatican Museums
text: An immense museum. It took us more than 4 hours to visit it. Really worth it. If you're coming on free sunday come a little bit before 7am.

title: Vatican Museums
text: An incredible collection of artworks... and not only that. Unique masterpieces of priceless value, which you honestly need an entire day just to 'take a look' at.The very large crowd and the stunning images create a pleasant headache. You definitely need a second visit to be able to absorb the information - as much as that is possible.Comfortable shoes are a must, tickets in advance and a lot of patience!

title: Vatican Museums
text: As a history lover i truly enjoyed the Musei Vaticani. The amount of historic artifacts is immense. From ancient Egypt to modern history. I very much enjoyed the room with the geographical maps. The Borgia apartments also. The Sistine Chapel is a joy to behold. There is not much roaming like in the Louvre. The "musem path" feels like it is set already so you don't go in circles. I recommend going to the museum the very first day you arrive because you will be tired of walking around the city. The football memorabilia owned by the late Poppe Francesco was incredible.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Beatiful art and the sixtene Chapel. One way direction to walk and even if u go fast it will take at least 2 hours to get true because of the crowds. Buy tickets up front. The line for tickets for the day go around the corner and u will be there for hours in line with no security that u will get a tickets to get in. Dont come early and hope u will get in. They will send u away to wait until it is your time to enter, not a minute sooner.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Beautiful and truely majestic place to visit when in Rome. The artefacts have so much history and are so beautiful, but there are a lot of them. I found the Sistine Chapel a little under whelming, no photos allowed inside so respect that. It's also very crowded with lots of tour groups moving through. Be very careful in St Peter's Square, lots of pick pockets looking to take advantage of the tourist. Buy your ticket in advance, people were queued up and it would be 3 hours before they could enter.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Beautiful museums. More than you can see in a single day! It would take hours in each room to really take it all in. This was my second time there and still feel there is so much more to see!

title: Vatican Museums
text: Beautiful! Must see! While we booked a tour (also recommend) you get history and see a lot of the stuff it has to offer. While at first he said we’d skip a lot after a two hour long history class and hundreds of people I thought it was a great amount of time. If you don’t mind a lot of people (unlike me who HATES when people are in my personal space) you could just raw dog the museum and look at what you want but I enjoyed the group to block heavy flow of people. And everything is absolutely stunning history and art are on every wall this is an absolute must see!

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Beautiful. When they said you can spend an entire day here they weren’t kidding.
Definitely buy tickets ahead of time to avoid the line, and don’t miss the Sistine Chapel!

title: Vatican Museums
text: Began our Vatican Museum visit with the Vatican City Open Bus Tour at 1 pm, after spending the morning marveling at St. Peter’s Basilica. After the 45 min. tour through the gardens, we looked around at some of the less populated exhibits before joining the dense stream of visitors through the museum en route to the Sistine Chapel, catching a glimpse of the magnificent Gallery of Maps and the Raphael Rooms along the way. After appreciating the Sistine Chapel for a while we headed to the courtyard for a bite to eat and then finished up the day with the Carriage Pavilion, reminiscing over the “Pope Mobile” and other exhibited vehicles from over the ages. Would have loved more time and less crowds, but appreciated the opportunity to enjoy this beautiful, amazing place.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Best to book tickets in advance to avoid the very long queue, even for the booked time, make sure you reach there before at least 20mins as you shall expect another long queue to enter.
The museum is impressive with the amount of details it holds and rich history, however i reached a point where i wanted to get out as i spent too much time on details and became overwhelmed.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Book a tour first!
Vitally important as you can skip the lines, which are huge, and we went in the "off season".
Once inside, you can take your time, and you'll need it.
It's massive, and its amazing.
Every room is just stunning.
The artwork, the architecture, the painted ceilings, the long hallways, the tapestries...all absolutely fabulous.
You will have to book the St. Peter's Basilica separately, but visiting the Vatican City is an absolute must whilst in Rome.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Book tickets in advance and preferably from apps like GetYourGuide. It significantly reduced my waiting time! I barely needed to stand in Corridor/Line 3 for 15 mins before being let in. Whereas those who booked online on their website were standing in a very long queue and no clue when they were let in.

Do not rush to the Sistine Chapel and Raphael’s rooms because everything before that is a masterpiece in itself.

Once you enter the museums you have a choice to either directly go to the Sistine Chapel or go through all the museums present. It takes 1h35mins of continuous brisk walking without taking a lot of pictures.

There are many people on guided tours inside that you’ll encounter on your way.

There are designated places to sit and rest for some time.

The walk ends 5-10 mins after you come out of the Sistine Chapel. Photos are prohibited inside the chapel.

There are washrooms near entry and exit points.

They also have many gift shops from where you can purchase magnets, post cards, books etc.

It’s best to reserve half a day for the Vatican museums.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
BRING PASSPORTS or IDs, they are requires although nobody opened ours. Crowds outside are rightout scary. Don't be afraid, it gets much better once they disperse inside the roomy halls of the museum, actual a succession of interconnected museums that easily require walking to exhaustion, say 4 to 6 hours.
The cafeterias leave a lot to be desired. There are plenty of restrooms at every corner. Signage is very weak, leaving one mostly disoriented (a great idea is to download and bring their map, non-existing in printed version).
Crowds seem to dislike the Pinacoteca, the quietest of the museums, by the way offering plenty of seating! Try not to miss departments on your way, since it's very hard to return, not because you can't, but due to the enormous extensions to be covered. Through every window there's a nice view of the tiny country.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Buy ticket in advance and make sure you get the one with guide . The tour with the guide only takes an hour and  after that you can still stay inside the building
If you have ticket go straight to the entrance .
There’s always a big big lane but that’s for people without tickets.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Buy tickets from advance, we took the guided tour with Sophia and it was very good and unique. The only downside is that in warm weather it is very difficult because it is very hot outside and in the indoor

title: Vatican Museums
text: By far the best museums in Rome and in general from all that I have visited. Perfect and biiiig, you need at least 4 hours. Tickets in advance!!! The Egyptian rooms and connection with the Romans and the rest of the artifacts were so interesting

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Definitely reserve your ticket in advance and make sure you follow the instructions on your ticket.
Audio rental available

title: Vatican Museums
text:
DO NOT BOOK WALKER TOURS… FRAUD ALERT

THE MUSEUMS ARE FANTASTIC. THE collection is the best of the best in the world.
Think Louvre Think Smithsonian Think British Museum.

Something new I found out was that there are giving societies in USA or Canada. Your group raises money for restoration or preservation.
I saw a wonderful gift from the Michigan Society. Marvelous idea and I will support in the USA when I get home.

One warning. There is too much to see in one day or your eyes become glazed over. I recommend study the information and pick your favorites. Since I have been here many times, I focused on the Egyptian collection since I am heading to the GEM in Cairo.

The Vatican staff goes out of its way to help. I am disabled and except for a few bumps things went smashingly. The office for special needs or disabled is inside. They are very warm and friendly. I needed help and THE WOMEN DELIVERED. DEC 5th 2:00 pm.

title: Vatican Museums
text: During my recent visit to Vatican City, I had the remarkable opportunity to witness one of the most spiritually significant and historically rich places in the world. This visit reminded me not only of the enduring power of faith but also of humanity's timeless pursuit of beauty and meaning. It was a journey that touched both my heart and soul an experience I will always treasure.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Even though we had a priority entry ticket, we still had to wait for quite a long time. Tickets should definitely be purchased in advance, and choosing the right tour is essential. The atmosphere was amazing.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Excellent museum. You don’t need to arrive too early. I was there at 8 am for the 8:30 entrance and people were still in line getting in at 8:15 so they made me stand in another line.  I thought the audio guide was a waste of money. There are plenty of signs in English. My RS guide book on Rome was good enough. Note that the last judgement wall of the Sistine Chapel is covered with scaffolding now although you can see a bit around it. I spent about four hours to see everything.  My favorites were the Borgia apartments and of course Sistine Chapel. The Etruscan rooms have great views of Rome. I bought my ticket online.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Extensive, exquisite, overwhelming conveying the power and wealth more than I have seen in any palace or museum.
Without a guide you will wander aimlessly and without prebooking a time slot you will spend a few hours queuing up.
Most want to get to the Sistine chapel- but they like to build the suspense... The official guide takes you through the interactive video derailing the frescoes of Sistine so that your 10 mins in the chapel are well spent.
Maybe it's the jubilee year, but even in December the crowds are tremendous.
Set aside a half day for the trip and enjoy it.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Fantastic experience, but a long wait to get entry, even arriving well before the opening time.
Incredible amount of artefacts and exhibitions with enough to keep you occupied for many hours.
The flow through the building is well managed considering the sheer number of people who visit every day.  The staff are friendly and helpful.
Everything is preserved immaculately and it is easy to see everything as the building is well laid out and carefully curated.
There are gift shops and a cafe at the end, toilets at strategic points along the route, and outside there are more gift shops and cafes, so plenty to sustain anyone for a visit.
Tgis will take up a while day of a trip to Rome by the time you have waited to gain admission and been through the building.
The end of the journey through the building is time in the Sistine chapel which is of course a highlight.  I didnt have any photos of this as photography is not allowed in the chapel.
There is a lot of contemporary art to see and to be honest, they seem to have collected unless treasures feom all countries and genres, so would highly recommend.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
For you own sanity buy tickets in advance and even then get there early if not right at opening. I cannot overstate how many people will be trying to get in. I was there on a weekday in November and I shudder thinking about the crowds in peak season.
I'd also recommend splitting the museum and St. Peter's into two different days. You're going to spend a lot of time there. Even if you start early you'll run into peak crowds at the Bassilica when you get out. Crowds aside, what more can you say about this site. The amount of history and art is truly staggering. The only place I've been that's even close is the Royal Museum in London. Just awe inspiring.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Forget about the queues for going here. It's one of the best places I've ever been to. There's so much to see. Once you're in, take your time to look at everything.  The Egyptian stuff at the beginning through to the Sistine chapel are absolutely stunning.
There is a cafe and different food outlets all in my opinion reasonably priced. There's also few gift shops after the Sistine chapel varying in price from around €2 - €300+.
I can't tell you how beautiful this place is. Go visit. Try and get the early entrance if you can. Enjoy 😉

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Get "skip the line" tickets in advance.
I used Headout platform and they were super prompt and helpful.
The tour takes a lot of time, but so worth it!

title: Vatican Museums
text: Get tickets in advance otherwise be prepared to be in line for 2 hrs. Definitely worth going, you will get to see the work of top artists on the walls

title: Vatican Museums
text: Great museum filled with antiques to the point my head was spinning. Visited during rainy evening so the atmosphere outside was only adding to the excitement. The only problems to mention were the noise, amount of people and very high prices on even simple rosaries making it feel more like a tourist trap rather than spiritual place.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Great museum.  Allow at least 2 hours minimum, more if you can. Ends at the Sistine Chapel.  Wear comfortable shoes.  You will go up and down stairs, however, there are some lifts.  You cannot have knees or shoulders exposed to enter the Sistine Chapel. I was there when they opened on a Monday and it was very full after an hour or so.  You definately want to get a skip the line pass or expect a loooong wait to get in.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Had ti line up as no more fast pass tickets..scalpers everywhere trying ti sell you fast tickets for double the price.We waited had lunch in line from a samich place uo the road..Once inside was pretty amazed allspice.. Ikea set up with following the one way ups n downs...Its a big place wear some comfy shoes n bring some snacks.zplenty of stairs too..
Cracking place worth the visit..Some weird n funky things and fun facts

title: Vatican Museums
text: Hell on earth at high season. Absolutely astonishing range of artefacts, but the crowds are something to be reckoned with. Feels a little like organised chaos. The atmosphere and crowds does not lend itself to an enjoyable museum visit. I'd recommend shoulder or low season

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Highlight of any Rome trip.

Tons and tons of artwork and historical artifacts highlighting not only the evolution of catholicism, but humanity.

There is also substantial evidence of whitewashing christianity since the museum is missing a distinct time period between African/Egyptian representation and the appearance of a non-black Jesus…

title: Vatican Museums
text: Highly recommend, so beautiful. Booking tickets in advance is a must. It can get very crowded so highly recommend the earlier the better. Dress code is no shoulders or knees showing, they do turn people away who don't meet this requirement

title: Vatican Museums
text:
I highly recommend this experience if you have an appreciation for history and art.

It is highly recommended to buy a skip-the-line entry ticket in order to miss the long queue outside. During the summer, it can be very hot and during the winter (when I went) it rains a lot. I bought a skip-the-line entry ticket without a guide, because I wanted to browse on my own. If you are into history and art facts, then a guide is a good idea. However, once inside there are audio guides that you can rent for an additional fee. This frees you up to browse at your own pace, but still have the guided experience.

It really is a wonderful experience. The artwork and the statues reminds you of how much history the world has and how small we are in comparison to the global picture. You stand there in awe, but also feel humbled.

I would recommend going early if you want to skip the biggest crowds. I went at 8:30 and now in hindsight I should have gone even earlier, because by 8:30 (in winter) the place was already packed with people.

There are signs that guide you through the entire museum and indicates how far you are from the Sistene Chapel. In certain areas of the museum you are allowed to wander in any direction and in other parts the guards tell you that for the best flow of people you can't go back in the opposite direction.

For me, I would have appreciated if there was a shortcut to the Sistene Chapel instead of forcing you to walk through everything and placing the chapel almost right at the end. By the time you get to the chapel, you are so saturated by all the art on the walls and roofs and carpets that it can happen that the beauty and weight of the art in the chapel is underwhelming. That is just something to consider of you are sensitive to a sensory overload.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
I mean it’s pretty much the best museum there is I feel like I don’t even have to explain.
Just book your tickets in advance and I’d recommend a skip the line ticket as well, unless you’re quite patient then whatever, and cover your knees and shoulders for the chapel which you NEED to see, don’t miss it!

title: Vatican Museums
text:
I recently visited the Vatican Museums, and it was truly one of the best experiences of my trip. The collection is absolutely breathtaking — from ancient sculptures to Renaissance masterpieces and the iconic Sistine Chapel. Every room feels like stepping into a different chapter of history and art.
The museum is huge, but incredibly well-organized, and even with the crowds, the atmosphere stays inspiring. I especially loved wandering through the long galleries and discovering details I’d only seen in books before. Seeing everything in person felt unforgettable.

title: Vatican Museums
text: I recommend taking the guided tour. It takes about 2 hours and you get to learn a lot of details about the lace and the artists. Also you avoid the long waiting line. The place is fantastic.

title: Vatican Museums
text: I suggest you book 2 to 3 months in advance. you can spend 3 to 7 hours in this museum. It’s really big and the paintings are all beautiful. They’re also jewelry and sculptures. there are many guided tours and there’s a cute café where you can eat at, but there are no chairs. it’s a bit crowded, but remember to take your time. however, you aren’t allowed to take photos at the Sistine chapel, but you can take pictures anywhere else. there are many famous Renaissance artists such as MikaAngelo and Rafael.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
I visited the Vatican Museums today. Although my ticket was for 10:30, the long queue meant I only managed to get through security around 11:00. It was extremely crowded and a bit chaotic at first.

However, all the waiting was worth it once I began exploring the museum. There are so much to see and learn — everything are incredibly beautiful and captivating. I was genuinely amazed by how stunning and detailed everything are.

Additionally, I recommend booking tickets well in advance, as they often sell out. You could try to buy tickets on the spot and wait in line, but that takes a lot of time. In my case, I purchased tickets through a travel agent, and they helped me skip the line. That was extremely helpful, although I still needed about 30 minutes to get through security.

Another advantage is that the ticket price is much cheaper if you book well in advance.

Vatican Museums, most beautiful museums I’ve visited in my life ✨🍃🇻🇦💖

title: Vatican Museums
text: I'd you are in Rome, obviously you are coming here. The museum has a whole lot of statues and paintings... From ancient to contemporary... Book your tickets in advance with timed entry. Get the suditoguide from inside or download any free app that can serve as an audio guide.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
I’m not usually a museum person, but I decided to visit this one, and I’m really glad I did. The art and artifacts on display are truly impressive.

The museum is quite large, and you can easily spend a few hours exploring every nook and corner of this vast place. I especially loved the artwork and intricate designs on the walls, ceilings, and floors.

It was a truly memorable experience!

title: Vatican Museums
text: I'm still in awe of what I happened to experience the real master piece. It took us two hours and a half to walk through it with dropped Jaws. If you are in Rome, I believe that it's a must to visit Vatican Museum.

title: Vatican Museums
text: If you are into museums, history and lots of walking, this is the place for you. Buy tickets in advance and come at least 30 min ahead of your scheduled time. Look at the dress code before coming as well.

title: Vatican Museums
text: If you visit Rome, you absolutely must see the Vatican Museums and the Sistine Chapel. Be sure to buy your tickets at least two months in advance, as it can get extremely crowded. Despite the crowds, it’s definitely worth it — the art and atmosphere are unforgettable.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Incredible collection from around the world and particularly the ancient Rome pieces. It's super busy but the guided tour is worth the money just for all the extra information and in some cases to skip the line.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Incredible museum. Absolutely must see in Rome. That said, if you are not there at 8am at the first tour you will not have a good time. It was insanely crowded to the point of claustrophobia even at 10AM.

Make sure you have your ticket with your full legal name that matches your valid form of id. Also getting the tickets in advance is a must. The line to buy them was unreal.

title: Vatican Museums
text: It is beautiful and a bucket list item.  I would highly recommend getting a tour guide, unless u are a massive history buff or have a history art degree.  There is so much art work to admire and than at the end, the Sistine Chapel.  Just bad no photography allowed in the chapel.  Guards and tour guide will stop you from taking picture.  Also get a tour if u plan to go to the basilica, they get to cut the public line and get you in less than 20 mins.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
It is easy to be overwhelmed with so much to see in the Vatican museums. We booked two different tours offered via the official site. The first was of the museum and Sistine chapel that showed us the highlights and we went around again by ourselves before exiting.

The second was the Vatican gardens and Sistine Chapel that allows you to take a break after the garden tour in the cafe then explore more of the museums at your own pace. We had Frank as our tour guide and he brought his harmonica to add a special element to the tour, very engaging and not at all what we had expected. Two days is not enough but definitely better than one tour and buying direct gives a good price to allow yiu to go twice,

title: Vatican Museums
text: It is one of fbe most visited museum in the world. It hosts Vast collections of statues, articles, historical artifacts, porcelains, art works, etc., collected through centuries are kept in one place. One need atleast 4 to 5 hours to go through the collections leisurely. Good time to start is early morning. As time goes, there will be more people. Waiting time is more.

title: Vatican Museums
text: It was a fantastic experience; I spent over two hours walking around and could have easily spent more time there. Please be prepared for many wonderful exhibits. Buy tickets in advance to avoid queues.

title: Vatican Museums
text: It was a great experience. I especially loved the paintings and ceilings. I would recommend the audio guide very much since it is a lot of information to take in alone.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
It would be criminal to come to Rome and not visit the Vatican.
My first time here was 17 years ago during the summer and all I remember is that it was so hot. My clothes felt like it was sticking to my person. This was my fourth visit to Italy and second time here.
This time around it was still unbelievably hot, but we’re older and wiser so our tickets let us cut to the front of the line. And because we had a stroller, they let us cut through everyone else.
Make sure you bring a form of identification because they do in fact check when you go through security. They make sure the name on the ticket matches with the ID.
The entrance can be a little chaotic. The amount of tourists trying to get in seem endless. Unless you have the time to waste by standing in line to get in, I highly recommend buying skip the line tickets so that all you need to do is get to the front of the line when you get there for the time slot you signed up for. The earlier, the better.

Once inside, the biggest challenge are the stairs. They never end. We were constantly having to carry the stroller by holding on to every corner so it wouldn’t be too heavy.

Much like the last time I was amazed at everything that I saw. The opulence. The grandeur.
But boy are you packed like sardines with everyone else. We got so many steps in admiring all the things. Before leaving, we stopped by the cafe right in the middle of the garden for a quick snack.

There’s lots to see here I’d probably come back the next time I’m in Rome.

title: Vatican Museums
text: It's a Jubilee year in 2025. We were glad to enter the Vatican through the holy door. Loved everything we saw. Highly recommend booking a tour with a licensed tour guide. The skip-the-line is a must; otherwise, the wait can be excruciating.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Its very huge! You need minimum 2 hours from the start untill the end. Sistine Chapel is at the end of the museum, so make sure you make right turn since its like one way so you can't easily go back and change direction. Buy ticket in advance is recommended, cause if you buy at the kiosk in front mean you must line up twice. One for ticket and one for museum entry

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Magic! The best museum I have ever been to. We did the circuit twice. Once with a guide and once with Rick Steves audio Europe app which I highly recommend. The Pinacoteca gallery was, other than the Sistine Chapel, was the highlight for me.
I recommend booking a tour on the Vatican website for a morning slot, the tour ends just before the Chapel and you are allowed to stay in the gallery's until closing.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Magnificent experience! Most of the people just rush to Raphael and Capella Sistina to see Michelangelo, but you can spend all day long to see all the beauty it offers you. Don't worry, it has cafeteria to take a breakfast or lunch, if you'll get hungry.
Do book your tickets online, more than one month in advance in order to avoid extremely long ticket queue and overcharge by resellers. Though, guided tours can offer you valuable information on art and spare you of organizational burden.
Regardless of that, you'll still need to wait for the scheduled entrance time queue and security check queue. But once you're in, it all worth it!

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Marvellous museum. It's highly recommended to get your tickets in advance from the Musei Vaticani website. The audio guide was worth the extra €8 for the added context of the art and artifacts on display. They have one for children as well at €5. I note that those without tickets bought in advance can still go in, however they'll have to queue at a separate queue downhill from the museum. For those with tickets bought in advance, note which Passageway is written on your ticket. It will determine which queue to join at the entrance.

There will be plenty of people so coming at the earliest time slot is advantageous. As with many Rome attractions, there is a security check at the entrance. You do not need to remove your coats or anything from your bag. Avoid bringing large backpacks to this museum.

I also observed that there are sections in the museum which are not wheelchair friendly. Noticed several visitors with guests on wheelchairs, having issues proceeding to next halls. There are plenty of narrow stairwells in this ancient building and few lifts.

Due to time constraint, I was not able to view all exhibits as thoroughly as I wished and only spent 2 hours there. Though I can easily imagine myself spending more than half a day here. Note the Sistine Chapel opens after 9am and will be rather crowded by 10ish onwards.

Once you are done with the museum sights, there is thankfully a cafeteria one floor down from the main area to recharge. The meal set was nice with croissant with coffee and/or juice. There is also a post office inside the museum itself in case you want to post a special Vatican postcard.

title: Vatican Museums
text: Marvellous museum. One of the largest collections in the world. It contains every possible Christian artifact and also some of the rarest exhibits from all over the world. In fact, one will take a whole week to have a good view of everything. We could only glance at objects here and there and move on. The building is very beautiful with magnificent roof paintings. The exhibits are all arranged artistically.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Me and my boyfriend visited the museum on a Friday in January, which turned out to be the ideal time to go. There was almost no queue at the entrance and very few visitors inside, allowing us to explore at a relaxed pace.

We spent several hours wandering through the exhibitions and never once felt rushed or bored. The museum itself is truly beautiful, with an impressive amount to see and experience. Without a doubt, it is well worth a visit.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Must visit! Buy tickets in advance (like 2-3 weeks) to skip the line! Can only enter when ticket time is reached and they will ask for IDs.

It’s so crowded everyday and it is hard to see everything in one day, definitely come back if I got a chance. They don’t have much AC either, bring a portable fan or visit in winter!

I love their gift shops! They also have a post office inside, though I still haven’t received my postcard.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Must visit.

The Vatican Museums feel less like a single museum and more like a long, unfolding journey through human creativity. From the moment you enter, it’s clear this isn’t a place meant to be rushed. Corridor after corridor reveals layers of art, history, and symbolism, each more detailed than the last.

What stands out most is the sense of accumulation. Statues, tapestries, maps, and frescoes aren’t isolated pieces — they feel like chapters in a story built over centuries. The Gallery of Maps is especially striking, not just for its scale but for how precise and confident it feels, even by modern standards. Every ceiling, wall, and floor seems to demand attention.

And then there’s the Sistine Chapel. After hours of visual richness, the space feels both overwhelming and strangely quiet. Looking up at Michelangelo’s ceiling is less about spotting individual scenes and more about absorbing the sheer ambition of it. Despite the crowds, there’s a collective hush that settles in, as if everyone understands they’re witnessing something beyond ordinary art.

What makes the Vatican Museums memorable is how physically and mentally immersive they are. By the end, you’re tired, inspired, and slightly overloaded — but in the best way. It’s not just about masterpieces; it’s about experiencing how art, faith, and power shaped history, all within a space that still feels deeply alive.

title: Vatican Museums
text: My advice is to book in the afternoon so you can avoid crowds and waiting. Also be prepared to walk a lot and spend a good few hours so you don't miss anything. It was an unforgettable experience, definitely a must see.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
No visit to Rome would be complete without a stopover to Vatican City.

Situated on the banks of the Tiber River, sprawling over 109 acres within a 2-mile border, the Vatican is forever associated with the Catholic Church and began with the construction of a basilica over St. Peter’s grave in Rome in the 4th century A.D. The name Vaticanus traces its etymology back to the word “vaticinium,” which means “oracle,” since many psychics used to occupy the place during the Roman Classical Antiquity.

Millions of tourists make their way to the world’s smallest independent city-state each year, whether they be devout pilgrims, art enthusiasts, history buffs, or just hopelessly curious. No passports required!

Founded under the papacy of Clement XIV in the middle of the 18th century, the Vatican Museums were established to display the extensive papal collection, where my partner, our friends, family member, and I came in mid-September as first-timers. Reservations were easily made at the Vatican official website for 6 persons at the 8:30am tour, which we were lucky to get and was reasonably priced.

On the day of the tour, don’t arrive too early like us. The tickets were timed, and we had to step off the line until 30 minutes beforehand.

After going through security, which did not take too long, we were on our way to pick up our audio headset and enjoyed the art and history deemed awe-inspiring. With thousands of people, one is herded through miles of long, huge, and spacious corridors, everyone inching along towards the prize of the Sistine Chapel. Our guide, to Michaela's credit, tried to point out and describe certain artworks. However, the art one’s passing is almost impossible to see due to the incredible multitude of people. The route back was much less crowded, as most seem to go on to St. Peter's Basilica right from the Chapel.

What an incredible experience, which we can recommend.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
Phew, it was so overwhelming. In a good way and a bad way at the same time. I think you should be mentally prepared for it as an adventure, but you won't be disappointed, especially if you're a fan. First, you MUST buy tickets in advance, well in advance, otherwise you will have to stand in a very long line. When you approach one of the corners of the Vatican wall, you will see two lines, the one that moves quickly is for people with tickets. I had a ticket for 1 p.m., and I had to stand in line for about 20-30 minutes, but to be fair, I got inside at about one 1 p.m. Although the line looked terrible. Also, ask which line is for which time, because they are different. Bring something to drink and eat with you; you can do this in specially designated areas.
Then you can either join your tour group or go explore everything on your own or with friends. In any case, the first "stop" is Eastern Antiquities, the Gregorian Museum. Then follow the crowd, passing by lots of different museums and exhibitions. The highlight will be the so-called "Raphael Rooms" and the equally beautiful work of his rival, Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel. This is a MUST-SEE. The feelings you get when you go inside cannot be conveyed in words or photographs (and besides, photography is not allowed there). It's a bit chaotic, with staff constantly shouting "NO PHOTOS," but there are benches around the perimeter where you can sit and admire the beauty of the frescoes on the walls and ceiling of the chapel. At the end of the tour, you can buy souvenirs. I bought a guidebook and regretted not buying it earlier, because it really explained what was going on around us. And there was a lot going on. I believe that if you are visiting Rome, this is one of those places that is a must-see. And don't forget to visit the Pinacoteca if you are an art fan, as it has many beautiful religious paintings and masterpieces: Giotto, Poussin, Caravaggio, Da Vinci, Raphael, and more.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
The sheer scale and richness of the collection are breathtaking—you’re surrounded by centuries of art, history, and culture at every step. Walking through the galleries felt like moving through time, from classical sculptures to Renaissance masterpieces.

The highlight, of course, was the Sistine Chapel. No amount of photos or descriptions can capture the impact of standing beneath Michelangelo’s ceiling—it’s absolutely awe-inspiring. I also loved exploring the Raphael Rooms and the Gallery of Maps, which were equally impressive.

The museums are very well-organized, and the staff were helpful and professional. It does get very busy, so I’d recommend booking tickets in advance and arriving early to make the most of your visit. Even with the crowds, the atmosphere was incredible, and it’s clear this is a place that leaves a lasting impression on everyone who visits.

title: Vatican Museums
text: The Trevi Fountain is stunning and well worth the visit. Its intricate baroque design, flowing water, and lively atmosphere make it unforgettable. Even with the crowds, the fountain’s beauty and charm capture the essence of Rome—especially when tossing in a coin for good luck.

title: Vatican Museums
text: The Vatican Museum is one of the most beautiful and fascinating museums in the world. It is an amazing walk through centuries of art, sculptures and artefacts. From the Renaissance paintings through to the Sistine Chapel it is a complete sensory overload. I found the painted ceilings absolutely amazing, I really appreciate the attention to detail that has gone in to painting them. The Vatican garden at the end of the tour is a great place to sit and reflect on everything that you have seen.

title: Vatican Museums
text: The Vatican Museum itself is breathtaking.  Everyone should see it at least once in their lifetime, the history is amazing.  But, ve ready for crowds like you have bever seen.  If you go with a tour, be sure they offer "skip the line" tickets otherwise your wait to get in will be hours long.

title: Vatican Museums
text:
The Vatican Museums and Sistine Chapel were absolutely mind-blowing! Seeing Raphael’s and Michelangelo’s masterpieces in person was surreal, the details, colors, and scale are just incredible. Truly a once-in-a-lifetime experience!
