title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Its The Met, it will always be a 5/5 rating.

Although, I do have some tips and recommendations. Always purchase tickets online for smoother entrance experiences. Do not carry large items with you, they will ask you to throw it out or leave it. Take your time, please do not rush. I was able to spend perhaps 3 hours just in the American Wing when I had already been to The Met 4 times before and always skim past that section. When you walk through all the galleries, you get to some you wouldn’t have gotten to if you were rushing.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Just WOW! Located across from City Park. Lines go quickly.  We bout tickets at the counter inside for $30 each! Amazing museum with diverse collections.  Enjoyed the limited time display of Egypt.  Easy free audio guide through my smart phone.  Bring ear buds! Lots of clean bathrooms. Not crowded yesterday November before Thanksgiving. No parking.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Located on the UES and right next to Central Park, just a few blocks from the Guggenheim, the Met is a must‑see and easy to pair with other iconic UES spots. Inside, you’ll find everything from the Arms and Armor collection to the Egyptian wing, European paintings, modern art, and rotating special exhibits. If you’re already in the area, you might also consider stopping by the Neue Galerie, the Frick Madison, or taking a walk through Central Park afterward

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Man Ray exhibit was fantastic. Ancient Egypt was also very cool. So big, impossible to see all in 1 visit. upstairs musical instruments room was awesome and the armor and swords too. loved the big open natural light room where the Egyptian tomb was. Really great place.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Many people had called it the greatest museum of their lives, so my expectations were high — yet it did not disappoint me in the slightest.

The Egyptian antiquities displayed on the first floor were pure awe. I was stunned by the exceptionally high-quality artworks that seemed impossible to have been created thousands of years ago. If I had had more time, I would have loved to spend about 12 hours exploring every detail.

I’m even considering coming back to New York solely to visit the Metropolitan Museum again.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Massive museum ground and extremely friendly staff at the admission desk. It was a rainy day when I visited, coat check had a long line but move fairly fast. Admission line was short despite the crowd. The lady helped me with my membership was above and beyond and super friendly. My visit was off an excellent start from here and on.

Exhibits are packed with artifacts. You can really stay whole day and more here.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Must spend more than half a day to explore. So many different rooms and exhibits to see and soak in the full experience of all of these wonderful exhibitions. Staff are very understanding with how convoluted and maze-like this place feels; they’re happy to direct you to the next room you’re interested in visiting. Lots of people are attending whether it's photoshoots, marriage proposals, or families out for a trip. Lots of detail in the interior of the building, great attention to detail with lighting and positioning of all the exhibits and displays.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: My first time visiting and it was incredible. The museum is absolutely huge — there’s no way to experience everything in one day. The music exhibit paired with the audio tour is a MUST! 🎶

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
My first visit to The Met was honestly overwhelming in the best way. Walking into the Egyptian galleries, the scale of the statues and artifacts immediately sets the tone. The lighting and space are beautifully done and make you slow down without realizing it.

From European paintings to sculptures and the armor collection, every section feels thoughtfully curated. The building itself is just as impressive — the central hall and glass ceilings give the museum a calm, open feeling even when it’s busy.

I’d strongly recommend setting aside at least half a day. Weekdays are better if you want a bit more breathing room, but some popular areas will still have short waits. Even if you’re not an art expert, this place is absolutely worth it and easily a must-visit in New York.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
My wife and I had an incredible visit at the Metropolitan Museum of Art, thanks to our knowledgeable tour guide, Georgia. and others who were a part of the Jehovah's Witnesses tour group. As Jehovah's Witnesses, we especially appreciated how she connected historical artifacts to biblical accounts, offering a spiritually enriching experience.

The most impactful part of the tour was learning the specific reasons behind each of the plagues God used to free the Israelites from the Egyptians. The insights were absolutely amazing!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: New York City is home to some of the most magnificent museums in the world. History, Art, Culture come alive. Greek and Roman Art, Egyptian, Asian. Art of the Arab lands, Turkey, Iran.. and much more. The 4,5,6 train offer easy access to the museum. The cost ?? Well if you are a New York resident, the cost is what you are willing to donate… Take a trip you won’t be disappointed..

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: New York is so well known for the Art and galleries in it but the best experience is the Met Museum. Especially if you’re new to the city of New York. The changing exhibitions are powerful and challenging norms. The information and the items, the order - everything is just wow.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Obviously a must see if you are visiting NYC! I went with a friend on a Friday and it wasn't as busy as I expected it to be. We were there for about 3 hours and only got through 4 exhibits, you really could spend a lifetime exploring this museum. If you have a NY Public Library card you can get a voucher for FREE for 2 people! I thought for sure all the passes would be claimed already but I checked only 3 days in advance and they had some available! Support your local library and museums!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Obviously great exhibits. Perhaps also obviously, it can get very crowded. You should just be aware of that going in, especially if you're not from NYC. I think permanent exhibits were generally less crowded than popular temporary ones (e.g. Divine Egypt). You won't be able to see the whole museum in a day, so don't try--you're better off picking a few wings/exhibits and focusing.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Oh the met what can I even say about my favorite museum on earth. I have gone here over a dozen times and always see a new intriguing exhibit. I love the date nights they have, the free tours, pay what you wish for NY students, and all the different types of art. Sad the rooftop is now closed but excited for the new wing!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: One of the best museums I've been to. They've done a fabulous job building out the exhibits to almost feel as if you were there. This is a place to spend the entire day to really take in.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: One of the best museums I’ve ever visited. I spent 4 hours here and it wasn’t anywhere near enough. I recommend spending at least 2 full days if you want to enjoy the whole collection. I especially like the fashion exhibition which is renewed every May after the Met Gala.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
One of the best museums, a must-visit in NYC!
•Went here initially on a Wednesday, days after the snowstorm and it was closed. No announcement online but there was a signage on the entrance.
•Returned to finally see the Met and it was totally worth it!! Amazing collections and interactive displays!
•An absolute treasure. Two million objects, 5,000 years of history, and enough to fill a dozen visits.
•The Egyptian Wing, European paintings, and rooftop garden alone make it worth the trip. Wear comfortable shoes, arrive early on weekends, and don’t try to see it all — just pick a few wings and enjoy. One of the best museums in the world.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
•Staff are very kind and helpful.
•Clean, well-maintained areas.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: One of the reason why we came back to visit NYC was to dedicate a day to visit MET. It is huge and you want to take in everything. It easy to navigate though with the map provided and personnels are helpful. If you have limited time, the key is to look at the map and just prioritize what you to want to see the most. But then you can always come back as we planned to do.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Parking in the garage was easy. Its attached to the museum so you can leave your coats and bags in the car, no need to drag them along even on a cold day! At some point I lost my parking ticket but the charge wasn't terrible, about $50.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Pay what you wish pricing for NYC residents is amazing. The line for the roof can be intense on weekends but it's still such a fantastic experience to walk from room to room and see so many iconic pieces; I felt spoiled.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Plan to spend a lot of time here. Do some research before your visit to check which areas of interest you definitely want to see, note their location and head directly there first. Check your coat when you arrive, it's free. Stop to eat and have a coffee break. Everything is beautifully displayed and the Cubism galleries are excellent.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Really enjoyed my time at the MET. There were so many exhibits and it was so fun to walk around and explore. We were able to see the rooftop before it closes and the view was amazing.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Really large and an amazing museum.

You will need atleast one full day to see everything.

Really amazing exhibitions and staff. A must visit when visiting NYC

Please go early in the morning as it gets ultra crowded during the day.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Security at the entrance do not allow food, to be brought inside, only new bottled water. Thankfully, my friend bought me a sandwich, once inside the museum. The museum is a huge place to explore many beautiful treasures of the world. I liked the display of beautiful musical instruments from of old. I, even, got to see, close-up, the iconic 1950s Gibson, Les Paul, guitar, behind glass. There are, so, many things to see... Hopefully, we will visit the museum more in the spring when the weather is nice.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Spent a couple of hours here, but could easily have spent longer. Amazing galleries of endless paintings, musical instruments, so many more & a personal favorite -  with suits of armor, including one of Henry VIII's.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Spent a lovely day at the museum. It had been several years since I had been there. Some exhibition rooms are being updated, but there is plenty to see. Highly recommend doing the free tour with the guides. Austin, was wonderful orator, full of descriptive information on the pieces we stopped at. Then we got lost and saw more of the museum.  BTW - I heard the roof top bar maybe renovated to expand the top floor. Visit soon to get a look at the view.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Such a wonderful museum! All of exhibits were laid out nicely. Everyone was friendly and helpful. Such a great place to spend an afternoon looking through amazing works of art and history!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Super cool experience. My wife and I spent at least 3 hours inside and I know for a fact we didn’t see all of the exhibits. Here’s my advice to maximize your experience.
-Purchase tickets in advance.
-You are allowed to bring a camera, just follow the rules of the museum, no flash photography.
-There are several cafes inside so don’t worry about food.
-Wear comfortable shoes because you will be doing a lot of walking.
-If you can avoid it, don’t bring a bag. There’s going to be a lot of people exploring exhibits and it makes it easier to flow through traffic.
-Get a map and plan your route.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Thank goodness for the privately funded PUBLIC collections. While the many assume exhibitions of this size, prestige, AND diversity may not be accessible to those with limited budgets, NYC institutions such as the MET strive to make these works financially accessible for viewing to as many as possible. CHECK THINGS OUT BEFORE ASSUMING THE ARTS COMMUNITY DOESN'T CARE.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The best museum experience I’ve ever had! You need more than 1 day to visit all the exhibitions. Absolutely must visit place in NY!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The collection is strikingly vast and diverse, featuring an impressive array of art that spans Persian, Turkish, and African cultures, among others. Each piece tells a unique story, yet the origins of some items remain shrouded in mystery, raising questions about their journey to this collection. Despite these uncertainties, the overall condition of the collection is outstanding, offering a stunning visual experience for visitors. The knowledgeable staff on-site enhance the experience, providing insights and context that bring the artwork to life. However, whispers circulate about the troubling history of many items, suggesting that some may have been taken unlawfully and later acquired by American collectors, adding a layer of complexity to this otherwise beautiful display.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Divine Egypt Exhibit at the Met was fabulous. If you have even a slight interest in archaeology and history, you must visit. Located on the second floor, there was so much to take in. The artifacts, sculptures, and scrolls were fascinating. I enjoyed learning about the ancient Egyptian world and how humans interacted with their gods and goddesses. One thing I would suggest is having a tour guide, as it would have helped us to take in the information more deeply.

This is not a one-day trip unless you’re ready to wear yourself out. The museum is huge and like a maze, where you could easily get lost without a map. Although we passed through various sections of the museum, we didn't get to see everything. So make sure to plan your visit accordingly, especially if you’re visiting from out of town.

Looking forward to returning to see what other works of art they have on display.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Egypt exhibition at the Met is outstanding. Stunning artifacts, rich history, and beautifully curated galleries. Easily one of the highlights of the museum—don’t miss it.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The experience at The MET is outstanding. I could have spent the entire day here. I would recommend deciding before you arrive what sections you want to visit, otherwise you will get overwhelmed trying to take in everything. I personally enjoyed Egypt and the paintings the most.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The immersive exhibits are great. Loved the halls with the statues and the temple.

I’ve been there 3 times in 1.5months and I find myself returning to the temple of dendur, the statue halls, and the Asian art wing.

Students in NY is pay as u wish!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Met in NYC is one of those rare places that somehow exceeds the hype. The moment you walk in, you feel the weight of history, culture, and artistry from every corner of the world. The collection is massive — truly something for everyone — but it never feels overwhelming. Every gallery is intentional, beautifully curated, and full of details that pull you in.

What I appreciated most was how easy it was to get lost in time. One minute you’re in ancient Egypt, the next you’re staring at a Van Gogh that stops you in your tracks. The staff is friendly, the layout is intuitive, and the entire experience feels like stepping into a different world for a few hours.

If you’re in NYC, this is a non-negotiable stop. It’s not just a museum — it’s an experience you carry with you long after you leave.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The MET is a world famous museum showcasing art, photography, statues, and much more.

We came during the date night hours, and the place was very crowded, but didn't feel overwhelming. The layout is quite large and confusing though, and we got lost in the museum a few times while visiting.

The exhibits and artifacts on display are all intriguing to look at, with many of them being unique and ones I've never seen showcased at other museums. The exhibits are also changing throughout the year, and it offers many different things to go see.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Met is always worth a visit, and make it a day-long visit, if you can, there's so much to see. Or, go there for a specific exhibit and focus on that. Personally, I find that hard, as you invariably will pass other things that look amazing. 😃
Admission is pay-what-you-wish for NY residents.
What was unfortunate were the big crowds and long lines for coat check. Try to stick to weekdays.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The MET is definitely one of the top 5 museums to visit while in NYC and probably the top 1 to consider visiting if you are in the city for a short time.

Buy tickets online will save you time at the counter. The museum opens at 10, and there was already a long queue forming outside to enter.

The depth and quality of the art pieces are very good, with extensive artefacts ranging from Antiquity to modern history. Strolling through the museum will set you back in time and can be overwhelming, in a positive way.

As the museum is very big, do some research ahead to select the areas you’d like to see in priority. Around certain art exhibited it could get busy but that moves pretty fast.

The rooftop on level 5 offers. Nice view over Central Park and manhattan.

Finally, wear your most comfortable shoes as the museum is a maze that surely will amaze you.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The MET is so great! Tons of art and categories / genres in areas to visit. Secret- after 4:30pm it is free to guests. Enjoy the steps, cafe, Central Park is close by and the food trucks!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The MET is the biggest museum I’ve ever visited, covering an incredible range of fields, cultures, and time periods. The name truly says it all. You get endless insights into art and history, and seeing original works by masters like Van Gogh, Picasso, Rembrandt, and many others is simply breathtaking. The collection spans ancient civilizations, European painting, modern art, fashion, and more - you could easily spend multiple days here and still not see everything.

The building itself is impressive, the exhibitions are well curated, and the experience feels both educational and inspiring.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Met offers an unparalleled range of art from across history and the globe. Highlights include:

- European Paintings: Iconic works such as Van Gogh’s Self-Portrait, Vermeer’s Young Woman with a Water Pitcher, and Rembrandt’s portraits.

- African Art: Masks, sculptures, and textiles reflecting centuries of cultural traditions and storytelling.

- Egyptian Art: Mummies, tomb artifacts, and monumental works such as the Temple of Dendur, highlighting ancient Egyptian history and beliefs.

- Greek and Roman Art: Stunning sculptures like the marble Kouros and Roman sarcophagi, showcasing classical form, mythology, and daily life.

- Asian Art: Calligraphy, ceramics, and sculptures spanning thousands of years, including Persian carpets, Japanese samurai armor and Chinese scrolls.

- American Wing: Paintings, furniture, and decorative arts depicting U.S. history, with works like Mary Cassatt’s portraits.

The museum can get busy, but the layout and signage make navigation manageable. Each section is immersive, educational, and awe-inspiring.

It's iconic, educational, and essential for art lovers.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The MET was amazing! An immersive history lesson, you could visit multiple times and find something you missed the previous visit. My favorite part was the warriors segment that showed their armor and swords. The Macedonian / Greek / Roman exhibit was also phenomenal. The Americana section as well. Wow. Just so impressed

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Metropolitan Museum is truly one of the most beautiful museums in the world — I consider it second only to the Louvre. Walking through its halls was a unique and unforgettable experience for me. The collection is vast and fascinating, and every section tells a story of art, history, and culture. A must-visit place in New York City!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Metropolitan Museum of Art - MET is a spectacular museum in New York. It is the leading museum in the USA. There are so many exhibits that it is not possible to view all within a day. The museum caters the need for almost all sorts of visitors. It has a large and rich collection of artifacts from around the world, starting from Egyptian, Bygentine, Greek, Ottoman to Chinese and Indian civilization. It also has a large collection of European paintings and sculpture. The management is nice. The store is rich. The curating is praiseworthy.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Metropolitan Museum of Art (“The Met”) in New York is one of the largest and most impressive art museums in the world. Its vast collection spans over 5,000 years of history – from ancient Egyptian temples and Greek sculptures, through European masters like Rembrandt and Van Gogh, to modern and contemporary works. The building itself is grand, and wandering its halls feels like traveling through time and cultures. It can get crowded, but the sheer scale and richness of the exhibits make it an unforgettable experience. You could spend an entire day here and still not see it all.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City is undoubtedly the best art museum in the United States and one of the greatest in the world. Their Egyptian and Greek sections were incredible, while the Expressionist section was amazing. My favorite part was the collection of paintings by prominent figures like Monet and Van Gogh. It’s difficult to see everything in one day, so I recommend spending at least three days there.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Metropolitan Museum of Art is an absolute must-visit for anyone in New York City. With its immense collection spanning thousands of years and cultures, you can explore everything from ancient Egyptian artifacts to European masterpieces.

One standout piece is Emanuel Leutze’s “George Washington Crossing the Delaware” — a truly iconic painting that captures the drama and heroism of American history. Seeing it in person is much more powerful than any textbook or print.

The museum’s layout is vast and can be overwhelming, so plan your visit ahead of time.

Highlights like the American Wing, European paintings, and the Arms and Armor galleries are not to be missed. Staff are helpful, and there are plenty of resources to guide your visit, but it’s easy to spend hours wandering and still feel like you’ve barely scratched the surface.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Metropolitan Museum of Art is an absolute treasure trove of human creativity and history. Whether you're an art aficionado or a casual visitor, the Met offers an unforgettable experience that spans centuries and continents.
From the moment you step into the iconic Fifth Avenue building, you're transported into a world of artistic wonder. The museum’s vast collection is mind-blowing—everything from ancient Egyptian artifacts and classical Greek sculptures to Renaissance masterpieces and modern American art. The Temple of Dendur, set against a reflecting pool and natural light, is a breathtaking highlight.
One of the most impressive aspects is how well-curated and accessible the exhibits are. Each gallery flows seamlessly into the next, and the signage and audio guides provide just the right amount of context without overwhelming you. The European Paintings wing is a personal favorite—seeing works by Van Gogh, Rembrandt, and Vermeer up close is a surreal experience.
The museum is massive, so plan to spend several hours—or even multiple visits—to truly appreciate it. The rooftop garden offers stunning views of Central Park and the Manhattan skyline, making it a perfect spot to take a break.
Staff are knowledgeable and helpful, and the facilities are clean and well-maintained. Whether you're visiting solo, with family, or on a date, the Met is a must-see cultural landmark in NYC.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Metropolitan Museum of Art is not only one of the largest museums in the U.S., but also in the entire world. Because of its vast collection, it’s incredibly easy to get lost if you go in without a plan. I highly recommend joining one of the free guided tours — it’s the best way to see the highlights and hear the fascinating stories behind them. Beyond that, grab a museum map, mark the sections that interest you most, and explore those areas. Otherwise, trying to see the entire museum in one go is nearly impossible — even three full days wouldn’t be enough!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Metropolitan Museum of Art offers a captivating journey through art history. The diverse collections span various cultures and time periods, providing a wealth of knowledge. Visitors can explore ancient artifacts, stunning paintings, and intricate sculptures. Exploring the museum's vastness requires dedicating ample time for thorough exploration. Each exhibit tells a unique story, inviting contemplation and appreciation. The museum's scale and richness guarantee a rewarding experience for all visitors. Many find themselves spending hours immersed in the exhibits. It is an enriching and memorable experience to be had.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The Metropolitan Museum of Art was an incredible experience—like walking through the history of the world in one place, from Sudan to Chile and everywhere in between. If you’re planning a visit, set aside at least five hours; the museum is vast, and rushing it would be a mistake. Beyond the classical and historical collections, there are also some delightfully “funny” and abstract works that really stretch your imagination—pieces like I Dreamt I Saw Figure 5 in Gold demand creativity and patience to truly appreciate. It’s the kind of museum that challenges how you see art, culture, and time itself.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Metropolitan Museum of Art, The Met, is truly an extraordinary place and one of the most impressive museums in the world. Every lover of history, art, and culture should make it a priority to visit this iconic landmark. From the moment you step inside, you immediately sense the scale and significance of what surrounds you. The museum is not just a building filled with objects — it is a journey through centuries of human civilization.

The space is thoughtfully divided into geographical and cultural areas, allowing visitors to travel across continents and eras in a single visit. You can explore ancient Greece and admire classical sculptures and pottery, then move on to the fascinating world of ancient Egypt, where monumental statues, intricate jewelry, and even a reconstructed temple transport you thousands of years back in time. The American Wing showcases the artistic and historical development of the United States, while the European galleries present masterpieces of painting, sculpture, and decorative arts from different periods.

Beyond these major sections, The Met offers countless additional collections, from arms and armor to musical instruments, from Asian art to medieval artifacts. Each room has its own atmosphere, and every corridor reveals something unexpected. It is easy to lose track of time as you move from one exhibition to another, constantly discovering new details and stories.

One important piece of advice: be prepared. There is simply no realistic way to see everything in one day. The museum is vast, and rushing through it would mean missing much of what makes it special. It is better to choose a few sections that interest you most and explore them properly. Ideally, you would return more than once.

A visit to The Met is not just sightseeing — it is an experience that broadens perspective and deepens appreciation for human creativity and history.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The Metropolitan Museum of Art, usually just called the MET, is one of those places that impresses you the moment you walk in, both because of its scale and its sheer variety. At first it can feel a bit overwhelming, but in a good way, as if the museum invites you to get lost inside it. Each gallery feels like a shift in time or geography, and that constant change keeps the experience fresh and engaging.
The collection is incredibly rich and wide-ranging, covering everything from ancient civilizations and classical masterpieces to fashion and more modern works. Even without a specific plan, you’re almost guaranteed to stumble upon something that catches your attention, whether it’s an Egyptian artifact or a painting you didn’t expect to connect with. It’s not really a museum you try to “complete”, you just move at your own pace and let curiosity guide you.
Overall, the MET feels more like an experience than just a museum visit. It asks for time, comfortable shoes, and a relaxed mindset, but it gives a lot back in return. You leave with the feeling that you’ve encountered something truly significant, even if you’ve only seen a small fraction of everything it has to offer.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
The most amazing museum I have ever seen: in breadh and depth of its colection, quality of presentation, engagement, culture and dynamics. They constantly rotate many exhibitions, so it is impossible to follow everything even if you visit them every week.

Note that this is classical museum: they shsre with you arts and contexts, the opinions development is up to you. Though, they have dedicated space for very little kids, too.

The museum organizes a variety of programs, including in-gallery concerts, movie screenings, lectures, and workshops for all ages, many of which are free with the museum admission. They also have a an impressive team of devoted volunteers running insightful free museum tours.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: The museum has so much to see. I think I could stay for a month, still not see everything, and would never get bored. It always felt comfortable to walk around, even with a lot of people there, I never felt overly crowded. The museum workers were amazing and plentiful! Every person I asked for information or directions (which I did a lot of) to a particular exhibit were very friendly and helpful.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: There is a reason why the Met is world famous. The sheer volume of art is incredible. I came just to catch the Divine Egypt exhibit and ended up staying for a few hours - allowing myself to get lost and immersed in all of the beauty. The John Wilson exhibit “Witnessing Humanity” was excellent!!! Life is brighter and the world has more colors after a visit here!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: There is so much to look at, you need to spare and entire day with this museum. The Egyptian section was my favorite and alot of history to read into. This is great for someone who studied these stuff. Roughly $30 per person

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: There is something for everyone here. Although I'm not a huge fan of the Andy Warhol-esque contemporary art, I LOVE surrealism and discovered the Wilfredo Lam exhibit which was amazing.  Too many great things to list and an overall fantastic experience.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: There’s so much to see from ancient artifacts to modern paintings, every room had something that caught my eye. It’s big, so plan plenty of time, but it’s absolutely worth it. The staff are helpful and the galleries are well organized.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: This is a HUGE museum with so many pieces! Highly recommended and a must visit if you have the time.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: This is my 3rd time coming to this museum in the last 10 years. You can pay what you want if you are a New York resident, so bring your state ID. This is the painting that brought my attention. From afar it looked really dim and was astonished at how an artist can really showcase the lighting of a sunset with oil painting. My daughter loved the Joan of Arc painting.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: This is one of my favorites ever! I come here all the time. It always makes me feel so much better. The art is spectacular and the atmosphere is marvelous. They have options for NYC residents and students. The date night is also really cool and the rooftop is so much fun!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
This museum is truly a gem. It’s a place where you couldn’t possibly see everything even in two full days. The collection is impressive and incredibly diverse.
I would definitely recommend becoming a member so you can visit as often as you like and take the time to explore each area properly. The membership also includes access to The Cloisters, which is absolutely worth it.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: This place ... It's mesmerizing.  I could've spent days there.   The depth of the collections here is beyond comprehension.  Even on a holiday when it's very crowded, it is easy to maneuver and take your time to appreciate what you want to.  Absolutely amazing.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: This place is absolutely insane. Can you imagine a museum so big and so awesome, you get lost inside? We couldn’t find the exit! What a great experience. Everything about this place is awesome. Can’t wait to come back.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: This place is so huge and a treat for anyone who’s interested in art and or history. One can easily spend 3-6 hours going through various floors and exhibits. We could easily get tickets online just before going in after a Central Park visit. Restrooms aren’t on every floor and it can be a maze when trying to exit.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Took a bible tour here thru Oasis tours. It was awesome.  The museum is a place to even just relax and take in the ambiance.  To truly get a full experience you would need two days.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Truly a great museum that will take multiple days to explore.  Recommend to become a member to enter more than once during your stay or if a resident does have some discounts.  So many things to see, something for everyone.  Of course the Cafe and cafeterias are over priced but we just grabbed some waters to take a break.   Don't bother with the food carts outside, there are better options elsewhere just do some research. Overall a great museum in this country.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Truly an iconic building and experience in NYC. If you have a chance to catch the Superfine exhibit, please do. It is definitely a large space, so dedicated time will do this place good! Also, if you are a NY state resident or student, there are discounts -- so take advantage of those. There's a little bit of a line, but security goes fast. The line for tickets is long, so I recommend getting tickets online before hand. Definitely grab a map on your way in and stop by the museum book shop!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Used private tour for intimate 2.5 hour experience. There is so much to see in the MET but would recommend someone to show you the classics and even provide the history. Will definitely go back again to continue taking in all the incredible artwork and they are always changing it too! Looking forward to going back when the modern exhibit opens. Felt cool to also walk the area where the MET Gala is held with the pyramid room. So much to see and such a fascinating history involved.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Very beautiful. There's so much to see, got there at 6:40pm on a Saturday and could only see a floor & a half, will have to visit again to see the rest. It's very easy to get lost here, plus there's no network service in some areas of the museum so my friends & I weren't able to call each other.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Visited 2/3 of the exhibits within a few hours. I would recommend making a day out of it. Theres so much to see, read and observe! Purchasing a ticket online saves time in lines. Went a little after noon on a Sunday and there was practically no line for security. Audio tours are downloadable. Also, get a map. This place is like a giant maze without it!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Visited the MET on my birthday! Got there right at opening on a Monday, so it was very quiet. Spent over 4 hours exploring, and still couldn’t see everything. Such a wonderful experience with incredible art, very inspiring.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Visiting the Met was pure joy! I truly left my heart there. The Greek, Persian, Egyptian, African and Native Americans sections were exceptional and deeply inspiring, making the experience feel immersive and timeless.

Even during the busy end-of-year rush, the coat-check system was well organized and helpful, which I truly appreciated. An unforgettable museum and one I will gladly return to.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Visiting The Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City was an absolutely unforgettable experience! The museum is breathtaking in both size and scope, offering a rich and diverse collection that spans centuries and cultures. Every exhibit is thoughtfully curated, allowing visitors to feel fully immersed in art and history. From the stunning European paintings to the intricate Egyptian artifacts, each gallery offers something new and awe-inspiring. The museum’s architecture is just as impressive as the art itself, with grand halls and beautiful details that make exploring a true pleasure. The staff and volunteers are welcoming and knowledgeable, enhancing the experience even further. Whether you’re an art lover or just looking for a cultural adventure, The Met is a must-visit destination that leaves a lasting impression. I can’t wait to return!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We can not get enough. We keep coming back and are in awe each time. This is not your average art museum. If you are hesitant to go, don't be. It is 100% worth your time, and you will be here longer than you planned because it's that amazing.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We had a good time. Not exactly my favorite type of place but worth a visit.  I asked if they have a military discount and got free tickets.  Hard to beat that.  They have a nice cafe.  The roof top is pretty cool but on a hot day,  it's hot! Great restaurants nearby to pick from. NR was fantastic. If you like spicy food get a pizza at serafina. They have a habenero pizza, if you like it hot.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We had a really nice time at the Superfine exhibition. I suggest everyone go online and purchase their tickets in advance. Exhibit is only until October I believe.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
We had the morning free before flying home and wanted to visit somewhere new to us. You could actually spend the whole day in this magnificent museum.
It's structurally beautiful as well as having displays that are truly impressive.
The Egyptian items were outstanding,  the burial items, jewellery and finds took our breath away. The wall paintings on the burial building were spectacular.  Also the photograph of the wooden replica boats all broken and then repaired and on display were incredible. They all had a black bob hairstyle!
The Greek section was fabulous as was the  Asian.
The African panels on the ceiling, totem poles, tribal costumes and carvings were beautifully painted and definitely a favourite of ours.
The Japanese garden was quiet and a place of calm and very much represented the culture.
We had a quick stop at the cafe, the queue went down fairly quickly and people were constantly moving so tables became available.
This museum turned into a highlight of our trip and I have already told friends to put it on their to do list when visiting New York.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We loved our visit to The Met.  I love the vastness and the beauty of the place itself, and each exhibit is also well done and thoughtfully put together. And of course having original art you can't see anywhere else is pretty amazing as well. Definitely a spot you should have on your list for any visit to NYC.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
We only had 2 1/2 hours and it was not nearly enough time, even 2 1/2 days might not be enough.  The architecture of the building alone is worth the visit as well as the Frank Lloyd Wright and Tiffany rooms.  There was something for every type of art lover.  The Impressionist section is a must see and the Divine Egypt exhibit was a nice surprise.

Warning: Closed on Wednesdays.  Don't make our mistake.

The museum is disabled accessible with some work arounds necessary in places.  Staff was very considerate and accommodating.  Wheelchairs are available.

Subway stop closest to the Met is NOT currently disabled accessible and it is many stops before a stop is accessible.  Use accessible taxi or bus to get there.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We spent close to 4 hours here and it was not nearly enough time. Definitely recommend buying tickets in advance because the line was long. There is a coatcheck, but I recommend to just come with less items because that line was long too. I cannot wait to come back.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We try to go every time we visit NYC. I don’t think we’ll ever see everything it has to offer. I don’t think most people comprehend how massive this museum is. Definitely worth going. I wish I had more time to learn the details about most pieces but you really have to keep it moving if you want to see even a small fraction of displays during your visit.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We walked up to the MET to see the Superfine exhibit and the Michael C Rockefeller Wing.  We also looked at some other collections. The Superfine collection was amazing.  Very unique Exhibition. The collection in the Rockefeller wing was wonderful.  Have never seen anything like it.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: We were only in NYC for the day and definitely didn't have enough time to explore the entire museum. But we throughly enjoyed the exhibits and galleries we were able to get to. The staff were all knowledgeable and helpful as well. I will definitely be back next time I'm in town.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Went during Saturday date night hours, 5-9pm, on Valentine's. It was very busy, but it was our first time here and visiting from upstate, so it was worth it for us. Would absolutely go back to see everything we couldn't fit in!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Went to limited-time Sargent exhibit-truly fantstic!   2 million sq ft of awesome!! Exhibits are super easy to navigate, also,   many friendly staff to assist with directions. Weekends have longer hours. Map provided. Restrooms very clean, and coffee shop with delicious food options. Pricey but very satisfying. Themed exhibits had me totally geeking out!!! So in love with this groups curating. A one of a kind "History Fans" delight.  Had 8 hrs in there, and still only visited half. Must revisit.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Went to see the Superfine exhibit sponsored by Louis Vuitton.  I couldn't miss this great showcase of Black style though the years. It was a beautiful and very informative experience.  Dandyism at the Met was something extremely important that I wish everyone could have seen.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Wha a lovely experience! This place is huge so bring your walking shoes and some headphones if you get any kind of overstimulated. You can see so many things from Greek sculptures, Van Gogh’s paintings, to ancient Persian arts and ruins. There’s even a secret little garden, can you find it? I spent a good chunk of my day here and didn’t even get to visit all the exhibits before I was tuckered out. Definitely able to channel your inner Blair Waldorf.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
What a gorgeous experience.

The Met Museum — Brief Review

The Met is breathtaking in scale and richness, but it can get extremely crowded, even in winter. Popular galleries draw dense crowds, and it’s easy to feel overwhelmed if you’re not prepared. Go in with a plan, arrive early if possible, and focus on a few sections rather than trying to see everything. Despite the crowds, the museum’s grandeur and world-class collections make it absolutely worth the visit—just allow time, patience, and comfortable shoes.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: Where else can you walk from a complete Ancient Egyptian temple (The Temple of Dendur) to a French Impressionist salon in a single afternoon? Whether you are interested in medieval armor, Japanese calligraphy, or contemporary fashion, the Met treats every subject with scholarly excellence. It is just amazing!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
While very crowded it was still a breathtaking experience to explore the numerous halls. We came to catch the Superfine Exhibit before it closes. As a costumer in film and t.v. it was fantastic to see this exploration of Black Dandyism.
Go see and enjoy. Buy your ticket online to save time and ask questions to get around the building nimbly to avoid some crowded spaces in the museum. A perfect NYC cultural experience.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Why would anyone need to read reviews to decide whether to go to the best museum in the States?  You MUST go if you are in NYC,
Wear some comfortable clothing and shoes because you’ll be walking A LOT.
Nice cafes inside so you can grab some food, snacks and drinks to keep you going…
A NYC gem!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
Wonderful, huge, so nice collections. A must see.

The museum was huge, and while taking a break, I took the chance to keep reading my thriller Honesty Bar (found it on Amazon — available in both English and French). It’s a gripping story that’s great for the whole family, taking me on a journey from Paris to Warsaw to the Seychelles. Absolutely loving it!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: WONDERFUL!!  So worth it!   The displays are amazing, so well done!  We were DOG tired, however we couldn't stop (we had been to the MET Cloisters earlier)  and because it was so fascinating we closed Metropolitan Museum of Art.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
World class museum with something for everyone. It’s a gem not to be missed by lovers of history, art, craftsmanship, pottery, fashion, and the curious. Artifacts from around the world are on display in one massive collection.
I’ve added just a few photos from a recent visit to the newly reopened Rockefeller Wing collection of Oceania, Africa, and ancient Americas, and the special exhibition of George Morrison’s paintings.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: WOW! This museum is amazing. My friend and I didn't even make a dent in a tiny section of the French painters. Amazing collections. So much to see. Can't wait to go back.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: You can be here for hours and not even scratch the surface of what the museum has to offer. So many iconic pieces of artwork to be discovered! We came here on a weeknight  and it was quite busy at the entrance but did not feel overly crowded throughout the museum. We were told that the free coat check would take a long time but the lines moved fairly quickly and it was worth not having to carry our outerwear. Buying advance tickets was helpful

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text:
You can buy tickets online and theyll scan your digital ticket on your phone. You get to skip the ticket line and less of a chance to lose your physical pocket. Id also recommend only using bathrooms within the exhibit halls, i used 2 different ones with no lines vs the restrooms closest to entrance or cafe spots had long lines.
I could probably keep this to myself but I honestly started tearing up at the Met while walking through the Egypt exhibition hall (like tears and needing to blow my nose). To be in the presence of all this art and history, it really puts things into perspective. Highly recommend especially if youre visiting NYC like I was. Absolutely breathtaking.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: You could easily spend half a day here. It’s family friendly and there are some pretty well known pieces here, as well as international art and from various centuries for all types of art enthusiasts. Stroller parking is available.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: You could spend days here! It does get busy but never feels crowded. Join a guided tour and plan to get lunch! Pick your favorites and plan to come back!

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
text: You really have to see it for yourself, pictures barely give you a taste of what all there is to see here.  You could easily spend several days and still not see it all.  Definitely worth making some time for it when you are in New York.

title: Tate Modern
text: A very disappointing experience. The visit to Tate Modern felt like a waste of time, with empty rooms and random objects that did not feel like real art. The museum was boring and offered little cultural or artistic value. For a better experience, the British Museum or the V&A are much better choices.

title: Tate Modern
text:
I am a fully paid‑up Tate member and have been for several years, but my recent visit was deeply disappointing. The Tate is simply not suitable for young children: facilities are inadequate, and the sketch pads provided are nowhere near enough to meet demand. The café offers an astonishingly poor selection for younger visitors — essentially limited to sugary cakes, Pom‑Bears, and sugary drinks, with tap water as the only reasonable option.

To make matters worse, a male member of staff was openly rude when I asked for caramel syrup in my latte.

Overall, this was far from the experience I hoped to share with my five‑year‑old grandson.

title: Tate Modern
text: Like many other State funded institutions it's lost its sense of direction. 10 floors half of which were closed and empty. Of the remaining half two were ticketed sponsored events.  This left a miserley two lack lustre galleries to view.  A national disgrace. Views across the Thames to St Paul's were the highlight.

title: Tate Modern
text: The Theatre Picasso exhibition is a huge disappointment. Hailed as one the best Tate exhibitions in years, it has few paintings, many sketches and the dry up.looks cheap, almost like an afterthought for a museum running out of space. Other museums have done excellent Picasso exhibitions, this is not one of them.

title: Tate Modern
text: The worst experience. Overtourism has reached peak absurdity: ghost humans documenting art descriptions they won’t read, other humans farming Instagram content with their young humans as props. The art became background noise to compulsive documentation. Disturbing. Regulate the crowd before the culture fully dissolves

title: Tate Modern
text: This is my second visit and, while art is clearly subjective, I am baffled by the exhibits. A dog bed, a shelf full of knick knacks, a wall of orange carpet, and oh yes, the urinal is still hanging around.

title: Tate Modern
text: Great venue in a great location… but much of the content is not for me. I like art that clearly takes talent that most people will never have. The stuff in here (to me) seems like most people could do it, but they just aren’t in the position to be paid thousands for putting two suitcases on some newspaper or shoving some sticks in a pair of kids jeans. Give me the national gallery every time.

title: Tate Modern
text: I really like going to art galleries, especially  when I’m traveling but I didn’t really like this one. I found the lay out confusing with little rhyme or reason. Little of the art made me feel want to stop and stare at it to better get a look at it. Instead I mostly walked past the pieces fully stopping for a few that caught by attention. I like some modern art but this was not my style.

title: Tate Modern
text: I understand that modern art is often designed to have an impact which isn't always positive, but I was quite unnerved to see videos of people having sex with plants on my visit to the Tate Modern... and more disturbed that the accompanying explanation portrayed this work as addressing some important social issue, with interspecies sex being something we should be more open to. Is beastiality the next step that society needs to take? Disappointing that this was next to a wonderful, no-holds barred exhibition of work highlighting minority artists' experiences and the diminishing Sami culture. I guess the Tate forgot what really matters for a moment.

title: Tate Modern
text: Kinda was disappointing. The gallery has very limited access to some art display, but if you go for more, there are fees required.

title: Tate Modern
text: Mainly bizarre pieces , are vegetables on rocks really art?Some of it seems akin to the emperor’s new clothes. But guess each to their own. There are some stunning pieces. On the plus side the views over the Thames of London are spectacular from the coffee shop on the 10th floor

title: Tate Modern
text: Mate wanted to see some "art" so why not for a gathering. Even if i was not hungover, I don't think I can justify to say it was alright but no.. the only thing that was remotely like ah cool was the big spider sculpture. The Corner cafe was probably the best thing to do there which is coffee and pints! I saw some ppl just looking at some piece of artwork, which honestly was diabolical, sorry clearly I'm not a really Art person. There wasn't a single painting on the day that I was like ah wow... end of the day each to their own. If you don't like Art in general don't come. Can't imagine if you had to pay to get in, I would say daylight robbery. Oh also Security is like the UK borders control, just  literally walk pass like 👍 cheers mate, pointless. The building itself is impressive though.

title: Tate Modern
text: The building itself is interesting as it is a former power plant station and it has some interesting features and it is fascinating from an engineering stand point. The exhibits are a bit strange and did not catch my attention. If you want to visit all halls, you will need at least 2 hours. Would not recommend. If you have visited all other free places in London, then you might consider visiting this one.

title: Tate Modern
text: Went to the top floor which is a bar. Was in the lift with a couple and their baby. When the doors opened we started to walk out, and staff forcibly pushed the pram back in, shouting at us saying they were closed.

title: Tate Modern
text: As most galleries in London this has  free admission. The building is a large structure with lots of space but not well laid out, with tiny elevators! Lots of immense, winding staircases (too large to be helpful). Ground floor space is wasted with cut up exhibitions... the whole place seems as if it tries to impress with the space but not the art displays. It looks carvenous and not welcoming or cheerful. The exhibitions are just incongruous. Some good history of modern art movements but otherwise it is just okay. This is a surprise, as its counterpart, the Tate Britain is absolutely stunning. I didn't stay long here.

title: Tate Modern
text: Free entry, but need first to go and find out which exhibition needs ticket, otherwise you will running up and down. The collection was bit too modern for my liking, and the gallery is a bit hard to go between buildings (except level 4). It's different to National Gallery, I tend to spend more time there than at Tate. It's a day tour when you visit London & Tower Bridges, and St Paul Cathedral.

title: Tate Modern
text:
From the outside, the building looked very brown and industrial, and honestly it didn’t excite me enough to want to go in. When I did go inside, it was exactly what I expected — classic modern art, which is just not my thing at all.

That said, the inside is very big and spacious, and I can see why people enjoy walking around there. It didn’t feel cramped, and there were quite a few shops as well, which was probably the most enjoyable part for me.

Overall, it’s impressive in size and layout, but content-wise it confirmed that modern art just isn’t for me. I’m glad I went once, but I wouldn’t rush back.

title: Tate Modern
text: Go if you teach art.  Go if you need to tell others you’ve been.  It was under whelming for me.  Better than the Guggenheim,  but can’t hold a candle to the Chicago Museum of Modern Art.

title: Tate Modern
text: I can't say that modern art is really my thing, but it passed an hour or two. I would have liked the balcony on the 10th floor espresso bar to have been open.

title: Tate Modern
text:
I have visited the Tate every time I've been to London. This visit I didn't really enjoy it at all. It was way too busy and there was little information that helped me connect to the art.

There hasn't been anything interesting in the turbine room, the last few visits (and I remember the impact of the first thing I saw in there still to this day).

The location is great and it's awesome that it is still free. It's an amazing place and I'm glad it exists, but it is overwhelming if you don't have a plan.

title: Tate Modern
text: I'm a big fan of both modern & contemporary art but there was just something missing for me here. Maybe because we did not book any tickets for the limited exhibitions, which seemed more interesting to me! But it was still a good time, the installations were amazing and there’s also some cool interactive things.

title: Tate Modern
text: It was an ok exhibition hall. I didn't like much that was on display (yes I know art is subjective). It seemed to be lacking a lot considering how big the tate modern is. Most exhibits were just meh. The additional cost for the Picasso bit was too much.

title: Tate Modern
text: Sorry - I can't give more stars. The venue is an impressive investment in modern art, well laid out, with a cafe and shops that appeared to have good selections. There were many well known artists' creations and some were, indeed, creative and thought-provoking. However,  I am not a convert to mkdern art, especially in this quantity- some were just too overtly well-intentioned and, I'm sorry, but too many were overly simplistic/pretentious and too many artists focus on sex and genitalia, theirs or others - there is more to life :-) !

title: Tate Modern
text: The museum is average. It is simply highlighting all kind of arts that go against the norm. Entire exhibition rooms are blocked to show case gay relationships, feminists, parental authorities, etc. The art pieces weren't impressive and the sound room is no different to any sound healing sessions. You can go if you have 2 hrs and would like to witness something different

title: Tate Modern
text: the tate modern is definitely an impressive and huge museum, and it‘s great that it‘s free to visit. that said, i didn‘t really enjoy the art itself exept a few exceptions, i just wasn‘t my taste. still, i don‘t regret going, it was interesting to see once!

title: Tate Modern
text: Went to the Nigerian Modernism Exhibition with lunch. Only one lift working- long waits to enter. Restaurant is modern, staff welcoming, attentive and polite. Restaurant rooms needed a cleaner to regularly do checks. Overall a lovely afternoon.

title: Tate Modern
text: A contemporary art gallery  in London adjacent to the millennium bridge ,  the former Bankside Power Station was converted into the art musem.  extensive collection of international modern and contempory collections and is one of the world's most visited museums.

title: Tate Modern
text: Admission is free, allowing you to enjoy a wide range of modern and contemporary artworks without any cost(the fee is required for special exhibition )From the museum, you can enjoy a beautiful view of St. Paul’s Cathedral across the Thames. There’s also a café on the top floor where you can relax with coffee while taking in the scenery. It’s a must-visit spot in London for art lovers and anyone looking to spend a peaceful afternoon surrounded by creativity and great views.

title: Tate Modern
text: After seeing excellent exhibitions - Picasso, Nigerian modernism and indigenous artist Emily Kam Kngwarray - I had booked lunch at Tate Modern restaurant. Beautiful fresh starter  of Smoked chalk stream trout, blood orange, squid ink - which I thought the squid ink would overpowering but it was very delicate. Would order this again. The main I chose was Cornish cod, Jerusalem artichoke, truffled hazelnut pesto. Fish was under seasoned but generous portion and beautifully cooked. Good wine selection and attentive staff. Book early as it gets busy after 130pm.

title: Tate Modern
text: Amazing experience and the modern art collection will make you forget the time we spend inside.

title: Tate Modern
text:
Brutalist architecture with a mixed bag of "modern" artworks. Works well if you like echoing voids.. (I prefer more cosy settings) Well curated & well supervised security wise...& free!
Tate Britain across the river along from the Palace of Westminster offers a great contrast both setting and contents (historic building, Constable masterpieces. 20th C Bronzes et al).
Suggest visit both if time permits? 😉

title: Tate Modern
text: Great building though definitely difficult to navigate. I came with school so I can't really complain about the artwork because modern and contemporary just isn't really my thing, I'm sure if it you're a fan you'll love it. I'd definitely get a map because there was a lot of paid exhibits you obviously can't visit otherwise

title: Tate Modern
text: I don’t tend to love big overwhelming museums, I’ll admit. However, you can’t not appreciate the depth and scale of the Tate’s collections. No matter what is your preferred artistic medium, there’s gonna be something here for you. Just make sure you have enough time to roam around and DO NOT COME during half-terms.

title: Tate Modern
text:
I especially enjoyed the Leigh Bowery exhibit on my first visit to Tate Modern. Seeing his clothes in person was a treat and the paintings by Lucian Freud were amazing to see in person.
I also enjoyed the Gorilla Girls exhibit. Timely and needed.
The book selection in the large gift store is one of the best I've seen at a museum.
What I didn't like was how dank/dark in was inside and how spread out it was. Nowhere did I see a note that I'd need a ticket to get into Leigh Bowery... And you couldn't buy a ticket at the exhibit entrance or the special gift shop. Ok... But then you have to go down several floors, walk to another section and get your ticket... Then go back up.

title: Tate Modern
text: I have been visiting the Tate Modern for many years and it always fascinates me when I visit. I enjoy the wide variety of different types of art and different ways of expressing art. I like that the building is big enough so it never feels too crowded and I especially like that they have a quiet room for people who need space and somewhere quiet to sit. I also think it's amazing how accessible the whole museum is. Unfortunately the lifts were a little faulty today, but when they are functioning it makes it a lot easier to navigate the whole museum. Definitely worth visiting when you're in London! 🖼️

title: Tate Modern
text: I love the turbine Hall and the shops. There's maybe 5 pieces of art apart from the actual building I go there to see. Currently some interesting installations. 4* cos I'm very much a traditionalist.

title: Tate Modern
text: I went to the Tate Modern with my class and we can say that this was... Original... I guess... To be honest, that was actually pretty fun and interesting. The building is cool, and if you like contemporary art: go there !! I'm not a big fan of this kind of art but still, I liked this museum.

title: Tate Modern
text: If you ever feel like silencing the rushing thoughts in your mind,this is the place, 4 different layers of musuem, free entry ,large hollow rooms where art is displayed in all its forms, some movies are also displayed on screens, good for a one time visit. some places require buying tickets. Big beautiful cafe at one side with a perfect view.

title: Tate Modern
text: In just over twenty minutes from Old Street, I found myself standing before an iconic landmark: Tate. Today, I immersed myself in its history and archives, taking time to explore the space dedicated to Giacometti – an artist whose work has redefined the very way we think about art. Now more than ever, his essence and intimacy reveal a unique power in a world that feels increasingly distracted and inhuman!

title: Tate Modern
text: It would be wonderful to include a family and children's area, with the potential for further enhancements. Perhaps incorporating both hard and soft play areas where children can enjoy themselves, along with ample seating for parents. Additionally, the inclusion of expressive art installations that are engaging and interactive for children would be a delightful addition. Furthermore, establishing a proper food area, such as restaurants or food stalls offering diverse cuisines from around the world, would be a welcome improvement.

title: Tate Modern
text:
Large brick building that was converted into a modern museum, the Tate is an enjoyable experience right on the river Thames. There are several floors of free exhibits. As well, there are premium paid sections for those that want more.
It's a good place to visit for families couples, or school field trips too.

title: Tate Modern
text: Large range of art and interesting exhibitions, which are paid entry. Nice cafes and restaurants-not cheap but lovely food. Good range of products, including books, in the shops. If you like Art you should visit.

title: Tate Modern
text: Many floors and many different exhibitions and experiences! There is alot to see so prepare to spend well.over 4 hours or more if you want to see most of the floors!

title: Tate Modern
text: Massive gallery with some interesting exhibits. The building transformation is amazing.

title: Tate Modern
text: Massive museum with all kinds of different artwork. We spent a few hours and probably only got through 30% of the exhibits. Mapping is a little confusing with two buildings and having to cross back and forth.

title: Tate Modern
text: Much smaller than I expected. Nearly half the museum is seemingly empty. despite this, I think there's still a lot of inspiring art to see here. But I didn't spend as much time here compared to other London museums. No tickets needed for entry, just show up and have your bag checked. They have maps for sale for £2 but I don't think one is necessary. There is a lot of interesting stuff in their shop but it's very overpriced for what it is (i.e. £40 for a t-shirt). I know the museum is free but that's still steep.

title: Tate Modern
text:
Overall liked the exhibitions, loved some rooms, disliked others 😂
Although I am not really a fan of modern art, I spent around 4 hours there.
I liked most of the themes they chose and appreciated the descriptions of the artworks and artists as it helps to understand what they express, sometimes in very abstract ways.
Some modern artist celebrities were underrepresented in the Tate Modern in my opinion, but thankfully they can be seen in other museums in London 😜
I was a bit surprised by certain rooms that had a lot of nudity or sexual aspects, especially because there were many young children with their families around …
Concerning the Do Ho Suh temporary exhibition, I’m happy I got a discounted ticket, because the original price was too steep for what I saw, but it was a quick fun expo.

title: Tate Modern
text: Really enjoyed visiting Tate Modern. The museum has an impressive collection of contemporary and modern art, and the exhibitions are well curated. The riverside location and viewing areas offer great views of London. Free entry makes it even more worth visiting.

title: Tate Modern
text: Some very interesting exhibits/ displays. Some sections, like the Picasso or Emily Kam exhibits are payable. Otherwise, most of the displays are free to view. You’ll need at least 2h to see semi-thoroughly.

title: Tate Modern
text: Spacious, architecturally beautiful, and filled with incredible art. It’s free (though special exhibits cost about 10€) you just walk right in, and there’s plenty of places to sit and ponder, relax, and soak it all in. Theres a restaurant on the top floor, lots windows with a view of the water, and guided tours. There’s not a lot I could add that tens of thousands of others haven’t already said.

title: Tate Modern
text: Tate Modern is always a treat. It was short-staffed due to a strike but full of good shows. Emily Kam Kngwarray's retrospective was a revelation, with a good audio guide. Nigerian Modernism was interesting & worth seeing.

title: Tate Modern
text:
Tate Modern: Where Art Meets Architecture (But the Urinal Remains Elusive)

The Tate Modern sprawls across its former power station home with all the subtlety of a massive warehouse – which, to be fair, is essentially what it is. Turbine Hall's industrial grandeur makes you feel appropriately small and insignificant, exactly as modern art institutions intended.

I arrived with dual purpose: to appreciate fine art and to finally witness Duchamp's legendary urinal in person. The art part went swimmingly. The urinal part? Conspicuously absent.

The Theatre Picasso Exhibition was a revelation – compact but exquisitely curated. 'Portrait of a Younger Woman after Cranach the Younger' stopped me in my tracks with its intensity, while 'Seated Nude' held me captive for what felt like hours. There's something profoundly mesmerising about watching Picasso wrestle with form and femininity, his brush strokes capturing both reverence and reinvention.

But the real tragedy of my visit was the absence of 'Fountain' – Duchamp's infamous porcelain provocation that launched a thousand art theory debates. Was it away being cleaned? Had someone actually used it, finally fulfilling its original purpose after a century of ironic display? The mystery remains unsolved, leaving me with a peculiar sense of incompleteness, like visiting the Louvre and finding the Mona Lisa out for lunch.

Three stars – would have been four if I could have contemplated the philosophical implications of a urinal in a gallery setting. Sometimes the most mundane objects make the most profound statements, assuming they're actually present.

Next time I'll ring ahead to confirm the plumbing exhibits are on display.

title: Tate Modern
text:
The Tate Modern in London remains one of the most compelling spaces for experiencing modern and contemporary art, and on this visit the highlights created a vivid journey through color, philosophy, and abstraction.

Highlights for me:

Joan Mitchell
Mitchell’s work stood out for its physicality and energy. Her layered, sweeping brushstrokes translated raw emotion into paint, creating canvases that seemed to breathe with intensity. What was most striking was her ability to balance chaos with control. Spontaneous yet deeply intentional gestures that invited the viewer into an intimate realm of movement and memory. In person, the scale and texture of her work reveal nuances impossible to capture in reproductions, cementing her place as one of Abstract Expressionism’s most powerful voices.

Joseph Beuys
Beuys offered a conceptual counterpoint, pushing beyond aesthetics into ideas about society, healing, and human potential. His installations, often incorporating humble or organic materials like felt and fat, bridged myth and philosophy. The Tate’s presentation emphasized Beuys’ belief in art as social sculpture. Art not only as object, but as transformative process. Moving through his works felt like being invited into a conversation about survival, change, and the role of the artist as shaman and provocateur.

Mark Rothko
Rothko’s Seagram Murals remain one of Tate Modern’s most iconic holdings, and their meditative power has only deepened over time. The dimly lit room created a chapel-like atmosphere where his vast, hovering planes of red, brown, and black absorbed and enveloped the viewer. Unlike Mitchell’s vibrant pulse, Rothko’s canvases whispered, pulling the gaze inward. They were less paintings to be looked at than environments to be experienced—somber, immersive, and profoundly moving.

Gerhard Richter
Richter’s works showcased his squeegee abstractions. Layering chance and control in mesmerizing surfaces that shimmered like landscapes of color. In Richter’s work, history, memory, and uncertainty coexist, leaving viewers suspended between recognition and the unknown.

Overall Impression
The Tate Modern orchestrated these artists not simply as isolated masters but as a dialogue across generations. Mitchell’s visceral mark-making contrasting with Rothko’s solemn glow, Beuys’ conceptual rigor alongside Richter’s restless experimentation. Together, they underscored the museum’s strength: presenting art as both personal and universal, emotional and intellectual, physical and transcendent.

Visiting the Tate Modern on this occasion was not just about seeing great works, but about experiencing how art expands our sense of what it means to be human.

title: Tate Modern
text: Absolutely loved visiting Tate Modern on the South Bank — the riverside location is gorgeous. The exhibits were quite inspiring, especially the big Marilyn Diptych by Andy Warhol and the striking grids of Piet Mondrian. And then there are just must-see pieces: the LOVE (sculpture) by Robert Indiana, the Mondrian painting with red/blue/yellow blocks, and the surreal object artistry of Man Ray. The collection ranges from bold pop-art statements to subtle surrealist objects - that you’re constantly surprised. If you’re in London and love art (or just want something memorable), you really can’t skip it.

title: Tate Modern
text:
An artist friend and I visited the Tate Modern, to see the bold new exhibition of Nigerian art.

A brief visit so far, and exhilarating to see, and learn about the different artistic styles, and discover places with so many stories. There's a well-known saying: "Travel broadens the mind". But boy, this art exhibition will blow your mind.

We plan to return in a few days, and bring two more friends!

I cannot recommend this showcase enough, apart from to say: go then share what you think.

For me, it is an homage to a part of the world, where creativity is rich and varied - art, fashion, music, and literature.

It is a delight to witness this historic event in London, and share the experience with so many people - young and old, Londoners, visitors, and tourists alike - across all diversity.

Previous visitors told me to allow 2 hours. I agree - at least 2, as you'll probably want to take your time to view over 250 works, spread over 4 or so rooms.

I look forward to returning more than a couple of times - before it closes in May 2026.

Should you go? Absolutely. Should you also know that there is more than one Tate art museum so you don't end up at the one you didn't originally want to go to? Also yes.

Even if you end up there by mistake, you will find a lot to enjoy. The museum has a wonderful restaurant with a variety of offerings. It also has a lovely gift shop.

title: Tate Modern
text:
My visit to Tate Modern was an unparalleled journey through the history of modern and contemporary art. Housed in a dramatic former power station, the museum radiated the energy of human imagination and creativity through its massive collection.

I was especially thrilled to encounter the heavyweights of abstraction: standing before Rothko’s somber Seagram Murals and Richter’s layered Cage canvases felt like a physical immersion in colour and texture. The curation bridges generations with striking clarity, from the optical vibration of Bridget Riley to the raw, visceral energy of Arshile Gorky.

The true revelation was the rare opportunity to see Monet’s monumental Water‑Lilies (after 1916). The curators placed Rothko’s Seagram Murals in dialogue with Monet’s late masterpiece, a pairing that underscored Rothko’s admiration for Monet’s late works. Experiencing them side by side highlighted their shared pursuit of large‑scale works that draw viewers into the rich colour of their paintings.

title: Tate Modern

title: Tate Modern
text: Only surpassed by NYC MOMA, London's own interpretation of modern art culture set in a large open space carved out of an abandoned coal fired power station in a beeline from St.Paul's over a dinosaur spine bridge made for cyclists. Certainly the architectural theme shell should be applauded whilst the collections were not overwhelming with few classic masters like Warhol, Dali, Mondrian and Picasso swirling in the rubbish heap of Middle Eastern Asian and African unknowns reflective of the anarchy and chaos of the city. Unimpressive modernism curated by Wokey people whose efforts are voices fast becoming irrelevant and outdated in today's end of Empire turbulence. Hence like the city's mayor, this gallery needs a reset for a fresher experience in visual entertainment.

title: Tate Modern
text: Re Nadia Denton's African Heritage Tour at the Tate Modern: I've just spent a great afternoon in Nadia's company talking, thinking and seeing art. She is a brilliant commentator skillfully weaving art history with social history and art appreciation through a vital Black & Asian lens. From the African continent, to Europe, to the Philippines, to America and to Cuba, we journeyed with the diaspora. Not only is Nadia engaging but she invites her audience to comment too. Exceptional. 5*. 10/10. 🥳

title: Tate Modern

title: Tate Modern
text: Tate Modern is a must-visit in London. The space is striking, with its industrial design and riverside location. The collection spans powerful modern and contemporary works from global artists, and there’s always something thought-provoking. Entry is free for main exhibits, which is a bonus. The atmosphere is lively, and the café offers great views. A perfect spot for art lovers and curious minds alike.

title: Tate Modern
