title: State Hermitage Museum
text:
This place needs no introduction, so I'm just gonna give some practical tips for the best possible visit.
The second floor is where you find the most famous stuff, from palace intereriors to paintings. For this reason, it's absolutely crowded with huge tours for most of the day. If you wanna see it with some peace, I recommend going to the museum at Wednesdays and Fridays after 6pm. On the days the museum closes at 9pm and there are way fewer tours.
Do not ignore the other parts of the palace. They have a great collection on ancient Greece and Rome, ancient Siberia, Oriental weapons the tsars got as diplomatic gifts, central Asian arts and crafts. It's amazing.
You won't find here the works of the impressionists (that's in the general staff building) or the Amber Room (that's in Tsarkoye Selo).
Do take your own food. The options are few and expensive. I took a sandwich, a fruit and a water bottle, my bag was scanned, so I wasn't sneaking it in, and there was no problem. Lots of people in the cafe had their own sandwiches.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: This was an incredible place to visit. During you visit you'll go through different palace rooms where there's art from different countries and regions. The many rooms are also decorated with different themes. There're three ways, that I know of, on how you can get a ticket. Unfortunately just one allows you to skip long queues. The least recommended one, is the ticket office, the queue is crazy there, and add to that the queue to get into the palace. The second option is to get a ticket on one of the machines that looks like an ATM, there're a few in the palace surroundings, on them you can skip one of the queues, and the price is the same, about 700 rubles (~10 euros). Also arrive early. The third option is to book online, the disadvantage is the price, you will pay at least 7 extra euros doing so, but you'll skip the long lines, there's a different entry to the palace too. Once you have the ticket, you can show it with your mobile, no printing needed. One final recommendation, if you go the first Thursday of a month, the entrance is free, but the queues are even longer. I arrived 1h 30m before opening time and the queue was already crossing the whole Palace Square. Keep that in mind, and also that you might spend the whole day there, it took me 3hs to look at just one floor.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: This will be a long tour, but it's totally worth the visit

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: Though I already wrote a review for the Winter Palace I definitely need to say something for the museum as well. It was truly a magical experience. I recommend you take the time and plan to spend the whole day in the museum. You definitely need to see all the main attracions that are on display but the main magic happens when you strole thru the museum and let a random artwork reel you in. Truly magical.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text:
To be honest, It is the biggest and the most elegant museum. In my opinion it is superior than Louvre in Paris, British Museum in London. You need to spend minimum 4-5 hours just to have a nice museum tour of the most remarkable places of the art pieces.
There is an option while entering to buy an excursion with a museum staff. She was an awesome lady, with crystal clear English, strongly recommended to use this option.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: Visited this museum with friends, and it was an incredible experience! We booked our tickets online, which made entry smooth and hassle-free. The museum is a must-visit when in St. Petersburg, with its stunning architecture and vast collection of art and history. Don't miss it!

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: Visiting there during World Cup. It was very busy and ticket selling system didn’t work very well but generally it’s an awesome place to see with masterpieces from all around the world. Don’t miss it

title: State Hermitage Museum
text:
Walking in the footsteps of Catherine and the Romanovs, in a Palace of exquisite beauty.
I'm willing to say that it tops Versailles.
I'm very impressed with St Petersburg.
The only thing I hate are the two boys on Nevsky who keep trying to rob me ... and others.
Surely the police can do something!
They are regulars and its spoiling a perfect city.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: We had an unprecedented experience there; stood in awe of the art works and the unique masterpieces such as Da vinci's. Dont miss the ancient Egypt, Iran and Rome halls.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: We have had the good fortune to travel a lot. But i have never seen anything like this place. Take 3 amazing palaces combine it with 6 world famous museums and add the artwork from the Vatican, put it in a building half a mile square, and this is what you would have. Absolutely incredible. Art work was all famous names I remember from school. Sculptures, historical artifact from Greece and Egypt that rival any museum in those countries. People waited in line for hours in the rain to get in.  WOW. Would take days to see it all.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: We spent an entire day exploring the Hermitage Museum. The museum itself is a masterpiece that reflect the rich history and culture of St. Petersburg.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: We took advantage of an early guided tour arranged by our cruise line, which let us in before the general public. How glad we were, as there is no doubt this gets busy busy busy. Only managed to see a small part in 3 hours, the old masters artwork and the scale of some of the works, takes your breath away. A must see in St Petersburg

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: We visited the Hermitage just because it is one of the most known places in the world. It is truly grandiose, though. The exhibition is marvelous and everything is organized. It is very hard to find parking there, and we had to drive around for a long time to finally snatch a spot, so make sure to arrive earlier before your time to go in. Buying tickets online was super easy and the website in general is very website user-friendly.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: We went to the Hermitage with Alla Tours while on a 24-hour stopover on our Norwegian Getaway Baltic cruise in August 2019. It was only a quick two-hour tour but we saw many of the highlights - the grand entrance stairway, the Peacock clock, hall leading to the theater, sculptures including one by Michelangelo, and many paintings by Rembrandt and others. As the second largest museum in the world, this is definitely a must-do while in St. Petersburg!

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: Well maintained. Houses huge collection of art & antiquities.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: What an amazing place. Truth be told I was over consumed by all the art. Almost too much to take in. After 3 hours I still wasn't done. I've never seen so much Van Gogh in one place. Also a lot of Imperial artifacts live here. Life was good for a king in the 1700s.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: What else can you say about a museum that has art and Antiquities from ancient Egypt to 21st Century Furniture of History.   You can spend days there and still not see everything. The price of a ticket is 700 rubles per day.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: When people say there’s no place in the world like the Hermitage, it’s not an exaggeration. This collection and museum alone justifies a trip to St Petersburg. The winter palace is huge and all the palaces could take a full day to explore. It’s worth setting aside a decent chunk of time to visit and there will likely be queues to get in (the electronic ticket machines were not working when we visited) and you will have to check your coat upon entry. There are countless rooms to explore and it’s very easy to get lost but wherever you find yourself in this museum, you will be amazed. From absolute Da Vinci masterpieces to ceiling frescos and Egyptian sculptures, this collection has everything you would need.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: Wow this is the most impressive museum I’ve ever seen. Bought the skip the line passes in advance, which was fantastic. Walked straight in. Spent 4 hours here and still didn’t manage to see everything. There are so many groups going through, but still some spots to find a quiet moment. Plan to spend a few hours here minimum!

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: You can definitely feel that it is 2. largest museum in Europe. If you want to see all rooms properly you will have to reserve 3-4 full days. You have to definitely visit this place.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: You have to take a few days to see everything on the Museum, it is huge! It is not by chance that the people took power in the past, the palace is huge and very incredible, with a wealth of details. I strongly recommend the visitation, just includ in your tourists plans and go!

title: State Hermitage Museum
text:
You need one daylong to visit the whole museum of Hermitage. The iconic Hermitage is the symbol of Saint Petersburg which is allocated in Palace square and alongside Moyka river.
The ticket price is 700 Rubles (10$) included Winter palace which is fair enough.
I recommend everyone to visit this spectacular museum.

title: State Hermitage Museum
text: You need to give yourself as much time as possible here. Amazing art, and rich in history.  The Hermitage  (like the Vatican and Versailles) takes you back in time and gives you a glimpse into what life was like inside and outside of those walls during previous generations.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Amazing, free museum housed in an absolutely stunning building. Visited on a Friday evening and realised that the fact that they advertise that they close at 10pm on Fridays which isn’t totally correct. From 9:25 pm onwards the staff started closing off the galleries. We were constantly reminded by staff members that “they are closing” even before closing time and told this same line at least 10 times (“we are closing”) and even when we were walking towards the exit!!! The publicly advertised closing time should be 9:30pm on a Friday which is when they close off most galleries and so as not to deceive customers!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
The museum is absolutely huge. You think you are done at a floor and yet somehow it carries on. The museum has some amazing items and some not so interesting to me personally. I feel like that’s most museums especially large ones such as this one.

The Roman area is interesting but also somewhat what you’re expected or seen before but still nice. It also has China, Japan, Buddhism and Islam. I feel these areas were mixed, some times more interesting than others.

The jewellery and gems area was really cool especially the crowns.

The paintings area was great, most of the paintings were generally good with some I really enjoyed.

One area with black metal gates, I think floor 3 didn’t make a whole lot of sense it me. Looked a bit like a garden centre and wasn’t my favourite area.

The 4th floor has so many ceramics and was really interesting and beautiful

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Visited the Cartier exhibition at the museum.  A display of fabulous jewellery which is beyond the dreams and bank balances of 99% of the population. Content heavy on tiaras and lights on rings and nothing which could be described as everyday jewellery- which is the point of the exhibition.
Room layout was contra directional with no indication as to which way you should go so visitors were trying to view pieces from the left, right and centre.
If you are a fan, it is worth visiting.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: A great museum which is free. From the tube at South Kensington station one can go directly into the museum through the tunnel entrance. It has the Tiger toy and sword of Tipu Sultan apart from great halls for ceramic, plastic and sculpture of the world.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Always worth a visit. Lots to see and do, in a relatively compact space; one doesn’t end up feeling as if one has walked miles and miles inside. Well worth spending a few hours looking around. Can be busy, particularly during holidays. Somewhat less children than in the Science Museum and the Natural History Museum. Free entry of course.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Amazing collection of artifacts. With explanatios and historical references. Very interesting and informative!

It is politically BIASED and you should be aware. It does have in a major place a stand for customs of 19th-20th century Palestine and Naqba reference, but somehow fails to mention Jewish presence, influence and culture in the Middle East for thousands of years.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
An absolutely stunning museum,  the exhibitions were all fantastic!
Could spend days looking around.
Would be 5 star if it hadn't have been such ridiculously expensive prices in the cafes/ restaurant,  £19 for one of the butties 🙈, but everything else was great!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: An incredibly large and diverse museum with art, furnishings and items from around the world that originate in BCE, CE and until today. . It requires many visits to explore all it has to offer. The building itself is a masterpiece and worth seeing.  Free entry.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Anyone into art, design and history will enjoy it.

Here are my highlights:
Free entry
Was a bit hot inside
Incredible collections from fashion to sculpture.
Beautiful architecture and easy to navigate.
Enjoyed a break at the V&A café and explored the museum shop.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Arriving at a non main entrance is slightly overwhelming, or actually underwhelming as there's not much in the way of orientation. But, once into the museum there's a glorious range of exhibits, galleries and things to see. Family activities were interesting and accessible. Cafe feels totally crazy but just slow down, find a table and take your time.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
As a member, I recently visited the V&A to see the Marie Antoinette exhibition. The exhibition was, as expected, beautifully curated. The narrative was thoughtful, the historical context clear, and the overall presentation intellectually engaging.

That said, the information boards significantly detracted from the visitor experience. Many were positioned well below eye level and used fonts that were unnecessarily small. This made the text difficult to read without standing very close, which in turn blocked access for other visitors. For an exhibition of this calibre, this felt like an avoidable accessibility issue.

Larger fonts and better-lit, eye-level information panels would make a meaningful improvement and ensure the scholarship on display is as accessible as it deserves to be.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Definitely a great museum, but also a little difficult to navigate for some reason. Not sure why as it’s been there a long time so the thought has been tested, and perhaps this was just our experience. Maybe it’s because the exhibits are not only for each age but also for type, and that can be a little confusing.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Easy to get in and out of, plenty to see.  You need to be a member to eat at the restaurant upstairs for that reason only 4 stars.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Free to visit and so many galleries.  There is a nice courtyard when the air gets too airless.  There were enormous statues including a 6 story high trajan column. The signage could do with more clarity.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Give yourself time. There is so much to see from sculptures to furnishings to great art over the centuries.
I must say the descriptions were a bit lacking.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Honestly, it was raining so we popped in for the cafe. What a beautiful set of rooms they were. Will return, to look around museum, very soon.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: I thoroughly enjoyed visiting the Victoria and Albert Museum... there was so much to see, especially the women's sculptures. Unfortunately, I only spent about 30 minutes there as I didn't have enough time. I'll definitely come back!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: I went specifically to see the Marie Antoinette exhibition which was excellent. I think it would have been enhanced by showing us the amazing huge hoops under the dresses and told us about the weight of them and of the boned and metal corset and perhaps also the weight of some of the amazing neckpieces and earrings and the hair adornments!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
In some ways it is like a ' mini British Museum' but with more British content. The BM seems to have exhibits from all over the world but not much related to Britain.
Much smaller crowd compares to BM, which makes it far easier to move around.
Some exhibits are replicas. The special exhibitions can be very popular. It is a good idea to pre-book. I turned up thinking I would be able to buy the tickets at the time of visit, but it was fully booked.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Loved Marie Antoinette exhibition. Generally good access, though with one lift not working and another programmed not to stop on the 2nd floor (but no signs in the lift to explain this), finding the theatre exhibition was a challenge even for staff.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Nice and free museum. The staff is friendly and very helpful, which I appreciate. The museum is huge and quite complicated to get around, however everything is marked quite clearly. There are fun little activities for children, which is nice.
Overall I found it slightly underwhelming.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: The recent partial renovation of the Victoria & Albert Museum is stunning. It brings lightness and openness to both the building and the collection. I’m personally a fan of the sculpture galleries. Tip: if you want to see a special exhibition, make sure to book in advance, they sell out quickly. Alternatively, become a member; you’ll get free entry to all special exhibits and won’t need to reserve ahead of time.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
The Victoria and Albert Museum was a solid experience overall. General admission is free, which is a big plus, though some special exhibitions do require a ticket. We spent time in the photography exhibition, which was particularly enlightening and thoughtfully curated.

We visited during the holidays, just after Christmas, while on a long layover, and the museum was extremely busy. That definitely affected the pace of the visit and made some areas feel crowded.

Overall, it’s worth a visit, especially for the free admission and the rotating exhibitions, but be prepared for large crowds if you’re there during peak holiday travel times.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: They have a design and disability exhibition that shows how disabled people can be marginalized but then have a members area with a section that is inaccessible to wheelchair users. Otherwise it's a fantastic museum but that's why I didn't give it five stars.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: We loved the V&A, and were impressed by the sheer volume of intriguing and interesting exhibits on display. It was an incredibly hot day in London however, and the museum was just too hot and busy, and I would have expected water fountains/bottle filling facilities to be available because of this. I would love to re-visit in the winter!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Wonderful museum we went to the Cartier exhibition which was incredible. Followed up by lunch in the magnificent tea room. The food is very good, fresh but very pricey. It's a treat

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
⸻

I recently visited the Victoria and Albert Museum with a friend who’s a member, so we went to see the Marie Antoinette Style and Cartier exhibitions. The Cartier display was stunning but very crowded, while the Marie Antoinette exhibition was my favourite because the larger pieces, like the dresses, were easier to see.

We ate in the Members’ Café afterwards, but the food wasn’t great and service was slow, probably because it was busy. I’m giving five stars for the museum itself and its world class exhibitions, which are always inspiring. Next time, I’d recommend eating at one of the restaurants on Exhibition Road instead.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
🇬🇧★★★★★ 🇬🇧

From the moment we stepped through the grand entrance of the Victoria and Albert Museum, we knew we were in for something truly special. The stunning Victorian architecture and light-filled courtyard set the stage for an unforgettable journey through art, design, and history. Everywhere you turn, there’s something to marvel at from the intricate Renaissance sculptures to the vibrant contemporary fashion installations.

The museum’s collections are astonishingly diverse. We lost ourselves wandering among sumptuous medieval tapestries, gleaming Islamic ceramics, and the world’s finest examples of British design. Each gallery is thoughtfully laid out, with informative labels that strike the perfect balance between scholarly insight and approachable storytelling.

The staff were warm, however some could be a bit picky and annoying but I do appreciate they are just volunteers. They are also knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about the objects they are always ready with a friendly recommendation or fascinating tidbit.

We also loved the temporary exhibitions, which were impeccably curated and offered fresh perspectives on art and culture. Don’t miss the cozy café tucked away in the museum’s heart; it’s the perfect spot for a well earned cuppa ☕️ and a slice of cake 🍰 before diving back into the galleries.

Best of all, the Victoria and Albert Museum is FREE, yes FREE to enter, making it the ideal outing for art lovers and families alike. Whether you’re a seasoned museum goer or simply looking for an inspiring day out in London, this gem of a museum is not to be missed.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
🎨✨ A must-visit: The Victoria and Albert Museum! ✨🎨

I had an amazing time exploring the Victoria and Albert Museum. It’s truly a celebration of art, design, and human creativity from all around the world. From stunning fashion pieces and royal jewels to breathtaking sculptures and historic artifacts, every room feels like a new adventure.

Whether you’re an art lover or just curious to see something beautiful and inspiring, this museum has something for everyone. And the best part? It’s free to enter!

Highly recommended for anyone visiting London — you’ll leave with a new appreciation for art and design. 🇬🇧💎✨

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: A beautifully planned and executed design.  It's organized by years and parts of the world.  It's probably smart to go to both The British Museum and Victoria and Albert Museum,  but I prefer the Victoria and Albert.  There's a superb restaurant too.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: A great day out, give yourself plenty of time to see everything. Great cafe in a beautiful setting, the food is quite expensive but worth it.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
A great museum with pretty incredible temporary exhibits.

Definitely recommend getting here early and with a membership to see some of the more crowded temporary exhibits. We saw the Cartier one at 10am without a line (though toward the end of it's run) while they had a 30 minute line by the time we wrapped up. The quality of the exhibits is worth it though, a ton of items you won't see anywhere else.

The permanent collection is also quite good if you don't want to take a gamble on special exhibit entry or buy a membership. It's a surprisingly large museum with many areas of interest.

Even the cafe is very good. The member lounge had excellent food though it had quite a wait (probably because everyone is buying memberships for the Cartier and Marie Anthoinite exhibits at time of visit) and the open cafeteria has very nice decor and options.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
A must see and do. Free entry, just walk inside. No ticket required for the main museum. Only special exhibits require a ticket. We did not do those & had a full days visit last Saturday.

Museum is curated beautifully. The artifacts are either beautiful, incredible, monumental or, all 3 combined.

Convenient, Coat & Umbrella check on the right, off lobby.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: A place where art, history, and elegance meet. One of the highlights was seeing the Queen’s jewels — dazzling, delicate, and full of stories. It’s impossible not to feel a sense of awe standing before pieces that once belonged to royalty. A must-visit for anyone who loves beauty with meaning.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
A stunning, and magnificent museum.
Free for public.
We can donate or become a member too.

Sculptures, paintings, ceramics, ironworks, photography, artifacts, architecture, decorative arts, stained glass, tapestries, jewellery, metalware, silver, fashion, furnitures, library, monuments, theatre & performance, and many galleries, and exhibitions.

There's a garden with fountains in the middle that children can play, and a really nice restaurant.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
A Truly Remarkable Day!

I simply can't recommend the membership strongly enough!
My visit to the Marie Antoinette exhibition today was truly remarkable. Not only was the collection breathtaking, but the staff were incredibly kind and attentive throughout the day.
My daughter and I wore period-style Marie Antoinette costumes, and it was lovely to feel welcomed and celebrated. Every single person we encountered was charming and pleasant. What a fantastic museum experience!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: A wonderful museum. Amazing exhibits. Just do it. Don't miss the gloriously decorated cafe.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: A wonderful museum. I had a great time. There are both full and free exhibitions. I was impressed by the free exhibition. Such gorgeous sculptures, interesting ornaments, and much more. I highly recommend visiting.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Absolute fantastic staff,  couldn't do enough to accommodate clients,  great Marie Antoinette exhibition

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Absolute stunning!!
Well organized museum. With a membership card really make it easier to enter some exhibition. Would love to come again.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Absolutely amazing collection and beautiful structure and building itself. The history here is stunning. It's also far less busy than The British museum and it's also cooler, as in not as blooming HOT. If it's the hottest day of the year and you have to choose between the V&A or The British Museum, the V&A is where it's at.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Absolutely brilliant, I have never been to a museum like it. It's amazing how they got everything in and it's free.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Absolutely monumental.

The scale of this place is hard to describe - huge really means huge.

If you’re interested in something, chances are it’s in there.

I’m mostly into paintings and art, so I started there, and the museum had four or five rooms with some very solid British art pieces.

The Cast Courts completely blew me away. Seeing full-size casts of massive and not so massive other major masterpieces all in one place, side by side, is something else entirely. It’s overwhelming in a good way and definitely an experience worth having.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Absolutely wonderful ranges of collections and beautifully structured building itself. The history here is stunning and amazing!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Amazing changing exhibits. We saw the Marie Antoinette and also Cartier.   Had a very good lunch and shopped their gift shop!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Amazing experience. The Marie Antoinette exposition was awesome and it getting a Coffee after was a privilege.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Amazing museum. Supposedly the largest one in the world. More than 1.25 million objects, sorted by period and region. Free entry. Easy access by tube (South Kensington station - District line or Circle line). Perfectly designed website. Inside the museum there is a cafe with refreshments, plenty of toilets and places to rest.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Amazing place to have a calming look through history. It’s next to two quite busy museums so if you need a break from all the hustle and bustle it’s a great place to take a step away to and step into some beauty

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Amazing place. The building itself is a must see. They also have some extraordinary exponates. The Raphael room od out of this world! Also casts make a huge impression.

You cant easily miss Da Vinci's journal!! And the teathre part has some amazing exponates priceless for nerds!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: An absolute favourite when it comes to my international travel experiences. Its beautiful courtyard and international features make it a must see when visiting London! The fact that it is donation only makes it all the better. The gift shop is well worth a browse as well. I always leave each trip with something beautiful from this shop. What a wonderful experience. You can spend hours here wandering in the galleries and resting in the courtyard.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: An excellent museum, with magnificent Medieval and Renaissance era collections. I particularly loved the  Christian artifacts. Don't overlook this gem!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
An exceptional museum in London. A must. I became a member and well worth the investement.
The Cartier exhibit was absolutely exception, I saw it three times.
The Marie Antoniette exhibit was another I enjoyed. How Marie Antionette continues to influence fashion today.
The section on South East Asia have pieces from the Great Exhibit which are a history through fabric, jewellery with influences from invaders, colonialism and fashion of the time. Even French fashionwas influenced by the famous pashmina Kashmiri shawls still worn today.Dupattas have not changed
October 31st Friday the museum stayed open till 22h00. With a great party atmosphere

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Another amazing experience. Cartier exhibition. Absolutely worth £26 ticket. Hundreds of jaw dropping exhibits.  Soft and unobtrusive music in the background. Adjacent post-exhibition shop well supplied. Top marks again

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Aside from the British Museum and the British Museum, this is my personal favorite. The collection is incredibly rich and diverse, showcasing treasures from different regions and eras. You could easily spend a whole day here if you're willing to dedicate the time. Besides the museums, I highly recommend enjoying a leisurely lunch in the café, surrounded by a Victorian atmosphere.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Aside from the British Museum, this is easily one of my absolute favourites. The range of exhibits is outstanding, with an impressive mix of artefacts spanning continents and centuries. There’s so much to take in that you could comfortably spend an entire day exploring without getting bored.

After wandering through the galleries, I’d definitely suggest stopping for lunch in the cafe it has a charming Victorian feel that adds to the whole experience. A brilliant place to visit if you enjoy history, culture, and beautiful surroundings all in one spot.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Beautiful architecture and displays, I was absolutely captivated by the way the Victoria and Albert Museum presented the histories of various countries. Each exhibit told such a compelling story, and I found myself completely absorbed in learning about different cultures and eras. It was a truly enriching and fascinating experience. The cafe is stunning but food a little pricey if you’re okay with that. Spent a solid 2 hours here and it was truly worthwhile. Outside court yard with the fountain was stunning!!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Beautiful museum with a lot of global historical items. Recommend giving a visit
Unfortunately all exhibitions need to be booked days in advance as they are always sold out a day or two before. But the museum itself is free entry

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Beautiful museum! Highly recommend! If you want to avoid the crowds (which are not at all as big as other museums in London) get there early and be sure to be prepared to spend some hours admiring the beautiful pieces!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: BEAUTIFUL! Always wanted to visit this museum and was so delighted that it truly lived up to my expectations. I was lucky enough to go to the Cartier exhibit and that was an amazing exhibit. I spent way more time in the museum than I anticipated. So many things to see and I could have spent more time there easily. It had a great flow to it as well which of course is always great at any museum with a lot of visitors.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Been to the V&A museum to see the extraordinary exhibition Cartier. What an incredible exhibition in the most modern part of the museum.
The museum itself is a beautiful place to visit especially on a rainy day in London.  You can easily spend hours in that museum. The entrance is free but special exhibition like Cartier you have to buy a ticket.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Best free museum in London. Has something for everyone, from amazing jewellery to ancient sculptures, classic ironworks, furniture and even contemporary glass. The layout is well designed so you can visit the sections that interest you without having to see everything. Easily accessible via subway or bus, and free to enter. Staff were very helpful and knowledgeable. If you only have time for one museum day, skip the Natural History museum, National Gallery and head to the V&A.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Best museum in London and it's free!
Would take all day or longer to see everything as there is so many amazing things to see. Particularly all the jewellery from all over the world in one place and objects from cultures through the ages. Don't miss the Luck of Edenhall, which is an intact 600 year old Syrian vase, which must people seem to miss as it was in a quiet section with noone around. The cafe is also excellent in beautiful surroundings.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Better than the British Museum. Come here if you want to see the most amazing Jewels, the most intricate artifacts and tonnes of silver. The paintings of William Blake are a cherry on top.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Big and nice museum, one can get lost a bit 😆 very impressive, clean, staff welcoming. I bought a piece of jewellery at the boutique,  a gold plated brass ring. It's beautiful 😍

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Brilliant museum if difficult to navigate inside. My second visit this year and loads to see. Highlights were the William Morris tea rooms under a cupola, the cast room and that wing, the jewelry section and glassware.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Brilliant museum with so many interesting and different elements. I loved the Cast Courts featuring amazing old replicas/copies of original pieces done by Napoleon and the like. You may think because they are copies they aren’t “the real deal” but because they are copies done centuries ago, it is all the more amazing to me. Also loved the theatre section which had original costumes and set designs etc from movies, tv and theatre. Also there is stunning art, tapestries, silverware and many more geom all around d the world. And it is free! We went early (just after 10) on a Sunday and it was a great time to go. Not busy at all. The cafe is also very good- a bit expensive but great quality food. And many people were sitting outside in the courtyard where there is a pond, grass and tables etc.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Cultural icon!
Incredible museum that’s very well maintained - amazing free exhibitions as well as paid ones. A great place to spend the day alone, with friends/children. Plenty of staff on hand to assist, enough toilets, large cafe and shop, wheelchair access

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Don’t miss the Marie Antoinette or Cartier exhibits!  Wonderful museum with so much to see. We bought annual membership so we can come back. Don’t miss this!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Easily one of our favourite family spots in London. The galleries are diverse and thoughtfully laid out and the stunning architecture is the first thing that catches your eye as you walk in.

The museum is completely free, but you’ll definitely need more than a day to explore everything. The kids loved how the layout felt like a journey through different parts of the world with rooms dedicated to countries across Asia and Europe.

There’s plenty of space to breathe, clean loos, and a central courtyard with a water fountain that’s perfect for keeping the little ones entertained. Whether you’re an art lover or visiting with family, this museum is a brilliant day out.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Even when the museum is free is always good to book a free ticket to assure you can come in.
I really love this museum , was one of my favorites and has a lot of variety.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Excellent museum with large and impressive collection of arts around the world.  I particularly like the English collections through various periods.  It is easy to miss entrance to different floors as only certain stairways lead to them.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Excellent museum. Free entry. I didn’t have a lot of time so most saw the sculpture gallery, my favorite. Highly recommend it. No wait

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Fantastic free museum. You can easily spend 8 hours in this museum without being enough as they are lots of interesting things to see from all over the world. It is an unique museum and definitely worth visiting. The coffee shop located inside it is itself a piece of art with it's three marvelous rooms where you can take a break for a tea or coffee or a bite to eat. title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: First time visit and had a lovely few hours here. It was really easy to get in, no advanced booking, no queues or hassle (Saturday afternoon) and surprisingly not busy, which made the experience even better. The museum is clean, well laid out, and full of interesting and beautiful exhibits. A calm, enjoyable visit, highly recommend!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Free museum that's great for either a super hot day or rainy day out. This museum is huge so make sure you mentally prep for this as it can get a bit visually stimulating. In the Buddism sector, there are ancient carvings and sculptures that dates back to 100-200 years of human history. There are also 2 large rooms that have amazing sculptures in the European section. The amount of items and the quality of them really shows what a fantastic museum this is. You don't need to book a time to enter.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:

Wheelchair accessible

Look out for the giant statue of David in the Cast Courts, as well as the rather large pillar of Trajan. Also, the collection of sculptures donated by Auguste Rodin.

If you like Jewellery, head up to 2F.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
I was in London for the day primarily to visit the Natural History Museum and decided that I had time to visit the V&A, and I'm so glad that I did. I spent 2 hours exploring just a few galleries and particularly enjoyed the Medieval & Renaissance and the Cast Courts.
As an avid sender of postcards I headed for the shop which had a reasonable selection at an affordable price; I also picked up a few other items from the vast array on offer. I didn't visit the café on this occasion. The toilet facilities were very good.
Admission is free, though some special exhibitions are charged and the prices vary. The Cartier exhibition was sold out that day and looking at the booking site it's very popular indeed. Donations are welcomed. All of the staff that I encountered were friendly and helpful.
Obviously 2 hours is nowhere near long enough to explore the V&A, so I'm planning another visit in the autumn.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
I went for the Marie-Antoinette Exhibition
I thought it was well curated. My disappointment was the last part where after looking in details to fine details, we ended up flooded with inspiration design. It felt like we had more materials of something else than Marie-Antoinette dresses. And more quantity over quality.
Overall the exhibition was inspiring and insightful and the V&A is a fantastic museum. I wished I had more time to hang around.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
I've always wanted to visit here, and finally we went this last weekend.
Spaced out over three floors there was so much to see.
The museum was busy but it didn't feel packed and there was plenty of space to walk around and take in all the beautiful displays.
Unfortunately it was unbearably hot (due to the heatwave) and we didn't have much time. therefore we didn't get to see everything, but what we did see was great.
This place is full of history and interesting items and you could spend all day here.
The garden was lovely but shade was limited, and it was lovely to be allowed to cool my feet off in the water.
We also brought some gorgeous one off earrings in the gift shop.
The staff were all super helpful and friendly and the museum was spotlessly clean.
I would definitely recommend, but make sure you have a whole day.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
If you are even slightly into art, culture, history, then this is the place for you. We spent an entire day looking at all the exhibits ..and still didn't manage to cover everything.

Please wear comfortable shoes, please check if any special exhibits need advance booking- or they may be sold out on the day you get there. Access is super easy by tube as well as the bus.

They do have a cafe serving some food.. which is convenient if one wants to spend the entire day looking at art. Saves one the trouble of having to step out of the museum.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
If you like getting lost in a maze of art, fashion, jewellery, statues and things you’re not entirely sure are art but definitely look important, then the Victoria and Albert Museum is your place. It’s basically the attic of the world, except instead of broken toasters and old holiday souvenirs, it’s full of priceless treasures.

Best part: It’s free. You can spend hours pretending to understand Renaissance sculptures without anyone judging you… at least not out loud.

Overall: A great place to feel both cultured and slightly underdressed at the same time.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
If you’re anywhere near South Kensington, the Victoria & Albert Museum is one of those places you absolutely must pop into. As someone who’s lived in England for years, I can honestly say the V&A never gets old.

What surprised me the most on my recent visit was just how incredible the ceramics collection is. If you’re even slightly into pottery, porcelain, glazes, or anything to do with craft and kiln work, you’ll be blown away. There are shelves and walls filled—and I mean filled—with pieces from all over the world, from tiny delicate bowls to massive vases taller than a child. You could easily spend half a day in the ceramics section alone.

And it’s not just ceramics. The museum has an impressive range of sculptures as well—scrubbs, marble carvings, classical statues, and pieces that make you wonder how on earth someone carved something so smooth out of stone. You walk through a hall and suddenly you’re surrounded by history in 360 degrees.

The jewellery gallery is another highlight. It’s not just “oh that’s pretty”—it’s genuinely fascinating. Crowns, brooches, rings, intricate metalwork… some pieces are hundreds of years old, shining under the lights like they were made yesterday.

One thing I really love about the V&A is how hands-on it feels. They’ve got sections where you can take a closer look at materials, textures, and even a few areas where touching certain samples is allowed. And if you love photography or design, the V&A has a bit of everything—fashion, textiles, prints, historical photos. It’s like a treasure chest that never ends.

Honestly, if you’re truly into art, craftsmanship, or history, one day is nowhere near enough. You think you’ll breeze through it, but the more you look, the more there is. It’s the kind of place where you follow one hallway, and suddenly you’ve discovered an entirely new world.

So yes—highly recommended. If you’re visiting London, the V&A should be right near the top of your list. Free entry, world-class collections, and genuinely inspiring.

Come for an hour and you’ll wish you stayed the whole day.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Impossible to view this museum in just one day! We went here at my mum's request on her birthday trip to London. I'd never heard of it and I thought it was a fashion museum from what she said. I was quite surprised, it's complete with architecture, statues, paintings, and pretty much everything from all corners of the world. It even has a fence section - which we weren't keen on as it made us feel like we were in B&Q. Other than that, we thoroughly enjoyed our visit, and we will definitely be back to explore the bits we didn't see, on our next visit.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
In my opinion, this is the best museum that I have ever been into. I was surprised, because I wanted to skip it, but I was talked into seeing it by my fiancé who told me that we will go in just for 20 minutes. In the end, we stayed for two hours. Simply amazing, you can see everything, from historic items, such as luxury cabinets of royalty and wealthy people, vintage weapons, vintage furniture, finest pottery, and ceramics. I am still amazed by the fact how did they manage to get big sculptures and big columns inside this museum. Can’t think of a better museum.
And the ticket price? There is no price, it’s completely free.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Interested in History? This place to go.

"The Victoria and Albert Museum is fascinating, with its vast collection of antiques and historical treasures. You could easily spend hours exploring! The restaurant and cafe offer great spots to relax, and the souvenir shop has lovely items to take home. The sheer scale of the museum is amazing, and I thoroughly enjoyed my visit."

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: It’s a fantastic free museum!  The special exhibitions require tickets, please book ahead!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:

The shops are full of covetable posters jewellery and books.
The temporary exhibitions are fantastic ( currently Marie Antoinette) and well worth the price of a membership to have access without booking.
There are plenty of cafes and food options and toilets everywhere which is just as well as it’s huge.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

If you are short on time, walk across the passage with the gates and take pics of the 2nd floor so you can get a really good view of most of what is on that floor. The elevators are super slow so if you can, take the stairs!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text: Marie Antoinette exhibition is a dazzling, immersive journey thru the queen’s fashion, power, and cultural influence. Highly recommend visiting.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Marie Antoinette Style
20 September 2025 - 22 March 2026

Collection: 5/5 Excellent. What a fabulous assemblage of Marie Antoinette relics! So many items that she personally owned. It was magical!

Service: 5/5 Excellent. Everyone was polite and professional.

Ambience: 5/5 Excellent. The exhibition really evoked the world of 18th-century France and how the doomed queen influenced her time in art, fashion, and interior design.

Cleanliness: 5/5 Excellent. Everything was sparkling and pristine. A very welcoming space.

Value: 4/5 Very Good. I did hesitate on the £23 ticket price, but the experience was exceptional and the highest quality.

Overall: 4.8/5 Excellent. I am seriously impressed by the quality and size of the exhibition. I travelled from Paris to London specifically to experience this display. I was not disappointed.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

I really look forward to the update fashion display when it opens in 2027.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
My visit to the Victoria & Albert Museum was one of the most memorable experiences of my life. As I entered the galleries connected to Punjab and Sikh history, I felt a deep emotional connection. The artifacts linked to Maharaja Ranjit Singh—his weapons, jewelry, and artistic treasures—were beautifully preserved and displayed with great respect. Seeing them filled me with immense pride for my heritage.

The staff at the museum were incredibly helpful and knowledgeable. Each section was well-explained and thoughtfully organised. For anyone interested in Punjab, Sikh history, or Indian heritage, the V&A Museum is truly a treasure.

I highly recommend this museum to every traveller visiting London. It’s not just a museum—it’s a place where you feel connected to your roots.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
The Victoria and Albert (V&A) Museum in London is a world-renowned institution with a vast and varied collection that spans 5,000 years of art and design. The museum is celebrated for its sheer scale and the incredible diversity of its holdings, which include everything from fashion and textiles to ceramics, furniture, sculpture, and photography. The sheer size of the collection, with over 2.8 million objects, means that visitors could easily spend an entire day—or even a lifetime—exploring its 145 galleries.
A highlight for many visitors is the Cast Courts, a fascinating and surreal space that houses full-size plaster reproductions of some of the world's most famous sculptures, including Michelangelo's David and Trajan's Column. This unique collection was created to allow people to study and appreciate these masterpieces without having to travel. Other popular highlights include the stunning Jewelry Gallery, with 3,500 pieces spanning 3,000 years, and the bizarre and famous "Tippoo's Tiger," a mechanical organ depicting a tiger mauling a European soldier. The museum's fashion collection is also a major draw, featuring rare gowns from the 17th century and contemporary pieces.
Visitors often praise the V&A for its accessibility and family-friendly approach, with many exhibits designed to be engaging for all ages. The museum's architecture itself is a work of art, with a beautiful Victorian design and a serene courtyard garden. While general admission is free, the V&A is also known for its blockbuster temporary exhibitions, which are ticketed and often highly sought after. Overall, the V&A offers a rich and rewarding cultural experience, whether you're a serious art lover or simply looking for an interesting and free indoor activity in London.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
The Victoria and Albert Museum (V&A) in London is an absolutely fascinating place to visit. It’s one of the world’s leading museums of art, design, and performance, and every gallery feels like stepping into a different era or culture. The collection is incredibly diverse — from fashion, jewelry, and furniture to sculptures, paintings, and historical artifacts from all around the world.
The building itself is stunning, with elegant architecture and spacious halls that make exploring a real pleasure. The museum is well-organized, and the staff are welcoming and knowledgeable, always happy to share insights or help you find specific exhibits.

One of the highlights is the fashion section, where you can see everything from royal garments to modern couture, as well as the breathtaking Cast Courts with their full-scale replicas of famous European monuments.
Overall, the V&A offers an inspiring and enriching experience — a perfect mix of art, history, and beauty that captures the imagination and makes you appreciate human creativity through the ages.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
The Victoria and Albert Museum (V&A) is one of London’s most iconic cultural institutions, attracting over 3.5 million visitors each year. Founded in 1852, the museum is steeped in history yet continues to feel vibrant and relevant today.

Guests can enjoy a stylish café on-site, perfect for breaks during a day of exploring the museum’s vast collections. Best of all, general admission is free, making it an accessible destination for everyone, though some special exhibitions may carry a fee.

Located in the heart of South Kensington, London, the V&A is easy to reach and sits alongside other renowned institutions such as the Natural History Museum and the Science Museum.

On a personal note, I’ve had the pleasure of managing multiple events at the V&A, and it remains one of my favourite venues. The combination of stunning architecture, rich history, and world-class service makes it truly special.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:

The building itself is stunning, and every gallery feels thoughtfully arranged. You can easily spend hours exploring everything from fashion and textiles to sculpture, jewellery, photography, and furniture.

One of the highlights is the fashion collection, which shows how style has evolved over centuries. The sculpture galleries are equally captivating, with pieces from ancient times to modern artists. The museum does an excellent job of combining history with contemporary design, making it both educational and inspiring.

The staff are welcoming and knowledgeable, and the museum is well signposted, which helps when navigating such a large space. Entry is free, though some special exhibitions require tickets. The café and gift shop are also worth visiting for a quick break or a unique souvenir.

Overall, the Victoria and Albert Museum is a must-see for anyone interested in art, culture, or history. It’s a place that sparks curiosity and appreciation for creativity in all its forms a true gem in the heart of London.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
The Victoria and Albert Museum is not just a museum; it is the world's leading museum of art, design, and performance. Founded in 1852, its vast collections span 5,000 years of human creativity, from ancient Chinese ceramics to contemporary fashion and digital art. It is a place of inspiration, awe, and sometimes delightful overwhelm. Its size and scope can be daunting. Without a plan, you may suffer "museum fatigue" quickly. The layout is a labyrinth. The maps are essential but can still be confusing. The most famous galleries are often packed. Involves a great deal of walking and standing.
The V&A is a cathedral to creativity. It is a place that celebrates the artistry in everyday objects and the monumental triumphs of human design. A visit is as much about the feeling of wonder inspired by the space and the collections as it is about any single artifact. It is not just a museum to visit; it is an experience to be absorbed, and it rightly stands as one of London's essential cultural landmarks.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
This is easily my favourite museum in the entire world. It is not as well known as some of the other major museums in the world, such as The British Museum. In my opinion, it is vastly superior to the aforementioned museum, or any other museum for that matter.

It is classified as an "arts" museum, which doesn't really give enough of an indication as to what the museum contains within it.

The museum is absolutely massive, with a huge variety of ancient sculptures, some of which are enormous. The atmosphere is also incredible, and really peaceful.

There are plenty of places to stop and rest, as well as cafes and a restaurant.

Entrance to the museum is free, which is amazing, given the amount of artefacts on display. I wish other cities and countries around the world would take note of this and follow suit.

There are regular events throughout the year which require payment (or a membership) but even without paying for any of the events, the museum has more than enough on display to keep you occupied for hours. I always see or discover something new whenever I visit.
Highly recommended!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
This is hands down one of the best museums in London. If you’re looking to see real British artifacts, skip the “British” Museum and come here instead. There is so much authentic history packed into this place, it’s fascinating and absolutely beautiful.
One of the highlights is the famous Cast Courts, which feature replicas of iconic statues from around the world. The Raphael Cartoons room is stunning and full of detail, you’ll want to take your time in there.
There are tons of exhibits, including artifacts from the Middle East, India, China, and more. The Great Bed of Ware was fun to look at, it’s massive! It looks like at least five people could fit in it… depending on their weight, of course (lol).
Overall, this is a really interesting museum where you can easily spend 2 to 4 hours. It’s free to enter, and we didn’t need tickets in advance. When we arrived, there was a lovely lady offering a free two-hour guided tour. I’m not sure if she’s always there, but it was a great bonus.
I highly recommend visiting this museum!

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Victoria and Albert Museum (London): London’s decorative arts crown jewel—its permanent collection (furniture, textiles, sculpture) is a masterclass in design history, and blockbuster temporary exhibitions never disappoint.

• Perks: The ornate cafe (gilded details + great tea) is perfect for mid-visit breaks; the design shop sells unique, museum-exclusive gifts.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
text:
Victoria and Albert Museum (V&A) in Kensington is always a rewarding experience, but the “Marie Antoinette: Style” exhibition elevates it to something truly extraordinary. Set within the museum’s elegant galleries, the exhibition feels less like a standard display and more like a carefully choreographed journey into the life, image, and enduring myth of one of history’s most fascinating queens.

The V&A itself is a masterpiece — grand halls, soft lighting, and a sense of quiet reverence that invites slow exploration. Its refined atmosphere provides the perfect backdrop for an exhibition centered on luxury, fashion, and power. From the moment you enter, the transition from modern London to 18th-century Versailles feels seamless.

“Marie Antoinette: Style” goes far beyond historical biography. It explores how Marie Antoinette used fashion as a form of self-expression, rebellion, and influence — and how her image continues to shape modern aesthetics.
The exhibition showcases:
Exquisite gowns and reconstructions inspired by her wardrobe
Intricate textiles, corsetry, and embroidery that highlight the craftsmanship of the era
Portraits, personal objects, and archival pieces that humanize a figure often reduced to legend
What stands out most is how the exhibition connects 18th-century court fashion with modern designers, pop culture, and contemporary silhouettes. You begin to see Marie Antoinette not just as a historical figure, but as one of the earliest fashion icons — someone whose style choices were both admired and criticized, much like today’s celebrities.

The lighting, layout, and pacing of the exhibition are thoughtfully designed. Soft pastels, dramatic shadows, and carefully framed displays mirror the tension between opulence and fragility in Marie Antoinette’s life. Each room feels intentional, guiding visitors through themes of youth, excess, identity, and eventual downfall — without ever feeling heavy-handed.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum

Wonderful and Proud Experience at the Victoria and Albert Museum
Visiting the Victoria and Albert Museum was a truly good and memorable experience. It was a very proud moment for me to see Maharaja Ranjit Singh’s gold throne displayed in such excellent condition. Seeing such an important part of history preserved so beautifully filled me with pride.
I felt even more proud to see Punjabi culture and Sikh heritage being respected, protected, and kept safe in this museum. It means a lot to see our history represented with such care and dignity on an international level.
The museum itself is well maintained, peaceful, and very informative. The best part is that entry is completely free, with no tickets required. Honestly, after seeing the quality, history, and experience, I wouldn’t even mind if they charged £100—it is that valuable and meaningful.
This museum is a must visit for everyone, especially those interested in history, culture, and heritage. Highly recommended.

title: Museo Nacional del Prado
text:
I am writing to formally express my complaint regarding an unacceptable experience I had with your ticket booking process, which resulted in me being charged twice for the same visit, with no possibility of a refund.
On the day prior to my visit, I came in person to inquire about and book tickets for the following day. At no point was I informed by your staff that tickets cannot be booked in advance and must be purchased only on the same day of the visit. No such restriction was explained to me clearly or at all.
When I returned the next day with the expectation of entering the museum, I was refused entry and was informed for the first time that tickets must be booked only on the same day. This information was communicated only after the issue had already occurred.
In particular, the staff member at the ticket desk (cashier), whose English proficiency was limited, did not inform me at any time that it was not possible to book tickets for the next day. As a result of this lack of clear communication, I was forced to pay twice for six tickets, and I was then told that no refund is possible under these circumstances.
I find this situation completely unacceptable. Visitors should be clearly and explicitly informed of such policies in advance, especially when they lead to significant financial consequences. Being charged twice due to inadequate communication from staff is unfair and unprofessional.
I respectfully request that this matter be reviewed and that appropriate action be taken, including reconsideration of a refund or compensation for the additional amount paid.
I look forward to your response

title: Museo Nacional del Prado
text:
I visited the museum with high expectations given its international reputation, but unfortunately my experience with the staff was very disappointing.
Several staff members were noticeably rude and unhelpful. There was also a clear lack of basic English communication, which makes it difficult for international visitors to ask simple questions or receive assistance.
Most disappointing of all, visitors were asked to leave approximately 15 minutes before the official closing time, without proper explanation. If the museum advertises a specific closing hour, guests should be allowed to stay until that time.
It is a shame that such a world-renowned museum does not match its artistic excellence with a more professional and welcoming visitor experience.
