But, when it comes to experience, I hated the museum. There are external tour groups and also some managed by the employees. It is a nice to have a better understanding. The building is a large one but, when 20 people want to stand in front of the tour guide and try to take a photo, it is irritating for individual visitors. Museum guides are also doing the same in smaller groups. They stand in front of a parchment paper and explain it covering the whole thing to their group.

I love Italy, I visit Italy at least 3 times each year but, tourist groups are killing the mood and it is getting worse each year. It was the same case in Pompeii and Colosseaum.

I am sorry for sharing something negative about a Museum/country that I love and care very much but you have to know.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The collection is really impressive, but the halls are overcrowded. I could hardly earn a space in front of an object to read the caption or review it well.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
The Museum is extremely rich and collections are well presented. An obvious effort is put in improving the visitors experience with the setup, presentation and explanation of artifacts. The experience should not be missed when visiting Torino.
I have to make a notable negative remark about our guided tour, that we bought together with entrance tickets. As a multilingual group we selected the English language tour. I am not putting in question the guide's knowledge as an egyptologist, however her poor knowledge of English voided the tour of any interest. After about 30 minutes we decided it was worthless (borderline to embarrassing) and left the group, continuing the visit on our own.

title: Museo Egizio
text: There's a lot here, it's a great collection but even in December it's uncomfortably hot upstairs. We were also short changed because they failed to tell us there were special hours when we booked tickets.

title: Museo Egizio
text: This museum is incredibly impressive with the amount of artifacts and mummies they have on display, however it is also hard not to recognize how much artifacts and people ARE on display instead of in Egypt where they belong. The displays are labelled really well, the building is clean, and all the staff are super friendly and helpful! However, the layout is a little difficult and there are so many visitors and tours that it's extremely difficult to walk around and see some displays. Overall, it definitely deserves a visit, but maybe during the week would be better to avoid the crazy crowds. This museum can easily take an entire day, and definitely deserves at minimum 2 hours! The gift shop though is very small (in space and in quantity of gifts) but they have many good books and postcards!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Very impressive collection and artifacts. I was expecting a more interactive museum with an easier storytelling.
Arrived at 12:00 and there was no more tickets- had to negociate and find a solution > do buy in advance your tickets.
In 2026 thé central courtyard will be open should be nice with a cafe.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Very impressive of course, but some galleries were insanely crowded, and since everyone has their coats and bags with them, and there are many tours, it made for a somewhat claustrophobic, annoying navigation experience. But undeniably insane collection. The mummy displays did make me sad for the whole exploit of colonisation though.  It’s a bit morbid that these corpses were exhumed from eternal rest in North Africa to be now in vitrines here.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Very interesting museum with a huge variety of exhibits. The only negative that I would like to notice and I hope it will happen somewhen is that as visitors we need to leave jackets and bags, in some cases  backpack/suitcase, so that we can be more comfortable to enjoy the museum. Many museums in the world have a special cloakroom and it is a pity that this very nice museum does not have one.

title: Museo Egizio
text: “t’s the second largest Egyptian museum in the world, with an incredible amount of artifacts to see. The only downside is that there’s almost too much to take in at once , it can feel overwhelming, but definitely worth the visit!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
3.5 stars
Big museum and very informative. The walking path was a bit confusing. I am happy when museums have a lot of guests, the problem was that at a certain time, it was full of guided tours, big groups just blocking the rooms and the way.
Also some descriptions were falling apart so you could not read them properly. Some also were not placed properly, I had to sit on the floor to be able to read.
Also the descriptions on the glas boxes were written in white, without background, if the things in the box were in bright colors you could not read properly.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A great afternoon in Turin (even after a long day walking around the city). Having topped up with Bicerin in the cafe across the square, we entered the museum at 3pm on a Sunday. It was still busy with tours, but as we got further in, the numbers stretched, which meant we were able to stand and admire the ancient Egyptian craftmanship. I especially liked the section showing photos of items being removed from tombs, and then you see it for real! A bit of me wonders if such things should be left alone, but I guess either someone else would have taken artifacts instead, or just taken precious pieces and left the rest to rot... so I guess this is the best answer. Regardless... a must see if you are in Turin - our 2.5hour visit was well worth the effort and cost.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
A must in Torino. Well-shown exhibitions, amazing objects. Clear and instructive explanations, with sound guide or app. Plan to book well in advance and allow 2-4 hours for the discovery. Small snack area inside and guaranteed access for people with reduced mobility.

More than 4,000 years of history are watching you!

title: Museo Egizio
text: Amazing museum with a great exhibition about Egypt with a plethora of items and mummies. Everything was  greatly preserved and had an explanation of what you are seeing and it's history. It's a huge museum, we went without a guide and so we were kind of lost at times, not sure where to go to next. It's a little bit chaotic and can get real crowded real fast. I consider it a must visit. We also visited the ellesiya temple since it was free but I wouldn't say it was worth it because it was a tiny space with a lot of people and you couldn't walk around freely much.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
An unparalleled treasure of Egyptian civilization

The Egyptian Museum of Turin is without doubt one of the most extraordinary repositories of ancient Egyptian culture outside Cairo. The collection is vast and breathtaking in scope: monumental statues that radiate timeless power, intricately decorated sarcophagi, papyri that preserve millennia-old narratives, and everyday objects that reveal the intimacy of ancient life. Each piece speaks with historical and artistic authority, reminding the visitor of the sheer uniqueness of this institution.

The museographic layout can at times feel complex and slightly disorienting, given the immense quantity of material on display. Yet this abundance is precisely what makes the museum so remarkable: it is not a simple exhibition, but a total immersion into a civilization that shaped human history. The staff are consistently attentive and kind, always ready to guide and clarify, turning potential confusion into an opportunity for deeper discovery.

To walk through these galleries is to encounter the heartbeat of ancient Egypt—its grandeur, its spirituality, its craftsmanship—presented in a way that is both rigorous and awe-inspiring. The Egyptian Museum of Turin is not merely a visit; it is an encounter with eternity.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Beautiful, rich in content and instructive. Videos, video mapping and interactive screens are very well made. Path to follow not marked not easy to follow due many level and irregular floor plan. Pity that esthetic of the rooms and backgrounds are not ideal, making sometimes also text reading difficult , with no atmosphere and theatrical setting that it would deserve.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Fantastic museum, with unique pieces exposed in a perfect way. Some interactive areas are amazing. Better to buy the ticket in advance online. There are some lockers for leaving jackets and backpacks. The bar is too small and crowded. Average time for the visit 3 hrs, if you spend a bit of time in every floor. Super recommended

title: Museo Egizio
text: Good amenities, good price, friendly staff, awesome displays. Only negatice to note is that the beginning displays are the least interesting of the artefacts and should you have time constraints, it's worth skipping the first 2 levels or moving quickly through them. We didn't realise how long the experience would take, and we had to rush the most intetesting parts which were only accessed near the end. It would ve better to make your way quickly and then backtrack through the displays, as you can still exit easily wherever you decide to finish.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Great museum, but what they have recently done to the Galleria dei re is horrible. They made it soulless. Visual aesthetic does help to immerse yourself in a distant world, when pure reason is unable, and I don't see the point of deleting that spirit of exploration and wonder that the previous look had.
The same goes with the narrative the museum helps you to build. It's nearly absent, it's just a collection of things, that if you don't know much about you can little engage with.
Some more heart along with the brain doesn't hurt.

title: Museo Egizio
text: I don't think many words are needed to describe this museum, due to the fact that it is the most famous museum in the city and certainly it is one of the most famous museums in the world. To visit it you need a lot of time, at least 3 hours, and it is recommended to visit with a guide, who is able to explain the most interesting parts of the museum. Unfortunately, the most beautiful and interesting part of the museum (the Gallery of the Kings) is currently closed due to some restoration work

title: Museo Egizio
text: I took the guided tour in Italian and the guide was super nice, precise and funny! Joe are a complete narration around the main collection and gave us all suggestions on how to explore the other parts! Definitely a good way to learn and experience the huge amount of elements that the museum contains! Worthy

title: Museo Egizio
text:
It's a great museum, but the layout isn't well thought out, in my opinion.
The first exhibition shows woods, how they were used, then pottery. Incredibly interesting and well documented. The other rooms/floors didn't feel as cohesive. I wondered if the first exhibit was put up by one person, the rest by another because of the large differences in identifying the items.
As I had an injured knee and walking was a challenge, we used lifts. There are so many lifts and none of them seem to go to the same place. The first lift is in the pottery exhibit. It goes down to -1 and returns to 0. The second lift we took was opposite a bank of escalators, and it didn't have the same number of floor options as the third lifts.  The third lift seemed to go to most floors (including the café which the other lifts did not go to). The toilets are beside the third lifts but was not at all well signposted, we found it entirely through luck.
The other, main issue I had was that the most "interesting" items to see - the royal mummies / ancient Egyptian items from pyramids and tombs, were at the very end.
We spent 3½ hours at the museum and barely got any time to look at those "big ticket" items.
I'd happily go back, but in future I'd start the museum from last exhibit to first.
*I had a disabled ticket, which cost less and included a companion, this was very disabled friendly.
QR codes were set up so you could listen to the audiotour from your phone.
We visited the museum on the 30th Dec (a Tuesday) and it was incredibly busy.

title: Museo Egizio
text: No lockers available today, the temperature was so hot, unbearable at times. It's very confusing navigate the museum. People and tour guides touching the statues all the time is shocking. The collection is magnificent, impressive and worth seeing. It's a very interesting museum.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The collection is enormous and really interesting, especially the explanations about hieroglyphics. However, we bought tickets online and had to choose a specific time to visit - no problem, I expected this to mean that the number of visitors was time-managed, but in fact the museum was packed and remained so during our visit. If I returned, I would choose an earlier- the earliest- time possible in the day. The crowds made reading the information on exhibits difficult.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
The collection is massive.
Would like an improvement on the visit path, as right now is a bit confusing. Also, is great that they have included some audiovisual pieces, but some lack of a proper explanation of what they are trying to explain/highlight.

In general, if you love egipcian artifacts this is your museum. If you just want to check it or are midly interested, I'd recommend a speed run or prioritize other places before this one.
A guide is also a very good recommendation to really know what you are seeing.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The museo egizio is so interesting and it slightly alleviated my concerns about Egyptian artifacts being held outside of Egypt, as , at the time, selling artifacts was big business for Egypt, and now, all holders of artifacts are communicating with each other to piece together what they have. I spent 3 hours in this museum, so many beautiful and interesting things to see. And so ancient! There are some mummies, these are real human beings, and they made plans to enter the Netherworld, I always  offer up a little word to the universe that they are exactly where they hoped  to be. I definitely recommend a visit here.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Tour with guides. Better to reservation before visit. After tour, you can see again what you want to see.  Guide tour is not enough to see all!

title: Museo Egizio
text: Unbelievable, huge, full with history. I was amazed how many missions Italians had to Egypt.... a bit more than the brits

title: Museo Egizio
text: A beautiful and wonderful place

title: Museo Egizio
text: A brilliant collection of artifacts from Ancient Egypt. Definitely worth a visit if in Turin.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A giant collection of Egyptian artifacts. It was refreshing to see how they touched on the debate of the ethics of having mummies on display. My favorite section was the one on the carvings and other artwork and how pigment was made.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A magnificent museum, either for Egypt fans or the general public. The former will take several hours over their visit. The collection is comprehensive, at least up to Ptolemaic times, since in former times the museum carried out excavations specially to fill any gaps. It is also the best explained of any Egyptian museum I've been to, possibly of any museum in general. All explanations are in English as well as Italian; a few also in Arabic.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A most wonderful museum! I had come here 20 years ago and I have been impressed with the renovation. I like very much the beautiful, realistic settings that replicate original places, and the 3D movies that help experience the way the pieces were originally.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A museum with a lot of exhibits.  it is worth booking at least 3-4 hours for sightseeing.  All very interesting.  It's a pity that there were no descriptions in English everywhere.  It is worth taking a phone and headphones, because on the website you can listen to recordings explaining some of the exhibits.  It is often crowded, so you have to keep that in mind.  I recommend!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
A must do when in Turin.  We have been to a few of the Egyptian Museums in different cities...the artifacts in the Turin Museum are remarkable.

I never imagined that I would see the actual "Book of the Dead" scrolls.  How about the original Nefertiti bust that was so duplicated in the plaster shops of the 70's?  Saw it!

Take a half day or longer and tour the museum.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A must for everyone visiting Torino. It is a amazing collection covering severl thousend of years of history. It exceeded my original expectation, I understand better now why the museum of Torino is considered the most important after the one in Cairo. I recommend to buy in advance the ticket to be able to skip the line since there are many tourists every day.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A must see if you visit Turin. We spent over two hours wandering around the museum using the free downloadable English audio guide on our phones. Astonishing artefacts.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A must visit while travelling to Torino. It has an extensive collection of Egyptian artefacts.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A really unique and world famous collection, definitely worth visiting. Tickets must be booked in advance, online and an audio guide is available via app or browser on your own mobile phone so it is highly advised to take earphones with you. Internet connectivity is sketchy though so in some areas the audio guide won't work. If you can afford to, book a guided tour, as there is an overwhelming number of artifacts and way too little written about them.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
A really wonderful collection of artifacts from ancient Egypt. You can download an audio guide and listen to it on your phone and with your own headphones. It’s quite a large collection. You start at the top story and work your way down. Expect to spend at least two hours.

One thing I appreciated about the museum was the respectful way they handle human remains. They were displayed separately and labeled so people could avoid if they wished. The separation also helped it feel more like a sacred and respectful place for the people, while still allowing us as living people to learn from the stories they have to tell.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A true journey through history and pure, unadulterated beauty. The museum is well-organized and orderly. Easy to navigate and straightforward. All the pavilions are interesting. A source of pride for Italy and Turin. The only shame is the lack of restrooms.

title: Museo Egizio
text: A very cool experience in the middle of Turin. It is the second largest collection of Egypt antiquities outside of Cairo. We had a guided tour which I would highly recommend. A very cool experience!

title: Museo Egizio
text: A very rich and even luxury museum that costed 18€ for an adult ticket but the price was worth it. The toilets were clean and functional. The game with the lights and shadows created a mysterious ambiance.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
A well organized place on four floors with lots of artifacts from ancient Egypt.  A place full of history, which penetrates you to the depths of your soul.  It is extraordinary to see how much passion those people put into carving statues or sarcophagi.  Also, the objects and texts written on portions of the walls are very interesting. And more impressive are the mummies, one of which is about 6000 years old.  The hall of kings is gorgeous, from the artifacts to the arrangement and lighting of the hall.  Everything is superlative in this museum.
Regarding the visiting schedule, I went on a Tuesday and I didn't have to buy the tickets online.

title: Museo Egizio
text:

A story of love, death, art, fun, passion and life itself. Very tastefully and artfully presented. Great for all ages and you can quickly see everything in about an hour, but you can also spend nearly the whole day there and not quite see everything too ;) Your choice!

title: Museo Egizio
text: Amazing museum,  I spent 4 hours here but could have spent another 4 hours. Such interesting information and exhibitions

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Amazing museum, with a great collection of artifacts. Really well preserved and laid out, with an easy to follow route.

Highly recommended for anyone who visits the city!

title: Museo Egizio
text: Amazing museum! We spent 7 hours there, admiring all of the ancient Egyptian treasures. So much to see and well presented. A very special day!

title: Museo Egizio
text: An impressive museum inside a beautiful building. Lots to see. We went near closing and ended up being rushed, so definitely budget at least a few hours here if you want to see everything.

title: Museo Egizio
text: An impressive visit, with incredible pieces. A must-see, both mesmerizing and heart breaking to think all these pieces have been taken from their home.

title: Museo Egizio
text: An incredibly rich collection, but what impressed me even more was the curation - super interesting and accessible.

title: Museo Egizio
text: An unforgettable experience at an incredible value! A must-visit for history lovers. I spent almost 4 hours exploring its beautifully curated exhibits, and the mummies were the highlight for me—so well-preserved and captivating, absolutely mesmerizing! For just €14, you get access to a treasure trove of ancient artifacts, free audio guides in English and other languages, and even free Wi-Fi. It’s an immersive journey through history that I can’t recommend enough!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
As a fan of history, and Egypt, this place gives you the chills.

A superb blue eyes gentleman, dark hair, tall and slim - invites you inside of the museum - he is just adorable!!

There is a small queue at the tickets office, but it is expected, under 5 minutes. You have a spot for your clothes/bags, so do not worry about that.

Honestly, a superb experience. Turin has done a lot of excavation in the past in Egypt, so they have a tone of artefacts.

Tickets were €18 per person, and I bought them on the spot, on a Saturday. It wasn’t very busy, but still a good amount of people. Was it worth it? Hundred percent!

Has 4 floors (starts at -1) and over 14 rooms for you to explore.

It took me over 1.5 hrs to get through it, but I have not read every single bit of piece that’s in there. If you want to read every bit, get ready for little over 4 hours!!!

There is a coffee shop in there, brilliant choice of sandwiches and drinks; prices were decent too.

You can take some audio devices to explain things around the place, so that’s a good option too.

Overall, 10/10 for Egypt and history lovers.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Awesome museum with so much to see and learn.

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio

Very large and at crowded at times, plan for 2-3 hours minimum to see go through it without rushing too much

title: Museo Egizio
text: Definitely a must see Museum in Torino if you are into Eygptian history. They did an excellent job with display and preservation and particularly the efforts in creating the educational videos. My photos do no justice to the artifacts!

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio
text:

I highly recommend visiting, and if you want to explore all sections in detail, you’ll need at least 4 to 5 hours.
If you’re short on time, make sure not to miss the basement level (Level 0) — it’s the most captivating part!

title: Museo Egizio
text: Excellent museum with so many incredible things to see. Surely one of the best collections of Egyptian artefacts and history outside Egypt. Amat!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Fabulous place and so much to see. Allow a whole day to visit.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Fantastic collection of writing, sculptures, tombs, paintings and everything you would like to experience from the Egyptian culture.

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio

The Turin ancient Egypt museum is a must see if one travels to Turin, Italy.  It's also located in the Center of the city. They've also very good tour guides.

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio
text: I highly recommend to visit this museum

title: Museo Egizio

Some challenges when there are lots of school  children visiting at same time, but it's a large enough place to get ahead of them .I recommend going as early as possible. The staff were extremely pleasant and helpful.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
I was mesmerised by the Egyptian Museum’s vast and vivid displays of ancient Egyptian art and artifacts. The museum, which is the world’s oldest dedicated to Egyptian culture, offered a profound glimpse into the lives of pharaohs and the common people through its extensive collection. The layout of the museum facilitated a seamless journey through different eras of Egyptian history, making each exhibit come alive with stories from the past.

Unfortunately, the museum’s free WiFi was not functioning during my visit. This was particularly inconvenient in the basement, where there’s no cell reception either, making it impossible to use the free audio guide on my phone. Despite this, the richness of the museum’s offerings—from the striking statue of Ramesses II to the intricate tomb relics—ensured that the experience remained unforgettable.

The Egyptian Museum in Turin is a must-visit for anyone looking to delve into the magnificence of ancient Egypt.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
i’d been wanting to visit for the longest time, and finally got the chance.

the student discount was only €2, and it was absolutely worth it. i spent a solid two hours exploring. it was fascinating to see all the egyptian artifacts and learn about the colors, materials, and even the alphabet they used.

totally recommended if you’re in turin!

title: Museo Egizio

I spend around 2 hrs as I ran out of time but I can easily spend half a day to observe the collections and the history. Definitely worth visiting. Please buy ticket online to avoid the long queue

title: Museo Egizio
text: If you in Turin do plan some time to visit, indeed very interesting Egyptian Museum, may spend 2 to 3 hrs for exploring around, inside having a small cafeteria for coffee and sandwiches/bread.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Incredible museum full of relics and history. Highly recommend booking your ticket in advance (free with the Torino+Piedmont card).
Definitely worth coming here as early as possible during the day - especially on a Monday when most other places are shut.
The cafe was great value and really helpful for when I needed a break.

title: Museo Egizio
text: It was an immersive experience. The collection is absolutely stunning and unique. We spent 6 hours inside and could have stayed for much longer.

title: Museo Egizio
text: It was great to come back after 20 years. I love this museum. It is a must to visit when in Turin, not only for the exhibition itself but also because this is the most important museum after the one in Cairo.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
It was worth visiting. Amazing collection of ancient Egyptian culture. More than explaining in words. It takes more than 3 hrs to watch everything.  Went without pre booking on working day.
The new Kings gallery is just amazing.

title: Museo Egizio
text: It's one of the best museums I’ve visited in Torino so far! The Egyptian Museum is truly mesmerizing, and it offers a rich, immersive experience. Make sure to set aside at least three hours, as there’s a lot to see—from beautifully preserved artefacts to fascinating historical displays. The collection is vast, incredibly well curated, and meticulously maintained. It’s a journey through time that’s both educational and captivating. A must-visit for history lovers and curious minds alike!

title: Museo Egizio
text: Located within the "Academia delle Scienze", this museum is quite impressive, if you are interested in Egyptology. I enjoyed the Ancient Egypt collections of the British Museum in London and the Metropolitan in New York, but I must say this Museo Egizio in Turin is quite something else, only in Cairo I feel you can see more impressive exhibition. Definitiely a must visit!

title: Museo Egizio
text: Mind blowing collection. Would take multiple visits to take it all in and fully appreciate all on offer. Go early so you have plenty of time to experience it all. Slots for entry are timed so best to purchase tickets in advance. Excellent student discounts so remember your card if you have one!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Must visit if you are in Turin.
Book your ticket in advance and if you have young kids I'd suggest to book the English guided tour. Our guide was knowledgeable and spoke with passion and proud of the Museo collection. Don't miss going to the Writing section where kids could see what their names look like in the heliography !

title: Museo Egizio
text: My childhood self cried a little as I saw some pieces I'd only seen in books. Incredible display, well curated, amazing place where another culture's history is respectfully well-preserved. Remember to book/buy tickets in advance from their site. 100% recommend. 100000% going back.

title: Museo Egizio
text: My first experience to visit Egyptian museum and to see in real as I have only seen on screen. It's totally new things for me to see and there are many different kind of exhibitions, at least 2 hours to explore the site. One of the things to see if you're in Turino.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
My lifetime experience upon visiting this Museum. I was stunned looking at the mummies that we all have been studying in the school. Having seen so much from the history, it was worth going to this place.
Must visit place if you love civilization, history and Egypt....

title: Museo Egizio
text: My second visit to this museum and I loved it. I would recommend the guided tour, it gets a little hectic in the museum by which I mean very crowded. The docent was excellent. I highly recommend this museum.

title: Museo Egizio

text:

title: Museo Egizio

I would allow 5 hours to see it properly.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Really amazing Egypt Museum! So many artifacts and displays! Lots of interactive opportunities to learn and grow your knowledge! Huge building filled to the brim! Absolutely amazing!

Second largest Egyptian museum in the world.
Very well organized exhibits, 4 levels of different aspects of history fron over 4.000 years of civilizations.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Super museum, so much to see. Had a wonderful tour guide, so informative . Could spend hours here. Coffee shop is very small however. Highly recommend buying tickets in advance as we were unable to enter the last time we visited as it was sold out.

title: Museo Egizio
text: That stunning place is a must whenever you visit Italy, more than British national museum and other famous museum, this is the biggest reliques egiptians exposition in the World, only Cairo's museum can be positioned first.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The collection is bountiful. The information is clear in both English and Italian. The WiFi connection is good.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
The Egyptian Museum contains many treasures of the Egyptian Empire from several periods of the pharaohs throughout history, such as mummies, sarcophagi, household items and statuettes to which the ancient Egyptians prayed.
It is very important to book tickets and an entrance time in advance online! Otherwise it is very difficult to enter.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Egyptian Museum in Torino is a must-visit for history enthusiasts! The extensive collection is truly impressive, with a wide range of artifacts that offer a glimpse into ancient Egyptian culture. You could easily spend hours here exploring the exhibits, making it an unforgettable experience. I would recommend to make a prior reservation.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Egyptian Museum in Turin is a must-visit for anyone passionate about ancient history. As the world’s second largest collection of egyptian artifacts after Cairo, it offers a stunning journey through Egypt’s civilization. The museum’s vast and beautifully preserved exhibits bring ancient culture vividly to life. Its expert curation and detailed explanations make the complex history accessible and engaging. You can't leave Turin without vising this museum!

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Egyptian Museum in Turin is absolutely incredible. The collection is vast and beautifully curated — from delicate papyri to massive statues and perfectly preserved mummies. You can easily spend hours here and still discover new details in every room. The explanations are clear and engaging, and the layout makes it easy to follow the story of ancient Egypt from start to finish. The atmosphere is calm and respectful, allowing you to really appreciate the artifacts. A must-see for history lovers and one of the best museums I’ve visited in Italy!

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Egyptian Museum of Turin is a must-see destination for anyone who loves history, archaeology, and culture. It transports visitors into the heart of ancient Egypt through extraordinary collections and a carefully curated exhibition. Personally, it remains one of the most appreciated and in-depth cultural experiences in Italy.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Egyptian Museum of Turin, opened in 1824, is the oldest museum in the world dedicated to the Egyptian civilization, second only to the one in Cairo for the number of artifacts it owns. A must-see if you are staying in Turin even just for the weekend. Students are entitled to a special rate of 3 euros. There is also an audio tour (bring headphones) that is free and very useful if you do not know much about the history of this civilization. Unfortunately, of the 3 floors on which it is structured, the one on floor -1 was under renovation and could not be visited.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The exposition was as expected extremely interesting and in some way eye opening to the beauty of the past rituals and the craftiness of the Egyptians. A deep dive into some aspects of their and our world from thousands of years ago, just to look at the artifacts and think that they were made by other people so long ago is just incredible. No picture does justice to the beauty of their work.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The general impression of Turin was that it was a beautiful city but a the same time a kind of dull. This sparked King Charles Emmanuel III of Savoy to make the city more interesting by sending botanist Vitaliano Donati and bringing back archaeological pieces from the mystical Egyptian burial sights. King Charles Felix extended the museum in 1824 by buying the Drovetti collection existing over 5000 pieces ranging from papyri, textiles, statues and sarcophagus with mummies. The collection of papyri was of such an importance that Jean-François Champollion came  to test his knowledge of his newly deciphered hieroglyphics. The collection starts out with the rural primitive burials and follows the evolution of the thought of live after dead by mummification and impressive sarcofagi.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
The Museo Egizio in Turin is a spectacular destination for anyone fascinated by ancient Egypt. Its collections are both vast and profound, offering glimpses into rituals, daily life, and royal legacy. The thoughtful redesigns, particularly the Gallery of the Kings, elevate the museum into a living journey rather than a static display.

That said, for a truly enjoyable experience, consider visiting on a quieter weekday or booking timed tickets in advance to avoid peak crowds.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Museo Egizio is a truly exceptional institution; allocate several hours to fully appreciate its remarkable collection.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
The Museo Egizio is the world's oldest museum devoted entirely to ancient Egyptian culture. Discover the fundamental stages of its formation, from the creation of the collection to the present.🫡🙏🏻👏👏👏

Currently, the Museum preserves a collection of about 40,000 exhibits; 3,300 objects are exhibited in the museum rooms and about 12,000 in the Material Culture Galleries. The exhibits are located in a visitable space of 12,000 sq. meters, laid out on 4 floors.👍

Really mesmerized by the culture of Egypt and also the collection and maintenance of entire artifacts. One full day is required to have a detailed review of the entire museum. Excellent work by the Italian government to preserve and pass the same to next generations. Hats off !!! Thank you for making this trip so memorable 👍👏🥰

Plz book your tickets in advance. English tour would have been better. While the English recording machine had been given the charging is done in half the way so plz check the same with staff😞

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Museum is a must to visit. You could discover different interesting aspects of the Egyptian culture and story.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The Museum is HUGE and full of Egyptian relicts. If you're interested in these things, this will be more than perfect. You find more than a passionate can dream and hope to find, however, keep in mind that due to its enormous size and the huge amount of artifacts it's simple to be overwhelmed. Especially the rooms with more than one mummy can be overwhelming for little children (and not only) since you see in more than one case the faces of them (yes there are also the typical Egyptian mummies). Highly interesting, calcolate 2-3 hours for one round. I really hope, that Egypt can profit as well of their cultural artifacts here in Italy.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The museum is really amazing place for the people like acient history and want to know more about Egipt.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
The newly renovated Kings' Gallery is a feast for the eyes. The stark contrast between the sleek, modern design of the gallery and the timeworn statues is simply breathtaking. The hypermodern walls create a striking contrast with the ancient statues, which seem to float in a time machine.
This stunning visual experience is reason enough to visit the museum.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
The review is from an Egyptian tourist in Torino visiting his own Country treasures 😅.

Well the museum is very well designed and everything was very smooth from the entrance to buying a ticket for 18 Euro, to present my own identity to reserve a locker for free to store my bag.

Visiting the museum was easy as there is a well designed path to follow from top to bottom following the exact Timeline.

Lots of ancient Egyptian goods and monument that I was amazed to find it in Italy due to the Italian excavation of the Italians in egypt while presenting lots of photos for Egyptian workers digging and excavating their own heritage to be presented later on in another country, that felt weird.

I'll still give the Museum a well 5 stars away from my political opinion.

Loved that most of the explanation cards have English, Italian and Arabic. 👌

And yes it's a must visit.

title: Museo Egizio
text: The second most important egyptian Museum in the world. A trip in the past with an interactive vision of the masterpiecies and statues,and ancient craftmanship. This Museum is 500 years old

title: Museo Egizio
text: There were way more exhibits than I expected. I recommend joining a guided tour.You'll gain even more additional knowledge.

title: Museo Egizio
text: This is a dream of evry Egypt lover! Excellent exhibition!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
This is a lovely museum with an exhaustive collection. I now understand why Egypt is so much dessert because it seems that the ancients, and for thousands of years, used up all the soil for clay ink wells, vessels, lamps and uncountable numbers of bits and pieces.
It is well laid out and easily navigated. There are elevators, escalators and lockers. (One must leave an ID at the coat check for the key). Audio guides are available. Photography is allowed.
My entry was included in the Turino Card, (which was worth it), and we reserved a day and time. (I arrived an hour early and was able to enter, it was not a crowded day and a Monday--->).  They are open on Mondays which is a good day as other museums and palaces are closed.

title: Museo Egizio
text: This is an impressive collection with a very modern style of curating. For enjoying a bit of everything, you will need a good two hours and plenty of reading in order to make sense of the importance of what is being displayed. An extra interesting part is the history of why and how Turin ended up with such an enormous and essential collection of Egyptian art. The galleries are very busy, especially with endless streams of schoolchildren staring at the mummies.

title: Museo Egizio
text: This is how a dedicated Museum should be done - superb collection laid out in a way that makes sense to old  fans of Egyptology and neophytes alike. The thematic displays were especially cool, showing how we and they (the ancient people of the Nile) me meet our basic needs in similar ways using different material. Also the displays on how they manufactured colours for everything from cosmetics to crypts was wonderful.  This is a stunning museum and a must see for fans of history. I spent the afternoon there on a day trip from Milano by rail. Point to point, simple way to beat the heat - perfect air conditioned space that was crowded but not overly so. There are plenty of staff ushering people from place to place so there is always space and about of time to spend in front of these amazing pieces of history. Worth every penny!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
This is one of the best museums about the ancient Egyptian civilization in the world, probably the second after the one of Cairo. It is full of important artefacts and it should take a full day to see everything.
I recommend booking your tickets in advance and be punctual on the day of your visit. The museum is almost always crowded.
5 stars, a must visit if you are in Torino.

title: Museo Egizio
text: This is the largest museum with exhibits from Egypt that I have ever seen. It is a joy to see all the statues, columns, pots and jewels from ancient times gathered and shared with the visitors. Can't wait to see it again.

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio
text: Turin Egyptian Museum is amazing … seeing faces stare back from 4000 years ago. Egyptians made huge efforts to ensure their lives lived on beyond death. In these exhibits they do. Images of people and lives from thousands of years ago lead us to try and understand how they thought, how they lived. Arms wrapped around each other’s shoulders, their love lives on. Everyday objects, a bed, a side table, 4000 years old but preserved as if they were made yesterday. The array of objects and images is astonishing. Carved from hard volcanic rock, painted with mineral pigments, these objects and images still live like the day they were made. Kings and gods sit silently and stare through the years, unchanged.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Very comprehensive museum on Egypt. I think I managed to go through all of it. Felt like I got a complete rundown on the entire corpus of Egyptian history.
The app guide is glitchy. I downloaded some modules but sometimes it reloads if you don’t keep the phone open, and then it says you’re offline and won’t load the module anymore. So maybe the old school audio guide you can get at reception is a better option. However, the displays do a good job anyway and you can still get enough of a gist of it just with the displays.
To do the entire museum, reading everything and listening to the audio guides, would take the best part of a whole day. But there’s an in house cafe at a convenient midpoint.

title: Museo Egizio
text:

It has multiple floors and you start by going up an escalator to the 3rd floor.  It was a bit confusing the layout of each floor as you work your way down.

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio

text: Very interesting place in Torino. It should be the biggest egyptian museum in Europe. If you would read text next every monument, you would be there 6 hours. The whole visit took me 3 hours.

title: Museo Egizio

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Very nice place to be. I was literally a week ago in the British Museum (full of Egyptian artifacts, but also other historical stuff). This museum felt very small (managed to finish it in an hour due to time constraints but I barely read anything there), in comparison it took me to finish British Museum in almost 3 hours. But this museum had way more mummies and sarcophagus which I felt lacking in the other museum.
Went around lunch time on workday so it wasn't too crowded (didn't have to wait in any ques) but there were multiple school groups with children.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Very well organised museum and remarkable collection. It is a must see in Turin. It's said to be the second biggest collection of Egyptian art, after Cairo.
I have visited other Egyptian museum in Italy and abroad but this one is top notch.
It you want to walk the path of the Pharaohs, from the time they even weren't a thing to the Ptolemaic dinasty, this is the place. From vases, textiles to ostraka, papyrus and off sarcophagus and mummies.

Allow between 3-5h for a proper visit. You have a free audio guide but remember to bring your own headphones or download the museum app. Price is fair and students have a good discount.
Worth adding Turin to the itinerary just for this

title: Museo Egizio
text: We had a small group guide, who walked us through the timeline of exhibits in the museum. The quality of exhibits is first rate. I definitely recommend a guide as otherwise you might find navigating the museum exhausting. A must see if you are visiting Turin.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
We spent about 4 hours going through the various levels and exhibits.  You must purchase your tickets online.  I recommend booking in advance by a few days as the museum does sell out a week or more in advance in weekends,  during busy season week also sellout on week days.
Stunning collection and decent free audio guide (download and listen on your phone.)

title: Museo Egizio
text:
We visited this museum on a Sunday; understandably, it was quite crowded.
We had booked tickets online the previous day. That said, there was a short wait of around 20 mins to get our tickets validated and enter the museum.
The museum is a must visit. It boasts a wide variety of archaeological finds from Egypt. Detailed explanations about each exhibit help visitors gain a deep understanding of Egyption culture and traditions. There also is abundant information about the connection between the Egyptians and the Savoys and how this museum came to be. All in all, a very enlightening experience.
This is not a place that can be put under a 'quick visit' category. Make sure you have at least 2.5 to 3 hours to spare. The true value of this museum lies in understanding the history behind each exhibit, so you will have to spend some time reading the short explanations provided or listening to the audio guide which you can connect to and play on your phone. Guided tours are available too, which probably come at additional costs. We were happy to make do with the write-ups and audio guides, which were also available in English.
This museum can be visited for free with the Piemonte pass.

title: Museo Egizio
text: What a beautiful experience, I didn't realise how big and extensive this collection was going to be. The English translations are really well done and available for each object. The toilets were very clean, and the cloakroom was a very useful facility to leave heavy backpacks and fully enjoy the visit.

title: Museo Egizio
text: What a collection! Easy to follow the directions throughout. Very good explanations in English. We arrived at 11:00 and left about 2:00. School groups were somewhat annoying but you could wait only moments and they moved on. A good time to  arrive might be 3:00 since not too many people seemed to be coming in at that time. The final gallery is so beautiful! Don’t miss this museum!

title: Museo Egizio
text:
While my feeling is that the Egyptian museum in London gets all the fame, this museum in Turin actually takes it all in my opinion. It is the second biggest in the world after the Egyptian museum in Egypt and houses more than 30,000 artifacts.

So let's talk about the atmosphere inside and how well it is organized. The artifacts are very well kept in climatized rooms, where their condition can be best reserved. The museum is huge, so you would need at least half a day to skim through it, it is very easy to get lost inside, but with the help of Maps an audio guides, it will be manageable.

Some of the most noted treasures in the museum are the statues of Sekhmet, Ptah and Ramses II with Amun and Hathor. I won't say more, you have to visit and see for yourself. So if you are in Turin and have half a day to spare, I would highly recommend visiting this Egyptian museum, especially if you want to get a taste of Egypt and travel thousands of years back. This visit will surely excite you to visit Egypt one day and discover all the rich history it has to offer.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Wonderful huge museum, very complete.

title: Museo Egizio
text: Wonderful museum. Worth visiting to know about history and past cultures.

title: Museo Egizio
text:
Wowwww. What a Museum.
A must.
Best ever collection in Europe we ever saw.
We spent  almost 4 hours there.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text:
MoMA was overcrowded and noisy throughout the entire building, making it difficult to enjoy the museum at any level. This was especially bad in the Van Gogh / Starry Night gallery.

Instead of structured crowd control (such as lining people up or timed viewing), the attendant responsible for the area repeatedly physically pushed visitors backward while shouting for people to “back off.” This involved hands-on contact, not just verbal instructions, and there was no immediate safety emergency that justified it.

The constant noise, shouting, and pushing made it impossible to properly view or appreciate the artwork. A museum should allow space for reflection and respect for both the art and the visitors. What I experienced felt chaotic and unnecessarily aggressive.

Given the cost of admission, this visit was a waste of your money. MoMA needs better crowd-management strategies for its most popular works and clearer standards that prohibit physical contact with visitors. Until then, the experience prioritizes volume over quality.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: This museum may have been interesting at the beginning of the 20th century. Nowadays, it is a swan song. I recommend the dinosaur collection. The museum was made famous by a film, which is why we visited it, but the exhibitions (except for the dinosaurs) did not arouse our interest.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: MoMA is one of the world’s greatest museums, which is why the poor condition of the restrooms was surprising. The sinks were dirty, the paint was peeling, and even the soap bottle looked old and cheap, made of plastic. As a New Yorker, this is honestly a bit embarrassing!

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text:
The main reason why I wanted to visit this museum is to see Starry Night by Van Gogh. It was a dream of mine since I was a little girl. So I was so happy to see it for real!

However the rest of the museum wasn’t really my taste as I’m not into modern art. Although the 5th floor (I think?) with some paintings from Picasso, Dali, … was really interesting and beautiful.

I guess if you like modern art you’ll love it.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: Some amazing art from the classics like Picasso, but some of the more modern art was overly violent, sexual and grotesque in ways that didn't set well with me. I guess I'm more of an older art appreciator than I am modern art. As for the museum it was well laid out and my only gripe was that it closed early for an event and the workers were really pushy and annoying trying to clear the area.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: We came to visit on a Wednesday, there was a line to get to the bag check. We got the tickets online so we were able to have the attendant scan them and entered. There were a lot of people in every level. It's a little surprising it was that crowded on a Wednesday, but it's New York, so I guess it was to be expected. I didn't get a lot of the arts but there were a couple of pieces resonated with me (so I took some pictures). It's a nice place to walk around and spend two hours, but it's not my type of art museum.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: A sizeable collection of art with a relatively affordable entry fee given all there is.  Though, it doesn't really have a large percentage of what I would consider modern.  Much of the space features artwork in the 100 year old and more range.  Still, quite a few interesting pieces in the collection.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: There really needs to be a limit on how many people can enter the MoMA at once. It was so overcrowded that it made the whole visit really hard to enjoy. Instead of taking in the art and feeling inspired, we spent most of the time trying to navigate through massive crowds. It was incredibly loud — honestly, noise-cancelling headphones would’ve helped. I was so excited to visit, but the experience was overwhelming and disappointing because of how packed it was.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text:
The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) is huge with six floors of art and exhibitions, even some pieces taking up multiple floors of space. Truly impressive, especially the higher floors, which feature some of Van Gogh’s, Pollock’s, and Warhol’s most well-known works.

Our fun took a turn when we went down to the third floor and started seeing what modern art can also be. We saw a bicycle wheel, some bic pencils, M&Ms, a shovel, and even more things that led us to start speaking in a more negative light. It's interesting and bold, but I would personally use the word "useless."

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: I am a Van Gogh fan and was excited to see Starry Night in person. I also enjoyed seeing many of Picasso's pieces, a few of Frida Kahlo's and a few of Monet's. As for the rest of the artwork, there were a few pieces that made me question how they made it into the museum. I think this museum is worth a visit but probably not repeated visits.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text: The amount of people waiting for the melting clocks and starry night was crazy! You couldn’t get a look or a picture without touching every inch of the other people’s bodies. Besides that, they had Ikea furniture as art?!?!? Like literal ikea shelves

title: The Museum of Modern Art
text:
Nice exhibition. But to many people... Going down under its own succes.  Extreme rude security..
The restaurant was nice. Good wine and a nice snack. Friendly service up there.

title: The Museum of Modern Art

I would definitely recommend visit this museum if you’re looking to learn about how art has changed through the years. I suggest buying the tickets but you can also buy it there.

title: The Museum of Modern Art
